revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''笠縫邑'''（'''かさぬいむら'''、'''かさぬいのむら'''）とは、[[崇神天皇]]６年に、宮中に奉祀していた[[天照大神]]を移...'
revision.text:
'''笠縫邑'''（'''かさぬいむら'''、'''かさぬいのむら'''）とは、[[崇神天皇]]６年に、宮中に奉祀していた[[天照大神]]を移し、[[豊鍬入姫命]]に託して祀らせた場所。同時に宮中を出された[[倭国魂神]]は[[渟名城入媛命]]に託して[[大和神社]]に祀った、とされる。 笠縫邑は[[大嘗祭]]、[[豊明節会]]の起源に関係する土地との説もある。
比定地については、[[檜原神社]]（[[桜井市]] [[三輪]]）、[[多神社]]（[[磯城郡]] [[田原本町]]多）、[[笠縫神社]]（磯城郡田原本町秦荘、[[秦楽寺]]境内南東隅）、[[笠山荒神社]]（桜井市笠）、多神社摂社の姫皇子神社、[[志貴御県坐神社]]（桜井市金屋）、[[小夫天神社]]（桜井市小夫）、[[穴師坐兵主神社]]（桜井市穴師）、[[飛鳥坐神社]]（[[高市郡]] [[明日香村]]飛鳥）、磯城の厳橿の本（[[垂仁天皇]]紀5年３月条：所在地不詳）、等々。
檜原神社、笠山荒神社、小夫天神社（笠山荒神社よりもさらに東の山間部）、穴師坐兵主神社等はまさに山の中である。 また、笠縫神社に関係する秦楽（じんらく）という名が、[[築山古墳]]（[[大和高田市]]築山字城山）の前方部眼下に広がる平地にも神楽（じんらく）という名で存在する。この神楽は、古くは秦楽と書いたとの説もある。何らかの関係を示唆しているかも知れない。
多神社から、摂社の屋就神命神社（やつぎかみのみことじんじゃ、やつき？しんめいじんじゃ：祭神 天明豊玉命）の方角を見ると、神楽、築山古墳の方角となる。実際には、理由はよく判らないが地元住民に恐れられた「矢継ぎの森（やつぎのもり：屋就神命神社の杜でもある）」に隠されて見えない。
また、もしかすると、昔は、多神社から姫皇子神社の方向を見ると、[[箸墓]]が見えたのかもしれない。元々、姫皇子神社は、多神社の奥（北：上座）に位置していたので、前（南：下座）に移転させられている可能性がある。
多神社を笠縫邑の位置と比定するなら、これら[[古墳]]が、大嘗祭の悠紀殿（[[千木]]は[[伊勢神宮]]外宮の様に外削）、主基殿（千木は伊勢神宮内宮の様に内削）の起源とも関係している可能性がある。
==大阪の笠縫島== [[深江稲荷神社]]（[[大阪市]] [[東成区]]深江南）には、付近の深江は[[笠縫氏]]の居住地で、[[大和]]の笠縫邑から移住してきた、との伝承がある。[[高市黒人]]が「四極山（しはつやま） うち越え見れば笠縫の島 漕ぎ隠る 棚無し小舟」と詠んだ様に、古代には、笠縫島といわれた。笠縫島は、現在の深江から[[東大阪市]]足代にかけて、入江に浮かんだ島であった。笠の材料の確保のため、笠縫氏は島に住んだと思われるが、もともと大和の笠縫邑も、同じような低湿地か、島状の土地だったのではないかとも推測される。 現在も大嘗祭に使用する笠は、この深江から[[天皇家]]へ献上されている。また、深江は、皇祖の御神鏡に関係する[[鋳物]]師とも関係が深い土地とのことである。[[鏡作神社]]（磯城郡田原本町八尾）とも関係の可能性がある。

'''笠縫邑'''（'''かさぬいむら'''、'''かさぬいのむら'''）とは、[[崇神天皇]]６年に、宮中に奉祀していた[[天照大神]]を移し、[[豊鍬入姫命]]に託して祀らせた場所。同時に宮中を出された[[倭大国魂神]]は[[渟名城入媛命]]に託して[[大和神社]]に祀った、とされる。 笠縫邑は[[大嘗祭]]、[[豊明節会]]の起源に関係する土地との説もある。

'''笠縫邑'''（'''かさぬいむら'''、'''かさぬいのむら'''）とは、[[崇神天皇]]６年に、宮中に奉祀していた[[天照大神]]を移し、[[豊鍬入姫命]]に託して祀らせた場所。同時に宮中を出された[[倭大国魂神]]は[[渟名城入媛命]]に託して[[大和神社]]に祀った、とされる。 笠縫邑は[[大嘗祭]]、[[豊明節会]]の起源に関係する土地との説もある。
比定地については、[[檜原神社]]（[[桜井市]] [[三輪]]）、[[多神社]]（[[磯城郡]] [[田原本町]]多）、[[笠縫神社]]（磯城郡田原本町秦荘、[[秦楽寺]]境内南東隅）、[[笠山荒神社]]（桜井市笠）、多神社摂社の姫皇子神社、[[志貴御県坐神社]]（桜井市金屋）、[[小夫天神社]]（桜井市小夫）、[[穴師坐兵主神社]]（桜井市穴師）、[[飛鳥坐神社]]（[[高市郡]] [[明日香村]]飛鳥）、磯城の厳橿の本（[[垂仁天皇]]紀5年３月条：所在地不詳）、等々。
檜原神社、笠山荒神社、小夫天神社（笠山荒神社よりもさらに東の山間部）、穴師坐兵主神社等はまさに山の中である。 また、笠縫神社に関係する秦楽（じんらく）という名が、[[築山古墳]]（[[大和高田市]]築山字城山）の前方部眼下に広がる平地にも神楽（じんらく）という名で存在する。この神楽は、古くは秦楽と書いたとの説もある。何らかの関係を示唆しているかも知れない。
多神社から、摂社の屋就神命神社（やつぎかみのみことじんじゃ、やつき？しんめいじんじゃ：祭神 天明豊玉命）の方角を見ると、神楽、築山古墳の方角となる。実際には、理由はよく判らないが地元住民によって大切に残された「矢継ぎの森（やつぎのもり：屋就神命神社の杜でもある）」に隠されて見えない。
また、もしかすると、昔は、多神社から姫皇子神社の方向を見ると、[[箸墓]]が見えたのかもしれない。元々、姫皇子神社は、多神社の奥（北：上座）に位置していたので、前（南：下座）に移転させられている可能性がある。
多神社を笠縫邑の位置と比定するなら、これら[[古墳]]が、大嘗祭の悠紀殿（[[千木]]は[[伊勢神宮]]外宮の様に外削ぎ）、主基殿（千木は伊勢神宮内宮の様に内削ぎ）の起源とも関係している可能性がある。

多神社から、摂社の屋就神命神社（やつぎかみのみことじんじゃ、やつき？しんめいじんじゃ：祭神 天明豊玉命）の方角を見ると、神楽、築山古墳の方角となる。実際には、理由はよく判らないが地元住民によって大切に残された「矢継ぎの森（やつぎのもり：屋就神命神社の杜でもある）」に隠されて見えない。
また、もしかすると、昔は、多神社から姫皇子神社の方向を見ると、[[箸墓]]が見えたのかもしれない。元々、姫皇子神社は、多神社の奥（北：上座）に位置していたので、前（南：下座）に移転させられている可能性がある。
多神社を笠縫邑の位置と比定するなら、これら[[古墳]]が、鏡に映した様に南北及び東西が逆になるが、大嘗祭の悠紀殿（[[千木]]は[[伊勢神宮]]外宮の様に外削ぎ）、主基殿（千木は伊勢神宮内宮の様に内削ぎ）の起源とも関係している可能性がある。
==大阪の笠縫島== [[深江稲荷神社]]（[[大阪市]] [[東成区]]深江南）には、付近の深江は[[笠縫氏]]の居住地で、[[大和]]の笠縫邑から移住してきた、との伝承がある。[[万葉]][[歌人]][[高市黒人]]（たけちのくろと）が「四極山（しはつやま） うち越え見れば笠縫の島 漕ぎ隠る 棚無し小舟」と詠んだ様に、古代には、笠縫島といわれた。笠縫島は、現在の深江から[[東大阪市]]足代にかけて、入江に浮かんだ島であった。笠の材料の確保のため、笠縫氏は島に住んだと思われるが、もともと大和の笠縫邑も、同じような低湿地か、島状の土地だったのではないかとも推測される。 現在も大嘗祭に使用する笠は、この深江から[[天皇家]]へ献上されている。また、深江は、皇祖の御神鏡に関係する[[鋳物]]師とも関係が深い土地とのことである。[[鏡作神社]]（磯城郡田原本町八尾 等周辺）とも関係の可能性がある。
[[Category:神道|かさぬいむら]]

'''笠縫邑'''（かさぬいむら、かさぬいのむら）とは、[[崇神天皇]]6年に、宮中に奉祀していた[[天照大神]]を移し、[[豊鍬入姫命]]に託して祀らせた場所。同時に宮中を出された[[倭大国魂神]]は[[渟名城入媛命]]に託して、後に[[大和神社]]に祀った、とされる。 笠縫邑は[[大嘗祭]]、[[豊明節会]]の起源に関係する土地との説もある。
比定地については、[[檜原神社]]（[[桜井市]] [[三輪]]）、[[多神社]]（[[磯城郡]] [[田原本町]]多）、[[笠縫神社]]（磯城郡田原本町秦荘、[[秦楽寺]]境内南東隅）、[[笠山荒神社]]（桜井市笠）、多神社摂社の姫皇子神社、[[志貴御県坐神社]]（桜井市金屋）、[[小夫天神社]]（桜井市小夫）、[[穴師坐兵主神社]]（桜井市穴師）、[[飛鳥坐神社]]（[[高市郡]] [[明日香村]]飛鳥）、磯城の厳橿の本（[[垂仁天皇]]紀5年3月条：所在地不詳）、等々。
檜原神社、笠山荒神社、小夫天神社（笠山荒神社よりもさらに東の山間部）、穴師坐兵主神社等はまさに山の中である。 また、笠縫神社に関係する秦楽（じんらく）という名が、[[築山古墳 (大和高田市)|築山古墳]]（[[大和高田市]]築山字城山）の前方部眼下に広がる平地にも神楽（じんらく）という名で存在する。この神楽は、古くは秦楽と書いたとの説もある。何らかの関係を示唆しているかも知れない。
多神社から、摂社の屋就神命神社（やつぎかみのみことじんじゃ、やつき？しんめいじんじゃ：祭神 天明豊玉命）の方角を見ると、神楽、築山古墳の方角となる。実際には、理由はよく判らないが地元住民によって大切に残された「矢継ぎの森（やつぎのもり：屋就神命神社の杜でもある）」に隠されて見えない。
また、もしかすると、昔は、多神社から姫皇子神社の方向を見ると、[[箸墓]]が見えたのかもしれない。元々、姫皇子神社は、多神社の奥（北：上座）に位置していたので、前（南：下座）に移転させられている可能性がある。
多神社を笠縫邑の位置と比定するなら、これら[[古墳]]が、鏡に映した様に南北及び東西が逆になるが、大嘗祭の悠紀殿（[[千木]]は[[伊勢神宮]]外宮の様に外削ぎ）、主基殿（千木は伊勢神宮内宮の様に内削ぎ）の起源とも関係している可能性がある。
==大阪の笠縫島== [[深江稲荷神社]]（[[大阪市]] [[東成区]]深江南）には、付近の深江は[[笠縫氏]]の居住地で、[[大和]]の笠縫邑から移住してきた、との伝承がある。[[万葉]][[歌人]][[高市黒人]]（たけちのくろと）が「四極山（しはつやま） うち越え見れば笠縫の島 漕ぎ隠る 棚無し小舟」と詠んだ様に、古代には、笠縫島といわれた。笠縫島は、現在の深江から[[東大阪市]]足代にかけて、入江に浮かんだ島であった。笠の材料の確保のため、笠縫氏は島に住んだと思われるが、もともと大和の笠縫邑も、同じような低湿地か、島状の土地だったのではないかとも推測される。 現在も大嘗祭に使用する笠は、この深江から[[天皇家]]へ献上されている。また、深江は、皇祖の御神鏡に関係する[[鋳物]]師とも関係が深い土地とのことである。[[鏡作神社]]（磯城郡田原本町八尾 等周辺）とも関係の可能性がある。
[[Category:神道|かさぬいむら]]

{{出典の明記|date=2012年3月25日 (日) 08:39 (UTC)}} '''笠縫邑'''（かさぬいむら、かさぬいのむら）とは、[[崇神天皇]]6年に、宮中に奉祀していた[[天照大神]]を移し、[[豊鍬入姫命]]に託して祀らせた場所。同時に宮中を出された[[倭大国魂神]]は[[渟名城入媛命]]に託して、後に[[大和神社]]に祀った、とされる。 笠縫邑は[[大嘗祭]]、[[豊明節会]]の起源に関係する土地との説もある。
比定地については、[[檜原神社]]（[[桜井市]] [[三輪]]）、[[多神社]]（[[磯城郡]] [[田原本町]]多）、[[笠縫神社]]（磯城郡田原本町秦荘、[[秦楽寺]]境内南東隅）、[[笠山荒神社]]（桜井市笠）、[[多神社]]摂社の姫皇子神社、[[志貴御県坐神社]]（桜井市金屋）、[[小夫天神社]]（桜井市小夫）、[[穴師坐兵主神社]]（桜井市穴師）、[[飛鳥坐神社]]（[[高市郡]] [[明日香村]]飛鳥）、磯城の厳橿の本（[[垂仁天皇]]紀5年3月条：所在地不詳）、等々。
檜原神社、笠山荒神社、小夫天神社（笠山荒神社よりもさらに東の山間部）、穴師坐兵主神社等はまさに山の中である。 また、笠縫神社に関係する秦楽（じんらく）という名が、[[築山古墳 (大和高田市)|築山古墳]]（[[大和高田市]]築山字城山）の前方部眼下に広がる平地にも神楽（じんらく）という名で存在する。この神楽は、古くは秦楽と書いたとの説もある。何らかの関係を示唆しているかも知れない。
多神社から、摂社の屋就神命神社（やつぎかみのみことじんじゃ、やつき？しんめいじんじゃ：祭神 天明豊玉命）の方角を見ると、神楽、築山古墳の方角となる。実際には、理由はよく判らないが地元住民によって大切に残された「矢継ぎの森（やつぎのもり：屋就神命神社の杜でもある）」に隠されて見えない。
また、もしかすると、昔は、多神社から姫皇子神社の方向を見ると、[[箸墓]]が見えたのかもしれない。元々、姫皇子神社は、多神社の奥（北：上座）に位置していたので、前（南：下座）に移転させられている可能性がある。
多神社を笠縫邑の位置と比定するなら、これら[[古墳]]が、鏡に映した様に南北及び東西が逆になるが、大嘗祭の悠紀殿（[[千木]]は[[伊勢神宮]]外宮の様に外削ぎ）、主基殿（千木は伊勢神宮内宮の様に内削ぎ）の起源とも関係している可能性がある。
==大阪の笠縫島== [[深江稲荷神社]]（[[大阪市]] [[東成区]]深江南）には、付近の深江は[[笠縫氏]]の居住地で、[[大和]]の笠縫邑から移住してきた、との伝承がある。[[万葉]][[歌人]][[高市黒人]]（たけちのくろと）が「四極山（しはつやま） うち越え見れば笠縫の島 漕ぎ隠る 棚無し小舟」と詠んだ様に、古代には、笠縫島といわれた。笠縫島は、現在の深江から[[東大阪市]]足代にかけて、入江に浮かんだ島であった。笠の材料の確保のため、笠縫氏は島に住んだと思われるが、もともと大和の笠縫邑も、同じような低湿地か、島状の土地だったのではないかとも推測される。 現在も大嘗祭に使用する笠は、この深江から[[天皇家]]へ献上されている。また、深江は、皇祖の御神鏡に関係する[[鋳物]]師とも関係が深い土地とのことである。[[鏡作神社]]（磯城郡田原本町八尾 等周辺）とも関係の可能性がある。
==関連項目== *[[笠縫駅]]

{{出典の明記|date=2012年3月25日 (日) 08:39 (UTC)}} '''笠縫邑'''（かさぬいむら、かさぬいのむら）とは、[[崇神天皇]]6年に、宮中に奉祀していた[[天照大神]]を移し、[[豊鍬入姫命]]に託して祀らせた場所。同時に宮中を出された[[倭大国魂神]]は[[渟名城入媛命]]に託して、後に[[大和神社]]に祀ったとされる。 笠縫邑は[[大嘗祭]]、[[豊明節会]]の起源に関係する土地との説もある。[[垂仁天皇]]紀25年3月丙申条に、[[倭姫命]]は天照大神を[[磯城の厳橿の本]]に祀ったとあり、厳橿は神霊の神木で、崇神朝に[[磯堅城の神籬]]を立てた。（崇神紀６年条）。[[古語拾遺]]には「磯城の神籬」とあり、「磯城の厳橿の本」と「磯堅城の神籬」は同所とも考えられる。
比定地については、[[檜原神社]]（[[桜井市]] [[三輪]]）、[[多神社]]（[[磯城郡]] [[田原本町]]多）、[[笠縫神社]]（磯城郡田原本町秦荘、[[秦楽寺]]境内南東隅）、[[笠山荒神社]]（桜井市笠）、[[多神社]]摂社の姫皇子神社、[[志貴御県坐神社]]（桜井市金屋）、[[小夫天神社]]（桜井市小夫）、[[穴師坐兵主神社]]（桜井市穴師）、[[飛鳥坐神社]]（[[高市郡]] [[明日香村]]飛鳥）、長谷山口坐神社（桜井市初瀬手力雄）、等々。
檜原神社、笠山荒神社、小夫天神社（笠山荒神社よりもさらに東の山間部）、[[穴師坐兵主神社]]等はまさに山の中である。 また、笠縫神社に関係する秦楽（じんらく）という名が、[[築山古墳 (大和高田市)|築山古墳]]（[[大和高田市]]築山字城山）の前方部眼下に広がる平地にも神楽（じんらく）という名で存在する。この神楽は、古くは秦楽と書いたとの説もある。何らかの関係を示唆しているかも知れない。
多神社から、摂社の屋就神命神社（やつぎかみのみことじんじゃ、やつき？しんめいじんじゃ：祭神 天明豊玉命）の方角を見ると、神楽、築山古墳の方角となる。実際には、理由はよく判らないが地元住民によって大切に残された「矢継ぎの森（やつぎのもり：屋就神命神社の杜でもある）」に隠されて見えない。
また、もしかすると、昔は、多神社から姫皇子神社の方向を見ると、[[箸墓]]が見えたのかもしれない。元々、[[姫皇子神社]]は、多神社の奥（北：上座）に位置していたので、前（南：下座）に移転させられている可能性がある。
多神社を笠縫邑の位置と比定するなら、これら[[古墳]]が、鏡に映した様に南北及び東西が逆になるが、[[大嘗祭]]の[[悠紀殿]]（[[千木]]は[[伊勢神宮]]外宮の様に外削ぎ）、主基殿（千木は伊勢神宮内宮の様に内削ぎ）の起源とも関係している可能性がある。
==大阪の笠縫島== [[深江稲荷神社]]（[[大阪市]] [[東成区]]深江南）には、付近の深江は[[笠縫氏]]の居住地で、[[大和]]の笠縫邑から移住してきた、との伝承がある。[[万葉]][[歌人]][[高市黒人]]（たけちのくろと）が「四極山（しはつやま） うち越え見れば笠縫の島 漕ぎ隠る 棚無し小舟」と詠んだ様に、古代には、笠縫島といわれた。笠縫島は、現在の深江から[[東大阪市]]足代にかけて、入江に浮かんだ島であった。笠の材料の確保のため、笠縫氏は島に住んだと思われるが、もともと大和の笠縫邑も、同じような低湿地か、島状の土地だったのではないかとも推測される。 現在も大嘗祭に使用する笠は、この深江から[[皇室]]へ献上されている。また、深江は、皇祖の御神鏡に関係する[[鋳物]]師とも関係が深い土地とのことである。[[鏡作神社]]（磯城郡田原本町八尾 等周辺）とも関係の可能性がある。
==関連項目== *[[笠縫駅]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:かさぬいむら}} [[Category:神道]] [[Category:元伊勢]] [[Category:奈良県の歴史]] [[Category:大阪府指定史跡]]

revision.comment: 大嘗祭の悠紀と主基殿の千木について
revision.text:

檜原神社、笠山荒神社、小夫天神社（笠山荒神社よりもさらに東の山間部）、[[穴師坐兵主神社]]等はまさに山の中である。 また、笠縫神社に関係する秦楽（じんらく）という名が、[[築山古墳 (大和高田市)|築山古墳]]（[[大和高田市]]築山字城山）の前方部眼下に広がる平地にも神楽（じんらく）という名で存在する。この神楽は、古くは秦楽と書いたとの説もある。何らかの関係を示唆しているかも知れない。

また、もしかすると、昔は、多神社から姫皇子神社の方向を見ると、[[箸墓]]が見えたのかもしれない。元々、[[姫皇子神社]]は、多神社の奥（北：上座）に位置していたので、前（南：下座）に移転させられている可能性がある。
[[多神社]]を笠縫邑の位置と比定するなら、これら[[古墳]]が、鏡に映した様に南北及び東西が逆になるが、[[大嘗祭]]の[[悠紀殿]]([[千木]]は[[伊勢神宮]][[皇大神宮]]の様に中削ぎ)<ref>[[鎌田純一]]、「平成大禮要話」p.182 ISBN 4-7646-0262-8 </ref>、主基殿(千木は伊勢神宮[[豊受大神宮]]の様に外削ぎ)<ref>鎌田純一、「平成大禮要話」p. 182</ref> の起源とも関係している可能性がある。

[[多神社]]を笠縫邑の位置と比定するなら、これら[[古墳]]が、鏡に映した様に南北及び東西が逆になるが、[[大嘗祭]]の[[悠紀殿]]（[[千木]]は[[伊勢神宮]][[皇大神宮]]の様に内削ぎ）{{r|平成大禮要話/p182}}、主基殿（千木は伊勢神宮[[豊受大神宮]]の様に外削ぎ）{{r|平成大禮要話/p182}}の起源とも関係している可能性がある。
==大阪の笠縫島== [[深江稲荷神社]]（[[大阪市]] [[東成区]]深江南）には、付近の深江は[[笠縫氏]]の居住地で、[[大和]]の笠縫邑から移住してきた、との伝承がある。[[万葉]][[歌人]][[高市黒人]]（たけちのくろと）が「四極山（しはつやま） うち越え見れば笠縫の島 漕ぎ隠る 棚無し小舟」と詠んだ様に、古代には、笠縫島といわれた。笠縫島は、現在の深江から[[東大阪市]]足代にかけて、入江に浮かんだ島であった。笠の材料の確保のため、笠縫氏は島に住んだと思われるが、もともと大和の笠縫邑も、同じような低湿地か、島状の土地だったのではないかとも推測される。 現在も大嘗祭に使用する笠は、この深江から[[皇室]]へ献上されている。また、深江は、皇祖の御神鏡に関係する[[鋳物]]師とも関係が深い土地とのことである。[[鏡作神社]]（磯城郡田原本町八尾 等周辺）とも関係の可能性がある。
==脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="平成大禮要話/p182">{{Citation|和書 | last = 鎌田 | first = 純一 | author-link = 鎌田純一 | title = 平成大禮要話 : 即位禮・大嘗祭 | publisher = [[錦正社]] | date = 2003-7 | page = 182 | id = {{全国書誌番号|20452668}} | isbn = 4-7646-0262-8 }}</ref> }}
==関連項目== *[[元伊勢]] *[[笠縫駅]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:かさぬいむら}} [[Category:神道]] [[Category:元伊勢]] [[Category:奈良県の歴史]] [[Category:大阪府指定史跡]]

revision.comment: 記事全体に曖昧な記述。編集者に出典を求めます
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2024年6月}} {{独自研究|date=2024年6月}} {{言葉を濁さない|date=2024年6月}} '''笠縫邑'''（かさぬいむら、かさぬいのむら）とは、[[崇神天皇]]6年に、宮中に奉祀していた[[天照大神]]を移し、[[豊鍬入姫命]]に託して祀らせた場所。同時に宮中を出された[[倭大国魂神]]は[[渟名城入媛命]]に託して、後に[[大和神社]]に祀ったとされる{{要出典|date=2024年6月}}。 笠縫邑は[[大嘗祭]]、[[豊明節会]]の起源に関係する土地との説もある{{要出典|date=2024年6月}}。[[垂仁天皇]]紀25年3月丙申条に、[[倭姫命]]は天照大神を[[磯城の厳橿の本]]に祀ったとあり、厳橿は神霊の神木で、崇神朝に[[磯堅城の神籬]]を立てた。（崇神紀６年条）。[[古語拾遺]]には「磯城の神籬」とあり、「磯城の厳橿の本」と「磯堅城の神籬」は同所とも考えられる{{誰2|date=2024年6月}}。
比定地については、[[大神神社#伊勢神宮との関係|檜原神社]]（[[桜井市]] [[三輪]]）、[[多神社]]（[[磯城郡]] [[田原本町]]多）、[[笠縫神社]]（磯城郡田原本町秦荘、[[秦楽寺]]境内南東隅）、[[笠山荒神社]]（桜井市笠）、[[多神社]]摂社の姫皇子神社、[[志貴御県坐神社]]（桜井市金屋）、[[小夫天神社]]（桜井市小夫）、[[穴師坐兵主神社]]（桜井市穴師）、[[飛鳥坐神社]]（[[高市郡]] [[明日香村]]飛鳥）、長谷山口坐神社（桜井市初瀬手力雄）、等々{{要出典|date=2024年6月}}。
檜原神社、笠山荒神社、小夫天神社（笠山荒神社よりもさらに東の山間部）、[[穴師坐兵主神社]]等はまさに山の中である。 また、笠縫神社に関係する{{読み仮名|秦楽|じんらく}}という名が、[[築山古墳 (大和高田市)|築山古墳]]（[[大和高田市]]築山字城山）の前方部眼下に広がる平地にも{{読み仮名|神楽|じんらく}}という名で存在する。この神楽は、古くは秦楽と書いたとの説もある。何らかの関係を示唆しているかも知れない{{要出典|date=2024年6月}}。
多神社から、摂社の屋就神命神社（やつぎかみのみことじんじゃ、やつき？しんめいじんじゃ：祭神 天明豊玉命）の方角を見ると、神楽、築山古墳の方角となる。実際には、理由はよく判らないが地元住民によって大切に残された「矢継ぎの森（やつぎのもり：屋就神命神社の杜でもある）」に隠されて見えない。
また、もしかすると、昔は、多神社から姫皇子神社の方向を見ると、[[箸墓]]が見えたのかもしれない。元々、[[姫皇子神社]]は、多神社の奥（北：上座）に位置していたので、前（南：下座）に移転させられている可能性がある{{要出典|date=2024年6月}}。
[[多神社]]を笠縫邑の位置と比定するなら、これら[[古墳]]が、鏡に映した様に南北及び東西が逆になるが、[[大嘗祭]]の[[悠紀殿]]（[[千木]]は[[伊勢神宮]][[皇大神宮]]の様に内削ぎ）{{r|平成大禮要話/p182}}、主基殿（千木は伊勢神宮[[豊受大神宮]]の様に外削ぎ）{{r|平成大禮要話/p182}}の起源とも関係している可能性がある{{要出典|date=2024年6月}}。
==大阪の笠縫島== [[深江稲荷神社]]（[[大阪市]] [[東成区]]深江南）には、付近の深江は[[笠縫氏]]の居住地で、[[大和]]の笠縫邑から移住してきた、との伝承がある。[[万葉]][[歌人]][[高市黒人]]（たけちのくろと）が「四極山（しはつやま） うち越え見れば笠縫の島 漕ぎ隠る 棚無し小舟」と詠んだ様に、古代には、笠縫島といわれた。笠縫島は、現在の深江から[[東大阪市]]足代にかけて、入江に浮かんだ島であった。笠の材料の確保のため、笠縫氏は島に住んだと思われるが、もともと大和の笠縫邑も、同じような低湿地か、島状の土地だったのではないかとも推測される{{誰2|date=2024年6月}}。 現在も大嘗祭に使用する笠は、この深江から[[皇室]]へ献上されている。また、深江は、皇祖の御神鏡に関係する[[鋳物]]師とも関係が深い土地とのことである。[[鏡作神社]]（磯城郡田原本町八尾 等周辺）とも関係の可能性がある。
==脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="平成大禮要話/p182">{{Citation|和書 | last = 鎌田 | first = 純一 | author-link = 鎌田純一 | title = 平成大禮要話 : 即位禮・大嘗祭 | publisher = [[錦正社]] | date = 2003-7 | page = 182 | id = {{全国書誌番号|20452668}} | isbn = 4-7646-0262-8 }}</ref> }}
==関連項目== *[[元伊勢]] *[[笠縫駅]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:かさぬいむら}} [[Category:神道]] [[Category:元伊勢]] [[Category:奈良県の歴史]] [[Category:大阪府指定史跡]]

{{出典の明記|date=2024年6月}} {{独自研究|date=2024年6月}} {{言葉を濁さない|date=2024年6月}} '''笠縫邑'''（かさぬいむら、かさぬいのむら）とは、[[崇神天皇]]6年に、宮中に奉祀していた[[天照大神]]を移し、[[豊鍬入姫命]]に託して祀らせた場所。同時に宮中を出された[[倭大国魂神]]は[[渟名城入媛命]]に託して、後に[[大和神社]]に祀ったとされる{{要出典|date=2024年6月}}。
== 概要 == 笠縫邑は[[大嘗祭]]、[[豊明節会]]の起源に関係する土地との説もある{{要出典|date=2024年6月}}。[[垂仁天皇]]紀25年3月丙申条に、[[倭姫命]]は天照大神を[[磯城の厳橿の本]]に祀ったとあり、厳橿は神霊の神木で、崇神朝に[[磯堅城の神籬]]を立てた。（崇神紀６年条）。[[古語拾遺]]には「磯城の神籬」とあり、「磯城の厳橿の本」と「磯堅城の神籬」は同所とも考えられる{{誰2|date=2024年6月}}。

檜原神社、笠山荒神社、小夫天神社（笠山荒神社よりもさらに東の山間部）、[[穴師坐兵主神社]]等はまさに山の中である。 また、笠縫神社に関係する{{読み仮名|秦楽|じんらく}}という名が、[[築山古墳 (大和高田市)|築山古墳]]（[[大和高田市]]築山字城山）の前方部眼下に広がる平地にも{{読み仮名|神楽|じんらく}}という名で存在する。この神楽は、古くは秦楽と書いたとの説もある。何らかの関係を示唆しているかも知れない{{要出典|date=2024年6月}}。
多神社から、摂社の屋就神命神社（やつぎかみのみことじんじゃ、やつき？しんめいじんじゃ：祭神 天明豊玉命）の方角を見ると、神楽、築山古墳の方角となる。実際には、理由はよく判らないが地元住民によって大切に残された「矢継ぎの森（やつぎのもり：屋就神命神社の杜でもある）」に隠されて見えない。
また、もしかすると、昔は、多神社から姫皇子神社の方向を見ると、[[箸墓]]が見えたのかもしれない。元々、[[姫皇子神社]]は、多神社の奥（北：上座）に位置していたので、前（南：下座）に移転させられている可能性がある{{要出典|date=2024年6月}}。
[[多神社]]を笠縫邑の位置と比定するなら、これら[[古墳]]が、鏡に映した様に南北及び東西が逆になるが、[[大嘗祭]]の[[悠紀殿]]（[[千木]]は[[伊勢神宮]][[皇大神宮]]の様に内削ぎ）{{r|平成大禮要話/p182}}、主基殿（千木は伊勢神宮[[豊受大神宮]]の様に外削ぎ）{{r|平成大禮要話/p182}}の起源とも関係している可能性がある{{要出典|date=2024年6月}}。

revision.comment: Created page with "'''{{ill|Kasanui-mura|ja|笠縫邑}}''' (かさぬいむら, かさぬいのむら) is the place where **{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}** was **transferred from the imperial palace in the 6th year of Emperor {{ill|Suijin|ja|崇神天皇}}'s reign** and entrusted to **{{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|ja|豊鍬入姫命}}** for enshrinement. At the same time, **{{ill|Yamato Ōkunidama no Kami|ja|倭大国魂神}}**, who was also removed from the imperial palace, was ent..."
revision.text:
'''{{ill|Kasanui-mura|ja|笠縫邑}}''' (かさぬいむら, かさぬいのむら) is the place where **{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}** was **transferred from the imperial palace in the 6th year of Emperor {{ill|Suijin|ja|崇神天皇}}'s reign** and entrusted to **{{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|ja|豊鍬入姫命}}** for enshrinement. At the same time, **{{ill|Yamato Ōkunidama no Kami|ja|倭大国魂神}}**, who was also removed from the imperial palace, was entrusted to **{{ill|Nunakiirihime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入媛命}}** and later enshrined at **{{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}}**{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
== Overview == There is a theory that **Kasanui-mura is related to the origins of the {{ill|Daijōsai|ja|大嘗祭}} and {{ill|Toyoakari no Sechie|ja|豊明節会}}**{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}. According to the **25th year of Emperor {{ill|Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}'s reign**, **Princess {{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}}** enshrined **Amaterasu** at **{{ill|Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto|ja|磯城の厳橿の本}}**. The **Ihokashi tree** was considered a **sacred tree for divine spirits**, and during Emperor Suijin's reign, the **divine pillar of {{ill|Isokashiro Shrine|ja|磯堅城の神籬}}** was erected (**Suijin Chronicles, 6th year**){{who|date=June 2024}}. In **{{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}}**, it is referred to as **"Ishiki no Himorogi"**, suggesting that **"Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto" and "Isokashiro no Himorogi"** might be the same place{{who|date=June 2024}}.
### Possible Locations There are various theories about the **location of Kasanui-mura**, including: * **{{ill|Hibara Shrine (Nara)|ja|檜原神社}}** ({{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}} **Miwa**) * **{{ill|Tashinja|ja|多神社}}** ({{ill|Shiki District, Nara|ja|磯城郡}} **Tawaramoto Town, Ta**) * **{{ill|Kasanui Shrine|ja|笠縫神社}}** (Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, **Hazushō**, located in the **southeastern corner of {{ill|Jinraku-ji|ja|秦楽寺}}**) * **{{ill|Kasayama Kōjinja|ja|笠山荒神社}}** (Sakurai, **Kasa**) * **Hime-Ōji Shrine** (a subsidiary shrine of Tashinja) * **{{ill|Shiki Mikenuza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社}}** (Sakurai, Kanaya) * **{{ill|Obu Tenjinja|ja|小夫天神社}}** (Sakurai, Obu) * **{{ill|Anashizahyozu Shrine|ja|穴師坐兵主神社}}** (Sakurai, Anashi) * **{{ill|Asuka Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社}}** ({{ill|Takaichi District, Nara|ja|高市郡}}, **Asuka Village, Asuka**) * **Haseyamaguchi Shrine** (Sakurai, Hase-Tejikarao){{citation needed|date=June 2024}}
Some shrines, such as **Hibara Shrine, Kasayama Kōjinja, and Obu Tenjinja**, are **located deep in the mountains**. Additionally, the name **Jinraku (秦楽)**, associated with Kasanui Shrine, is also found **in the plains below the front part of the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun|ja|築山古墳}} in {{ill|Yamatotakada, Nara|ja|大和高田市}}**, where it was historically written as **"神楽" (Jinraku/Kagura)**. This could suggest some connection{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
From **Tashinja**, looking in the direction of **Yatsugikamino Mikoto Shrine (矢継ぎ神命神社)** (dedicated to **Tenmyō Toyotama no Mikoto**), one faces the **Jinraku and Tsukiyama Kofun direction**. However, due to **Yatsugi no Mori (矢継ぎの森)**, a sacred grove preserved by locals, **the view is blocked**.
It is also speculated that **in ancient times, the view from Tashinja to Hime-Ōji Shrine may have included the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓}}**. Originally, **Hime-Ōji Shrine was located to the north (upper seat) of Tashinja**, but was possibly **relocated southward (lower seat)**{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
If **Tashinja is indeed the site of Kasanui-mura**, then these kofun formations might correspond to **the origins of the {{ill|Daijōsai|ja|大嘗祭}}'s {{ill|Yuki-den|ja|悠紀殿}}** (which has **cut-inward 千木 [Chigi] like {{ill|Kōtai Jingu|ja|皇大神宮}}**) and **Shuki-den (主基殿)** (which has **cut-outward Chigi like {{ill|Toyoukedai Jingu|ja|豊受大神宮}}**){{r|Heisei Daijōsai/p182}}{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
== Kasanui Island in Osaka == At **{{ill|Fukae Inari Shrine|ja|深江稲荷神社}}** ({{ill|Higashinari-ku, Osaka|ja|大阪市 東成区}} **Fukaeminami**), there is a tradition that **Fukae was once the residence of the {{ill|Kasanui clan|ja|笠縫氏}}**, who migrated from **Kasanui-mura in Yamato**.
The **Man'yōshū poet, {{ill|Takechi no Kurohito|ja|高市黒人}}**, wrote: > *"Shihatsu-yama uchi-koe mireba Kasanui no shima kogi-kakuru tana nashi kobune"* > *("Crossing over Mount Shihatsu, I see Kasanui Island, where small boats without roofs disappear.")*
In ancient times, **Kasanui Island** was believed to have been an island in an inlet **stretching from present-day Fukae to {{ill|Higashiōsaka, Osaka|ja|東大阪市}} Ashiya**. The **Kasanui clan likely lived on the island** to secure **materials for making straw hats (笠, kasa)**. This suggests that **the original Kasanui-mura in Yamato might also have been a low-lying wetland or an island-like terrain**{{who|date=June 2024}}.
Even today, **the straw hats used in the Daijōsai are made in Fukae and presented to the Imperial Household**. Additionally, **Fukae has strong historical ties to the casting of the Imperial Divine Mirror (御神鏡)**, suggesting a connection with **{{ill|Kagamitsukuri Shrine|ja|鏡作神社}}** in **Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, Yao**{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Motoise|ja|元伊勢}} * {{ill|Kasanui Station|ja|笠縫駅}}
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="Heisei Daijōsai/p182">{{Citation|和書 | last = Kamata | first = Junichi | author-link = 鎌田純一 | title = Heisei Daijōsai Yōwa: Sokui-rei & Daijōsai | publisher = {{ill|Kinsei-sha|ja|錦正社}} | date = July 2003 | page = 182 | id = {{NDL|20452668}} | isbn = 4-7646-0262-8 }}</ref> }}
{{DEFAULTSORT:Kasanui-mura}} [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Motoise]] [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]]

'''{{ill|Kasanui-mura|ja|笠縫邑}}''' (かさぬいむら, かさぬいのむら) is the place where **{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}** was **transferred from the imperial palace in the 6th year of Emperor {{ill|Suijin|ja|崇神天皇}}'s reign** and entrusted to **{{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|ja|豊鍬入姫命}}** for enshrinement. At the same time, **{{ill|Yamato Ōkunidama no Kami|ja|倭大国魂神}}**, who was also removed from the imperial palace, was entrusted to **{{ill|Nunakiirihime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入媛命}}** and later enshrined at **{{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}}**{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
== Overview == There is a theory that **Kasanui-mura is related to the origins of the {{ill|Daijōsai|ja|大嘗祭}} and {{ill|Toyoakari no Sechie|ja|豊明節会}}**{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}. According to the **25th year of Emperor {{ill|Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}'s reign**, **Princess {{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}}** enshrined **Amaterasu** at **{{ill|Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto|ja|磯城の厳橿の本}}**. The **Ihokashi tree** was considered a **sacred tree for divine spirits**, and during Emperor Suijin's reign, the **divine pillar of {{ill|Isokashiro Shrine|ja|磯堅城の神籬}}** was erected (**Suijin Chronicles, 6th year**){{who|date=June 2024}}. In **{{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}}**, it is referred to as **"Ishiki no Himorogi"**, suggesting that **"Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto" and "Isokashiro no Himorogi"** might be the same place{{who|date=June 2024}}.
### Possible Locations There are various theories about the **location of Kasanui-mura**, including: * **{{ill|Hibara Shrine (Nara)|ja|檜原神社}}** ({{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}} **Miwa**) * **{{ill|Tashinja|ja|多神社}}** ({{ill|Shiki District, Nara|ja|磯城郡}} **Tawaramoto Town, Ta**) * **{{ill|Kasanui Shrine|ja|笠縫神社}}** (Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, **Hazushō**, located in the **southeastern corner of {{ill|Jinraku-ji|ja|秦楽寺}}**) * **{{ill|Kasayama Kōjinja|ja|笠山荒神社}}** (Sakurai, **Kasa**) * **Hime-Ōji Shrine** (a subsidiary shrine of Tashinja) * **{{ill|Shiki Mikenuza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社}}** (Sakurai, Kanaya) * **{{ill|Obu Tenjinja|ja|小夫天神社}}** (Sakurai, Obu) * **{{ill|Anashizahyozu Shrine|ja|穴師坐兵主神社}}** (Sakurai, Anashi) * **{{ill|Asuka Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社}}** ({{ill|Takaichi District, Nara|ja|高市郡}}, **Asuka Village, Asuka**) * **Haseyamaguchi Shrine** (Sakurai, Hase-Tejikarao){{citation needed|date=June 2024}}
Some shrines, such as **Hibara Shrine, Kasayama Kōjinja, and Obu Tenjinja**, are **located deep in the mountains**. Additionally, the name **Jinraku (秦楽)**, associated with Kasanui Shrine, is also found **in the plains below the front part of the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun|ja|築山古墳}} in {{ill|Yamatotakada, Nara|ja|大和高田市}}**, where it was historically written as **"神楽" (Jinraku/Kagura)**. This could suggest some connection{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
From **Tashinja**, looking in the direction of **Yatsugikamino Mikoto Shrine (矢継ぎ神命神社)** (dedicated to **Tenmyō Toyotama no Mikoto**), one faces the **Jinraku and Tsukiyama Kofun direction**. However, due to **Yatsugi no Mori (矢継ぎの森)**, a sacred grove preserved by locals, **the view is blocked**.
It is also speculated that **in ancient times, the view from Tashinja to Hime-Ōji Shrine may have included the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓}}**. Originally, **Hime-Ōji Shrine was located to the north (upper seat) of Tashinja**, but was possibly **relocated southward (lower seat)**{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
If **Tashinja is indeed the site of Kasanui-mura**, then these kofun formations might correspond to **the origins of the {{ill|Daijōsai|ja|大嘗祭}}'s {{ill|Yuki-den|ja|悠紀殿}}** (which has **cut-inward 千木 [Chigi] like {{ill|Kōtai Jingu|ja|皇大神宮}}**) and **Shuki-den (主基殿)** (which has **cut-outward Chigi like {{ill|Toyoukedai Jingu|ja|豊受大神宮}}**){{r|Heisei Daijōsai/p182}}{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
== Kasanui Island in Osaka == At **{{ill|Fukae Inari Shrine|ja|深江稲荷神社}}** ({{ill|Higashinari-ku, Osaka|ja|大阪市 東成区}} **Fukaeminami**), there is a tradition that **Fukae was once the residence of the {{ill|Kasanui clan|ja|笠縫氏}}**, who migrated from **Kasanui-mura in Yamato**.
The **Man'yōshū poet, {{ill|Takechi no Kurohito|ja|高市黒人}}**, wrote: > *"Shihatsu-yama uchi-koe mireba Kasanui no shima kogi-kakuru tana nashi kobune"* > *("Crossing over Mount Shihatsu, I see Kasanui Island, where small boats without roofs disappear.")*
In ancient times, **Kasanui Island** was believed to have been an island in an inlet **stretching from present-day Fukae to {{ill|Higashiōsaka, Osaka|ja|東大阪市}} Ashiya**. The **Kasanui clan likely lived on the island** to secure **materials for making straw hats (笠, kasa)**. This suggests that **the original Kasanui-mura in Yamato might also have been a low-lying wetland or an island-like terrain**{{who|date=June 2024}}.
Even today, **the straw hats used in the Daijōsai are made in Fukae and presented to the Imperial Household**. Additionally, **Fukae has strong historical ties to the casting of the Imperial Divine Mirror (御神鏡)**, suggesting a connection with **{{ill|Kagamitsukuri Shrine|ja|鏡作神社}}** in **Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, Yao**{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.

Some shrines, such as **Hibara Shrine, Kasayama Kōjinja, and Obu Tenjinja**, are **located deep in the mountains**. Additionally, the name **Jinraku (秦楽)**, associated with Kasanui Shrine, is also found **in the plains below the front part of the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun|ja|築山古墳}} in {{ill|Yamatotakada, Nara|ja|大和高田市}}**, where it was historically written as **"神楽" (Jinraku/Kagura)**. This could suggest some connection{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
From **Tashinja**, looking in the direction of **Yatsugikamino Mikoto Shrine (矢継ぎ神命神社)** (dedicated to **Tenmyō Toyotama no Mikoto**), one faces the **Jinraku and Tsukiyama Kofun direction**. However, due to **Yatsugi no Mori (矢継ぎの森)**, a sacred grove preserved by locals, **the view is blocked**.
It is also speculated that **in ancient times, the view from Tashinja to Hime-Ōji Shrine may have included the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓}}**. Originally, **Hime-Ōji Shrine was located to the north (upper seat) of Tashinja**, but was possibly **relocated southward (lower seat)**{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
If **Tashinja is indeed the site of Kasanui-mura**, then these kofun formations might correspond to **the origins of the {{ill|Daijōsai|ja|大嘗祭}}'s {{ill|Yuki-den|ja|悠紀殿}}** (which has **cut-inward 千木 [Chigi] like {{ill|Kōtai Jingu|ja|皇大神宮}}**) and **Shuki-den (主基殿)** (which has **cut-outward Chigi like {{ill|Toyoukedai Jingu|ja|豊受大神宮}}**){{r|Heisei Daijōsai/p182}}{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
== Kasanui Island in Osaka == At **{{ill|Fukae Inari Shrine|ja|深江稲荷神社}}** ({{ill|Higashinari-ku, Osaka|ja|大阪市 東成区}} **Fukaeminami**), there is a tradition that **Fukae was once the residence of the {{ill|Kasanui clan|ja|笠縫氏}}**, who migrated from **Kasanui-mura in Yamato**.
The **Man'yōshū poet, {{ill|Takechi no Kurohito|ja|高市黒人}}**, wrote: > *"Shihatsu-yama uchi-koe mireba Kasanui no shima kogi-kakuru tana nashi kobune"* > *("Crossing over Mount Shihatsu, I see Kasanui Island, where small boats without roofs disappear.")*
In ancient times, **Kasanui Island** was believed to have been an island in an inlet **stretching from present-day Fukae to {{ill|Higashiōsaka, Osaka|ja|東大阪市}} Ashiya**. The **Kasanui clan likely lived on the island** to secure **materials for making straw hats (笠, kasa)**. This suggests that **the original Kasanui-mura in Yamato might also have been a low-lying wetland or an island-like terrain**{{who|date=June 2024}}.
Even today, **the straw hats used in the Daijōsai are made in Fukae and presented to the Imperial Household**. Additionally, **Fukae has strong historical ties to the casting of the Imperial Divine Mirror (御神鏡)**, suggesting a connection with **{{ill|Kagamitsukuri Shrine|ja|鏡作神社}}** in **Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, Yao**{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Motoise|ja|元伊勢}} * {{ill|Kasanui Station|ja|笠縫駅}}
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="Heisei Daijōsai/p182">{{Citation|和書 | last = Kamata | first = Junichi | author-link = 鎌田純一 | title = Heisei Daijōsai Yōwa: Sokui-rei & Daijōsai | publisher = {{ill|Kinsei-sha|ja|錦正社}} | date = July 2003 | page = 182 | id = {{NDL|20452668}} | isbn = 4-7646-0262-8 }}</ref> }}
{{DEFAULTSORT:Kasanui-mura}} [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Motoise]] [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:笠縫邑]] [[Category:元伊勢]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Kasanuimura]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:笠縫邑]]

'''{{ill|Kasanui-mura|ja|笠縫邑}}''' (かさぬいむら, かさぬいのむら) is the place where **{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}** was **transferred from the imperial palace in the 6th year of Emperor {{ill|Suijin|ja|崇神天皇}}'s reign** and entrusted to **{{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|ja|豊鍬入姫命}}** for enshrinement. At the same time, **{{ill|Yamato Ōkunidama no Kami|ja|倭大国魂神}}**, who was also removed from the imperial palace, was entrusted to **{{ill|Nunakiirihime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入媛命}}** and later enshrined at **{{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}}**{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
== Overview == There is a theory that **Kasanui-mura is related to the origins of the {{ill|Daijōsai|ja|大嘗祭}} and {{ill|Toyoakari no Sechie|ja|豊明節会}}**{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}. According to the **25th year of Emperor {{ill|Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}'s reign**, **Princess {{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}}** enshrined **Amaterasu** at **{{ill|Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto|ja|磯城の厳橿の本}}**. The **Ihokashi tree** was considered a **sacred tree for divine spirits**, and during Emperor Suijin's reign, the **divine pillar of {{ill|Isokashiro Shrine|ja|磯堅城の神籬}}** was erected (**Suijin Chronicles, 6th year**){{who|date=June 2024}}. In **{{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}}**, it is referred to as **"Ishiki no Himorogi"**, suggesting that **"Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto" and "Isokashiro no Himorogi"** might be the same place{{who|date=June 2024}}.
### Possible Locations There are various theories about the **location of Kasanui-mura**, including: * **{{ill|Hibara Shrine (Nara)|ja|檜原神社}}** ({{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}} **Miwa**) * **{{ill|Tashinja|ja|多神社}}** ({{ill|Shiki District, Nara|ja|磯城郡}} **Tawaramoto Town, Ta**) * **{{ill|Kasanui Shrine|ja|笠縫神社}}** (Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, **Hazushō**, located in the **southeastern corner of {{ill|Jinraku-ji|ja|秦楽寺}}**) * **{{ill|Kasayama Kōjinja|ja|笠山荒神社}}** (Sakurai, **Kasa**) * **Hime-Ōji Shrine** (a subsidiary shrine of Tashinja) * **{{ill|Shiki Mikenuza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社}}** (Sakurai, Kanaya) * **{{ill|Obu Tenjinja|ja|小夫天神社}}** (Sakurai, Obu) * **{{ill|Anashizahyozu Shrine|ja|穴師坐兵主神社}}** (Sakurai, Anashi) * **{{ill|Asuka Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社}}** ({{ill|Takaichi District, Nara|ja|高市郡}}, **Asuka Village, Asuka**) * **Haseyamaguchi Shrine** (Sakurai, Hase-Tejikarao){{citation needed|date=June 2024}}
Some shrines, such as **Hibara Shrine, Kasayama Kōjinja, and Obu Tenjinja**, are **located deep in the mountains**. Additionally, the name **Jinraku (秦楽)**, associated with Kasanui Shrine, is also found **in the plains below the front part of the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun|ja|築山古墳}} in {{ill|Yamatotakada, Nara|ja|大和高田市}}**, where it was historically written as **"神楽" (Jinraku/Kagura)**. This could suggest some connection{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
From **Tashinja**, looking in the direction of **Yatsugikamino Mikoto Shrine (矢継ぎ神命神社)** (dedicated to **Tenmyō Toyotama no Mikoto**), one faces the **Jinraku and Tsukiyama Kofun direction**. However, due to **Yatsugi no Mori (矢継ぎの森)**, a sacred grove preserved by locals, **the view is blocked**.
It is also speculated that **in ancient times, the view from Tashinja to Hime-Ōji Shrine may have included the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓}}**. Originally, **Hime-Ōji Shrine was located to the north (upper seat) of Tashinja**, but was possibly **relocated southward (lower seat)**{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
If **Tashinja is indeed the site of Kasanui-mura**, then these kofun formations might correspond to **the origins of the {{ill|Daijōsai|ja|大嘗祭}}'s {{ill|Yuki-den|ja|悠紀殿}}** (which has **cut-inward 千木 [Chigi] like {{ill|Kōtai Jingu|ja|皇大神宮}}**) and **Shuki-den (主基殿)** (which has **cut-outward Chigi like {{ill|Toyoukedai Jingu|ja|豊受大神宮}}**){{r|Heisei Daijōsai/p182}}{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
== Kasanui Island in Osaka == At **{{ill|Fukae Inari Shrine|ja|深江稲荷神社}}** ({{ill|Higashinari-ku, Osaka|ja|大阪市 東成区}} **Fukaeminami**), there is a tradition that **Fukae was once the residence of the {{ill|Kasanui clan|ja|笠縫氏}}**, who migrated from **Kasanui-mura in Yamato**.
The **Man'yōshū poet, {{ill|Takechi no Kurohito|ja|高市黒人}}**, wrote: > *"Shihatsu-yama uchi-koe mireba Kasanui no shima kogi-kakuru tana nashi kobune"* > *("Crossing over Mount Shihatsu, I see Kasanui Island, where small boats without roofs disappear.")*

The **Kasanui clan likely lived on the island** to secure **materials for making straw hats (笠, kasa)**. This suggests that **the original Kasanui-mura in Yamato might also have been a low-lying wetland or an island-like terrain**{{who|date=June 2024}}.
Even today, **the straw hats used in the Daijōsai are made in Fukae and presented to the Imperial Household**. Additionally, **Fukae has strong historical ties to the casting of the Imperial Divine Mirror (御神鏡)**, suggesting a connection with **{{ill|Kagamitsukuri Shrine|ja|鏡作神社}}** in **Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, Yao**{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.

Originally, **Hime-Ōji Shrine was located to the north (upper seat) of Tashinja**, but was possibly **relocated southward (lower seat)**{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.

== Kasanui Island in Osaka == At **{{ill|Fukae Inari Shrine|ja|深江稲荷神社}}** ({{ill|Higashinari-ku, Osaka|ja|大阪市 東成区}} **Fukaeminami**), there is a tradition that **Fukae was once the residence of the {{ill|Kasanui clan|ja|笠縫氏}}**, who migrated from **Kasanui-mura in Yamato**.
The **Man'yōshū poet, {{ill|Takechi no Kurohito|ja|高市黒人}}**, wrote: > *"Shihatsu-yama uchi-koe mireba Kasanui no shima kogi-kakuru tana nashi kobune"* > *("Crossing over Mount Shihatsu, I see Kasanui Island, where small boats without roofs disappear.")*
In ancient times, **Kasanui Island** was believed to have been an island in an inlet **stretching from present-day Fukae to {{ill|Higashiōsaka, Osaka|ja|東大阪市}} Ashiya**. The **Kasanui clan likely lived on the island** to secure **materials for making straw hats (笠, kasa)**. This suggests that **the original Kasanui-mura in Yamato might also have been a low-lying wetland or an island-like terrain**{{who|date=June 2024}}.
Even today, **the straw hats used in the Daijōsai are made in Fukae and presented to the Imperial Household**. Additionally, **Fukae has strong historical ties to the casting of the Imperial Divine Mirror (御神鏡)**, suggesting a connection with **{{ill|Kagamitsukuri Shrine|ja|鏡作神社}}** in **Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, Yao**{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Motoise|ja|元伊勢}} * {{ill|Kasanui Station|ja|笠縫駅}}
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="Heisei Daijōsai/p182">{{Citation|和書 | last = Kamata | first = Junichi | author-link = 鎌田純一 | title = Heisei Daijōsai Yōwa: Sokui-rei & Daijōsai | publisher = {{ill|Kinsei-sha|ja|錦正社}} | date = July 2003 | page = 182 | id = {{NDL|20452668}} | isbn = 4-7646-0262-8 }}</ref> }}
{{DEFAULTSORT:Kasanui-mura}} [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Motoise]] [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:笠縫邑]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[en:Kasanuimura]][[ja:笠縫邑]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]]

'''{{ill|Kasanui-mura|ja|笠縫邑}}''' (かさぬいむら, かさぬいのむら) is the place where **{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}** was **transferred from the imperial palace in the 6th year of Emperor {{ill|Suijin|ja|崇神天皇}}'s reign** and entrusted to **{{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|ja|豊鍬入姫命}}** for enshrinement. At the same time, **{{ill|Yamato Ōkunidama no Kami|ja|倭大国魂神}}**, who was also removed from the imperial palace, was entrusted to **{{ill|Nunakiirihime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入媛命}}** and later enshrined at **{{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}}**{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
== Overview == There is a theory that **Kasanui-mura is related to the origins of the {{ill|Daijōsai|ja|大嘗祭}} and {{ill|Toyoakari no Sechie|ja|豊明節会}}**{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}. According to the **25th year of Emperor {{ill|Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}'s reign**, **Princess {{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}}** enshrined **Amaterasu** at **{{ill|Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto|ja|磯城の厳橿の本}}**. The **Ihokashi tree** was considered a **sacred tree for divine spirits**, and during Emperor Suijin's reign, the **divine pillar of {{ill|Isokashiro Shrine|ja|磯堅城の神籬}}** was erected (**Suijin Chronicles, 6th year**){{who|date=June 2024}}. In **{{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}}**, it is referred to as **"Ishiki no Himorogi"**, suggesting that **"Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto" and "Isokashiro no Himorogi"** might be the same place{{who|date=June 2024}}.
### Possible Locations There are various theories about the **location of Kasanui-mura**, including: * **{{ill|Hibara Shrine (Nara)|ja|檜原神社}}** ({{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}} **Miwa**) * **{{ill|Tashinja|ja|多神社}}** ({{ill|Shiki District, Nara|ja|磯城郡}} **Tawaramoto Town, Ta**) * **{{ill|Kasanui Shrine|ja|笠縫神社}}** (Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, **Hazushō**, located in the **southeastern corner of {{ill|Jinraku-ji|ja|秦楽寺}}**) * **{{ill|Kasayama Kōjinja|ja|笠山荒神社}}** (Sakurai, **Kasa**) * **Hime-Ōji Shrine** (a subsidiary shrine of Tashinja) * **{{ill|Shiki Mikenuza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社}}** (Sakurai, Kanaya) * **{{ill|Obu Tenjinja|ja|小夫天神社}}** (Sakurai, Obu) * **{{ill|Anashizahyozu Shrine|ja|穴師坐兵主神社}}** (Sakurai, Anashi) * **{{ill|Asuka Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社}}** ({{ill|Takaichi District, Nara|ja|高市郡}}, **Asuka Village, Asuka**) * **Haseyamaguchi Shrine** (Sakurai, Hase-Tejikarao){{citation needed|date=June 2024}}
Some shrines, such as **Hibara Shrine, Kasayama Kōjinja, and Obu Tenjinja**, are **located deep in the mountains**. Additionally, the name **Jinraku (秦楽)**, associated with Kasanui Shrine, is also found **in the plains below the front part of the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun|ja|築山古墳}} in {{ill|Yamatotakada, Nara|ja|大和高田市}}**, where it was historically written as **"神楽" (Jinraku/Kagura)**. This could suggest some connection{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
From **Tashinja**, looking in the direction of **Yatsugikamino Mikoto Shrine (矢継ぎ神命神社)** (dedicated to **Tenmyō Toyotama no Mikoto**), one faces the **Jinraku and Tsukiyama Kofun direction**. However, due to **Yatsugi no Mori (矢継ぎの森)**, a sacred grove preserved by locals, **the view is blocked**.
It is also speculated that **in ancient times, the view from Tashinja to Hime-Ōji Shrine may have included the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓}}**. Originally, **Hime-Ōji Shrine was located to the north (upper seat) of Tashinja**, but was possibly **relocated southward (lower seat)**{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
If **Tashinja is indeed the site of Kasanui-mura**, then these kofun formations might correspond to **the origins of the {{ill|Daijōsai|ja|大嘗祭}}'s {{ill|Yuki-den|ja|悠紀殿}}** (which has **cut-inward 千木 [Chigi] like {{ill|Kōtai Jingu|ja|皇大神宮}}**) and **Shuki-den (主基殿)** (which has **cut-outward Chigi like {{ill|Toyoukedai Jingu|ja|豊受大神宮}}**){{r|Heisei Daijōsai/p182}}{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
== Kasanui Island in Osaka == At **{{ill|Fukae Inari Shrine|ja|深江稲荷神社}}** ({{ill|Higashinari-ku, Osaka|ja|大阪市 東成区}} **Fukaeminami**), there is a tradition that **Fukae was once the residence of the {{ill|Kasanui clan|ja|笠縫氏}}**, who migrated from **Kasanui-mura in Yamato**.
The **Man'yōshū poet, {{ill|Takechi no Kurohito|ja|高市黒人}}**, wrote: > *"Shihatsu-yama uchi-koe mireba Kasanui no shima kogi-kakuru tana nashi kobune"* > *("Crossing over Mount Shihatsu, I see Kasanui Island, where small boats without roofs disappear.")*
In ancient times, **Kasanui Island** was believed to have been an island in an inlet **stretching from present-day Fukae to {{ill|Higashiōsaka, Osaka|ja|東大阪市}} Ashiya**. The **Kasanui clan likely lived on the island** to secure **materials for making straw hats (笠, kasa)**. This suggests that **the original Kasanui-mura in Yamato might also have been a low-lying wetland or an island-like terrain**{{who|date=June 2024}}.
Even today, **the straw hats used in the Daijōsai are made in Fukae and presented to the Imperial Household**. Additionally, **Fukae has strong historical ties to the casting of the Imperial Divine Mirror (御神鏡)**, suggesting a connection with **{{ill|Kagamitsukuri Shrine|ja|鏡作神社}}** in **Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, Yao**{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Motoise|ja|元伊勢}} * {{ill|Kasanui Station|ja|笠縫駅}}
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="Heisei Daijōsai/p182">{{Citation|和書 | last = Kamata | first = Junichi | author-link = 鎌田純一 | title = Heisei Daijōsai Yōwa: Sokui-rei & Daijōsai | publisher = {{ill|Kinsei-sha|ja|錦正社}} | date = July 2003 | page = 182 | id = {{NDL|20452668}} | isbn = 4-7646-0262-8 }}</ref> }}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "皇大神宮}}" to "皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}"
revision.text:
'''{{ill|Kasanui-mura|ja|笠縫邑}}''' (かさぬいむら, かさぬいのむら) is the place where **{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}** was **transferred from the imperial palace in the 6th year of Emperor {{ill|Suijin|ja|崇神天皇}}'s reign** and entrusted to **{{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime}}** for enshrinement. At the same time, **{{ill|Yamato Ōkunidama no Kami|ja|倭大国魂神}}**, who was also removed from the imperial palace, was entrusted to **{{ill|Nunakiirihime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入媛命}}** and later enshrined at **{{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}}**{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
== Overview == There is a theory that **Kasanui-mura is related to the origins of the {{ill|Daijōsai|ja|大嘗祭}} and {{ill|Toyoakari no Sechie|ja|豊明節会}}**{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}. According to the **25th year of Emperor {{ill|Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}'s reign**, **Princess {{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}** enshrined **Amaterasu** at **{{ill|Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto|ja|磯城の厳橿の本}}**. The **Ihokashi tree** was considered a **sacred tree for divine spirits**, and during Emperor Suijin's reign, the **divine pillar of {{ill|Isokashiro Shrine|ja|磯堅城の神籬}}** was erected (**Suijin Chronicles, 6th year**){{who|date=June 2024}}. In **{{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}}**, it is referred to as **"Ishiki no Himorogi"**, suggesting that **"Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto" and "Isokashiro no Himorogi"** might be the same place{{who|date=June 2024}}.
### Possible Locations There are various theories about the **location of Kasanui-mura**, including: * **{{ill|Hibara Shrine (Nara)|ja|檜原神社}}** ({{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}} **Miwa**) * **{{ill|Tashinja|ja|多神社}}** ({{ill|Shiki District, Nara|ja|磯城郡}} **Tawaramoto Town, Ta**) * **{{ill|Kasanui Shrine|ja|笠縫神社}}** (Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, **Hazushō**, located in the **southeastern corner of {{ill|Jinraku-ji|ja|秦楽寺}}**) * **{{ill|Kasayama Kōjinja|ja|笠山荒神社}}** (Sakurai, **Kasa**) * **Hime-Ōji Shrine** (a subsidiary shrine of Tashinja) * **{{ill|Shiki Mikenuza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社}}** (Sakurai, Kanaya) * **{{ill|Obu Tenjinja|ja|小夫天神社}}** (Sakurai, Obu) * **{{ill|Anashizahyozu Shrine|ja|穴師坐兵主神社}}** (Sakurai, Anashi) * **{{ill|Asuka Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社}}** ({{ill|Takaichi District, Nara|ja|高市郡}}, **Asuka Village, Asuka**) * **Haseyamaguchi Shrine** (Sakurai, Hase-Tejikarao){{citation needed|date=June 2024}}
Some shrines, such as **Hibara Shrine, Kasayama Kōjinja, and Obu Tenjinja**, are **located deep in the mountains**. Additionally, the name **Jinraku (秦楽)**, associated with Kasanui Shrine, is also found **in the plains below the front part of the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun|ja|築山古墳}} in {{ill|Yamatotakada, Nara|ja|大和高田市}}**, where it was historically written as **"神楽" (Jinraku/Kagura)**. This could suggest some connection{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
From **Tashinja**, looking in the direction of **Yatsugikamino Mikoto Shrine (矢継ぎ神命神社)** (dedicated to **Tenmyō Toyotama no Mikoto**), one faces the **Jinraku and Tsukiyama Kofun direction**. However, due to **Yatsugi no Mori (矢継ぎの森)**, a sacred grove preserved by locals, **the view is blocked**.
It is also speculated that **in ancient times, the view from Tashinja to Hime-Ōji Shrine may have included the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓}}**. Originally, **Hime-Ōji Shrine was located to the north (upper seat) of Tashinja**, but was possibly **relocated southward (lower seat)**{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
If **Tashinja is indeed the site of Kasanui-mura**, then these kofun formations might correspond to **the origins of the {{ill|Daijōsai|ja|大嘗祭}}'s {{ill|Yuki-den|ja|悠紀殿}}** (which has **cut-inward 千木 [Chigi] like {{ill|Kōtai Jingu|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}**) and **Shuki-den (主基殿)** (which has **cut-outward Chigi like {{ill|Toyoukedai Jingu|ja|豊受大神宮}}**){{r|Heisei Daijōsai/p182}}{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
== Kasanui Island in Osaka == At **{{ill|Fukae Inari Shrine|ja|深江稲荷神社}}** ({{ill|Higashinari-ku, Osaka|ja|大阪市 東成区}} **Fukaeminami**), there is a tradition that **Fukae was once the residence of the {{ill|Kasanui clan|ja|笠縫氏}}**, who migrated from **Kasanui-mura in Yamato**.
The **Man'yōshū poet, {{ill|Takechi no Kurohito|ja|高市黒人}}**, wrote: > *"Shihatsu-yama uchi-koe mireba Kasanui no shima kogi-kakuru tana nashi kobune"* > *("Crossing over Mount Shihatsu, I see Kasanui Island, where small boats without roofs disappear.")*
In ancient times, **Kasanui Island** was believed to have been an island in an inlet **stretching from present-day Fukae to {{ill|Higashiōsaka, Osaka|ja|東大阪市}} Ashiya**. The **Kasanui clan likely lived on the island** to secure **materials for making straw hats (笠, kasa)**. This suggests that **the original Kasanui-mura in Yamato might also have been a low-lying wetland or an island-like terrain**{{who|date=June 2024}}.
Even today, **the straw hats used in the Daijōsai are made in Fukae and presented to the Imperial Household**. Additionally, **Fukae has strong historical ties to the casting of the Imperial Divine Mirror (御神鏡)**, suggesting a connection with **{{ill|Kagamitsukuri Shrine|ja|鏡作神社}}** in **Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, Yao**{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Motoise|ja|元伊勢}} * {{ill|Kasanui Station|ja|笠縫駅}}
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="Heisei Daijōsai/p182">{{Citation|和書 | last = Kamata | first = Junichi | author-link = 鎌田純一 | title = Heisei Daijōsai Yōwa: Sokui-rei & Daijōsai | publisher = {{ill|Kinsei-sha|ja|錦正社}} | date = July 2003 | page = 182 | id = {{NDL|20452668}} | isbn = 4-7646-0262-8 }}</ref> }}

At the same time, **{{ill|Yamato Ōkunidama no Kami|ja|倭大国魂神}}**, who was also removed from the imperial palace, was entrusted to **{{ill|Nunakiirihime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入媛命}}** and later enshrined at **{{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}}**{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
== Overview == There is a theory that **Kasanui-mura is related to the origins of the {{ill|Daijōsai|ja|大嘗祭}} and {{ill|Toyoakari no Sechie|ja|豊明節会}}**{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}. According to the **25th year of Emperor {{ill|Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}'s reign**, **Princess {{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}** enshrined **Amaterasu** at **{{ill|Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto|ja|磯城の厳橿の本}}**. The **Ihokashi tree** was considered a **sacred tree for divine spirits**, and during Emperor Suijin's reign, the **divine pillar of {{ill|Isokashiro Shrine|ja|磯堅城の神籬}}** was erected (**Suijin Chronicles, 6th year**){{who|date=June 2024}}. In **{{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}}**, it is referred to as **"Ishiki no Himorogi"**, suggesting that **"Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto" and "Isokashiro no Himorogi"** might be the same place{{who|date=June 2024}}.
=== Possible Locations There are various theories about the **location of Kasanui-mura**, including: * **{{ill|Hibara Shrine (Nara)|ja|檜原神社}}** ({{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}} **Miwa**) * **{{ill|Tashinja|ja|多神社}}** ({{ill|Shiki District, Nara|ja|磯城郡}} **Tawaramoto Town, Ta**) * **{{ill|Kasanui Shrine|ja|笠縫神社}}** (Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, **Hazushō**, located in the **southeastern corner of {{ill|Jinraku-ji|ja|秦楽寺}}**) * **{{ill|Kasayama Kōjinja|ja|笠山荒神社}}** (Sakurai, **Kasa**) * **Hime-Ōji Shrine** (a subsidiary shrine of Tashinja) * **{{ill|Shiki Mikenuza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社}}** (Sakurai, Kanaya) * **{{ill|Obu Tenjinja|ja|小夫天神社}}** (Sakurai, Obu) * **{{ill|Anashizahyozu Shrine|ja|穴師坐兵主神社}}** (Sakurai, Anashi) * **{{ill|Asuka Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社}}** ({{ill|Takaichi District, Nara|ja|高市郡}}, **Asuka Village, Asuka**) * **Haseyamaguchi Shrine** (Sakurai, Hase-Tejikarao){{citation needed|date=June 2024}}
Some shrines, such as **Hibara Shrine, Kasayama Kōjinja, and Obu Tenjinja**, are **located deep in the mountains**. Additionally, the name **Jinraku (秦楽)**, associated with Kasanui Shrine, is also found **in the plains below the front part of the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun|ja|築山古墳}} in {{ill|Yamatotakada, Nara|ja|大和高田市}}**, where it was historically written as **"神楽" (Jinraku/Kagura)**. This could suggest some connection{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
From **Tashinja**, looking in the direction of **Yatsugikamino Mikoto Shrine (矢継ぎ神命神社)** (dedicated to **Tenmyō Toyotama no Mikoto**), one faces the **Jinraku and Tsukiyama Kofun direction**. However, due to **Yatsugi no Mori (矢継ぎの森)**, a sacred grove preserved by locals, **the view is blocked**.
It is also speculated that **in ancient times, the view from Tashinja to Hime-Ōji Shrine may have included the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓}}**. Originally, **Hime-Ōji Shrine was located to the north (upper seat) of Tashinja**, but was possibly **relocated southward (lower seat)**{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
If **Tashinja is indeed the site of Kasanui-mura**, then these kofun formations might correspond to **the origins of the {{ill|Daijōsai|ja|大嘗祭}}'s {{ill|Yuki-den|ja|悠紀殿}}** (which has **cut-inward 千木 [Chigi] like {{ill|Kōtai Jingu|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}**) and **Shuki-den (主基殿)** (which has **cut-outward Chigi like {{ill|Toyoukedai Jingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}}**){{r|Heisei Daijōsai/p182}}{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
== Kasanui Island in Osaka == At **{{ill|Fukae Inari Shrine|ja|深江稲荷神社}}** ({{ill|Higashinari-ku, Osaka|ja|大阪市 東成区}} **Fukaeminami**), there is a tradition that **Fukae was once the residence of the {{ill|Kasanui clan|ja|笠縫氏}}**, who migrated from **Kasanui-mura in Yamato**.
The **Man'yōshū poet, {{ill|Takechi no Kurohito|ja|高市黒人}}**, wrote: > *"Shihatsu-yama uchi-koe mireba Kasanui no shima kogi-kakuru tana nashi kobune"* > *("Crossing over Mount Shihatsu, I see Kasanui Island, where small boats without roofs disappear.")*
In ancient times, **Kasanui Island** was believed to have been an island in an inlet **stretching from present-day Fukae to {{ill|Higashiōsaka, Osaka|ja|東大阪市}} Ashiya**. The **Kasanui clan likely lived on the island** to secure **materials for making straw hats (笠, kasa)**. This suggests that **the original Kasanui-mura in Yamato might also have been a low-lying wetland or an island-like terrain**{{who|date=June 2024}}.
Even today, **the straw hats used in the Daijōsai are made in Fukae and presented to the Imperial Household**. Additionally, **Fukae has strong historical ties to the casting of the Imperial Divine Mirror (御神鏡)**, suggesting a connection with **{{ill|Kagamitsukuri Shrine|ja|鏡作神社}}** in **Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, Yao**{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.

* '''{{ill|Kasayama Kōjinja|ja|笠山荒神社}}''' (Sakurai, '''Kasa''')
* '''Hime-Ōji Shrine''' (a subsidiary shrine of Tashinja)
* '''{{ill|Shiki Mikenuza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社}}''' (Sakurai, Kanaya)
* '''{{ill|Obu Tenjinja|ja|小夫天神社}}''' (Sakurai, Obu)
* '''{{ill|Anashizahyozu Shrine|ja|穴師坐兵主神社}}''' (Sakurai, Anashi)
* '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社}}''' ({{ill|Takaichi District, Nara|ja|高市郡}}, '''Asuka Village, Asuka''')
* '''Haseyamaguchi Shrine''' (Sakurai, Hase-Tejikarao){{citation needed|date=June 2024}}
Some shrines, such as '''Hibara Shrine, Kasayama Kōjinja, and Obu Tenjinja''', are '''located deep in the mountains'''. Additionally, the name '''Jinraku (秦楽)''', associated with Kasanui Shrine, is also found '''in the plains below the front part of the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun|ja|築山古墳}} in {{ill|Yamatotakada, Nara|ja|大和高田市}}''', where it was historically written as '''"神楽" (Jinraku/Kagura)'''. This could suggest some connection{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
From '''Tashinja''', looking in the direction of '''Yatsugikamino Mikoto Shrine (矢継ぎ神命神社)''' (dedicated to '''Tenmyō Toyotama no Mikoto'''), one faces the '''Jinraku and Tsukiyama Kofun direction'''. However, due to '''Yatsugi no Mori (矢継ぎの森)''', a sacred grove preserved by locals, '''the view is blocked'''.
It is also speculated that '''in ancient times, the view from Tashinja to Hime-Ōji Shrine may have included the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓}}'''. Originally, '''Hime-Ōji Shrine was located to the north (upper seat) of Tashinja''', but was possibly '''relocated southward (lower seat)'''{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
If '''Tashinja is indeed the site of Kasanui-mura''', then these kofun formations might correspond to '''the origins of the {{ill|Daijōsai|ja|大嘗祭}}'s {{ill|Yuki-den|ja|悠紀殿}}''' (which has '''cut-inward 千木 [Chigi] like {{ill|Kōtai Jingu|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}''') and '''Shuki-den (主基殿)''' (which has '''cut-outward Chigi like {{ill|Toyoukedai Jingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}}'''){{r|Heisei Daijōsai/p182}}{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
== Kasanui Island in Osaka ==
At '''{{ill|Fukae Inari Shrine|ja|深江稲荷神社}}''' ({{ill|Higashinari-ku, Osaka|ja|大阪市 東成区}} '''Fukaeminami'''), there is a tradition that '''Fukae was once the residence of the {{ill|Kasanui clan|ja|笠縫氏}}''', who migrated from '''Kasanui-mura in Yamato'''.
The '''Man'yōshū poet, {{ill|Takechi no Kurohito|ja|高市黒人}}''', wrote:
> *"Shihatsu-yama uchi-koe mireba Kasanui no shima kogi-kakuru tana nashi kobune"*
> *("Crossing over Mount Shihatsu, I see Kasanui Island, where small boats without roofs disappear.")*
In ancient times, '''Kasanui Island''' was believed to have been an island in an inlet '''stretching from present-day Fukae to {{ill|Higashiōsaka, Osaka|ja|東大阪市}} Ashiya'''. The '''Kasanui clan likely lived on the island''' to secure '''materials for making straw hats (笠, kasa)'''. This suggests that '''the original Kasanui-mura in Yamato might also have been a low-lying wetland or an island-like terrain'''{{who|date=June 2024}}.
Even today, '''the straw hats used in the Daijōsai are made in Fukae and presented to the Imperial Household'''. Additionally, '''Fukae has strong historical ties to the casting of the Imperial Divine Mirror (御神鏡)''', suggesting a connection with '''{{ill|Kagamitsukuri Shrine|ja|鏡作神社}}''' in '''Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, Yao'''{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.

* {{ill|Motoise|ja|元伊勢}}
* {{ill|Kasanui Station|ja|笠縫駅}}

'''{{ill|Kasanui-mura|ja|笠縫邑}}''' (かさぬいむら, かさぬいのむら) is the place where '''{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}''' was '''transferred from the imperial palace in the 6th year of Emperor {{ill|Suijin|ja|崇神天皇}}'s reign''' and entrusted to '''{{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime}}''' for enshrinement. At the same time, '''{{ill|Yamato Ōkunidama no Kami|ja|倭大国魂神}}''', who was also removed from the imperial palace, was entrusted to '''{{ill|Nunakiirihime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入媛命}}''' and later enshrined at '''{{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}}'''{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
== Overview ==
There is a theory that '''Kasanui-mura is related to the origins of the {{ill|Daijōsai|ja|大嘗祭}} and {{ill|Toyoakari no Sechie|ja|豊明節会}}'''{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}. According to the '''25th year of Emperor {{ill|Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}'s reign''', '''Princess {{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}''' enshrined '''Amaterasu''' at '''{{ill|Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto|ja|磯城の厳橿の本}}'''. The '''Ihokashi tree''' was considered a '''sacred tree for divine spirits''', and during Emperor Suijin's reign, the '''divine pillar of {{ill|Isokashiro Shrine|ja|磯堅城の神籬}}''' was erected ('''Suijin Chronicles, 6th year'''){{who|date=June 2024}}. In '''{{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}}''', it is referred to as '''"Ishiki no Himorogi"''', suggesting that '''"Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto" and "Isokashiro no Himorogi"''' might be the same place{{who|date=June 2024}}.
=== Possible Locations
There are various theories about the '''location of Kasanui-mura''', including:
* '''{{ill|Hibara Shrine (Nara)|ja|檜原神社}}''' ({{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}} '''Miwa''')
* '''{{ill|Tashinja|ja|多神社}}''' ({{ill|Shiki District, Nara|ja|磯城郡}} '''Tawaramoto Town, Ta''')
* '''{{ill|Kasanui Shrine|ja|笠縫神社}}''' (Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, '''Hazushō''', located in the '''southeastern corner of {{ill|Jinraku-ji|ja|秦楽寺}}''')
* '''{{ill|Kasayama Kōjinja|ja|笠山荒神社}}''' (Sakurai, '''Kasa''')
* '''Hime-Ōji Shrine''' (a subsidiary shrine of Tashinja)
* '''{{ill|Shiki Mikenuza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社}}''' (Sakurai, Kanaya)
* '''{{ill|Obu Tenjinja|ja|小夫天神社}}''' (Sakurai, Obu)
* '''{{ill|Anashizahyozu Shrine|ja|穴師坐兵主神社}}''' (Sakurai, Anashi)
* '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社}}''' ({{ill|Takaichi District, Nara|ja|高市郡}}, '''Asuka Village, Asuka''')
* '''Haseyamaguchi Shrine''' (Sakurai, Hase-Tejikarao){{citation needed|date=June 2024}}
Some shrines, such as '''Hibara Shrine, Kasayama Kōjinja, and Obu Tenjinja''', are '''located deep in the mountains'''. Additionally, the name '''Jinraku (秦楽)''', associated with Kasanui Shrine, is also found '''in the plains below the front part of the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun|ja|築山古墳}} in {{ill|Yamatotakada, Nara|ja|大和高田市}}''', where it was historically written as '''"神楽" (Jinraku/Kagura)'''. This could suggest some connection{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
From '''Tashinja''', looking in the direction of '''Yatsugikamino Mikoto Shrine (矢継ぎ神命神社)''' (dedicated to '''Tenmyō Toyotama no Mikoto'''), one faces the '''Jinraku and Tsukiyama Kofun direction'''. However, due to '''Yatsugi no Mori (矢継ぎの森)''', a sacred grove preserved by locals, '''the view is blocked'''.
It is also speculated that '''in ancient times, the view from Tashinja to Hime-Ōji Shrine may have included the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓}}'''. Originally, '''Hime-Ōji Shrine was located to the north (upper seat) of Tashinja''', but was possibly '''relocated southward (lower seat)'''{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
If '''Tashinja is indeed the site of Kasanui-mura''', then these kofun formations might correspond to '''the origins of the {{ill|Daijōsai|ja|大嘗祭}}'s {{ill|Yuki-den|ja|悠紀殿}}''' (which has '''cut-inward 千木 [Chigi] like {{ill|Kōtai Jingu|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}''') and '''Shuki-den (主基殿)''' (which has '''cut-outward Chigi like {{ill|Toyoukedai Jingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}}'''){{r|Heisei Daijōsai/p182}}{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
== Kasanui Island in Osaka ==
At '''{{ill|Fukae Inari Shrine|ja|深江稲荷神社}}''' ({{ill|Higashinari-ku, Osaka|ja|大阪市 東成区}} '''Fukaeminami'''), there is a tradition that '''Fukae was once the residence of the {{ill|Kasanui clan|ja|笠縫氏}}''', who migrated from '''Kasanui-mura in Yamato'''.
The '''Man'yōshū poet, {{ill|Takechi no Kurohito|ja|高市黒人}}''', wrote:

Even today, '''the straw hats used in the Daijōsai are made in Fukae and presented to the Imperial Household'''. Additionally, '''Fukae has strong historical ties to the casting of the Imperial Divine Mirror (御神鏡)''', suggesting a connection with '''{{ill|Kagamitsukuri Shrine|ja|鏡作神社}}''' in '''Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, Yao'''{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.

From '''Tashinja''', looking in the direction of '''Yatsugikamino Mikoto Shrine (矢継ぎ神命神社)''' (dedicated to '''Tenmyō Toyotama no Mikoto'''), one faces the '''Jinraku and Tsukiyama Kofun direction'''. However, due to '''Yatsugi no Mori (矢継ぎの森)''', a sacred grove preserved by locals, '''the view is blocked'''.
It is also speculated that '''in ancient times, the view from Tashinja to Hime-Ōji Shrine may have included the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓}}'''. Originally, '''Hime-Ōji Shrine was located to the north (upper seat) of Tashinja''', but was possibly '''relocated southward (lower seat)'''{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
If '''Tashinja is indeed the site of Kasanui-mura''', then these kofun formations might correspond to '''the origins of the {{ill|Daijōsai|ja|大嘗祭}}'s {{ill|Yuki-den|ja|悠紀殿}}''' (which has '''cut-inward 千木 [Chigi] like {{ill|Kōtai Jingu|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}''') and '''Shuki-den (主基殿)''' (which has '''cut-outward Chigi like {{ill|Toyoukedai Jingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}}'''){{r|Heisei Daijōsai/p182}}{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
== Kasanui Island in Osaka ==
At '''{{ill|Fukae Inari Shrine|ja|深江稲荷神社}}''' ({{ill|Higashinari-ku, Osaka|ja|大阪市 東成区}} '''Fukaeminami'''), there is a tradition that '''Fukae was once the residence of the {{ill|Kasanui clan|ja|笠縫氏}}''', who migrated from '''Kasanui-mura in Yamato'''.
The '''Man'yōshū poet, {{ill|Takechi no Kurohito|ja|高市黒人}}''', wrote:
> *"Shihatsu-yama uchi-koe mireba Kasanui no shima kogi-kakuru tana nashi kobune"*
> *("Crossing over Mount Shihatsu, I see Kasanui Island, where small boats without roofs disappear.")*
In ancient times, '''Kasanui Island''' was believed to have been an island in an inlet '''stretching from present-day Fukae to {{ill|Higashiōsaka, Osaka|ja|東大阪市}} Ashiya'''. The '''Kasanui clan likely lived on the island''' to secure '''materials for making straw hats (笠, kasa)'''. This suggests that '''the original Kasanui-mura in Yamato might also have been a low-lying wetland or an island-like terrain'''{{who|date=June 2024}}.
Even today, '''the straw hats used in the Daijōsai are made in Fukae and presented to the Imperial Household'''. Additionally, '''Fukae has strong historical ties to the casting of the Imperial Divine Mirror (御神鏡)''', suggesting a connection with '''{{ill|Kagamitsukuri Shrine|ja|鏡作神社}}''' in '''Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, Yao'''{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.

'''{{ill|Kasanui-mura|ja|笠縫邑}}''' (かさぬいむら, かさぬいのむら) is the place where '''{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}''' was '''transferred from the imperial palace in the 6th year of Emperor {{ill|Suijin|ja|崇神天皇}}'s reign''' and entrusted to '''{{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime}}''' for enshrinement. At the same time, '''{{ill|Yamato Ōkunidama no Kami|ja|倭大国魂神}}''', who was also removed from the imperial palace, was entrusted to '''{{ill|Nunakiirihime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入媛命}}''' and later enshrined at '''{{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}}'''{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
== Overview ==
There is a theory that '''Kasanui-mura is related to the origins of the {{ill|Daijōsai|ja|大嘗祭}} and {{ill|Toyoakari no Sechie|ja|豊明節会}}'''{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}. According to the '''25th year of Emperor {{ill|Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}'s reign''', '''Princess {{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}''' enshrined '''Amaterasu''' at '''{{ill|Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto|ja|磯城の厳橿の本}}'''. The '''Ihokashi tree''' was considered a '''sacred tree for divine spirits''', and during Emperor Suijin's reign, the '''divine pillar of {{ill|Isokashiro Shrine|ja|磯堅城の神籬}}''' was erected ('''Suijin Chronicles, 6th year'''){{who|date=June 2024}}. In '''{{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}}''', it is referred to as '''"Ishiki no Himorogi"''', suggesting that '''"Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto" and "Isokashiro no Himorogi"''' might be the same place{{who|date=June 2024}}.
=== Possible Locations
There are various theories about the '''location of Kasanui-mura''', including:
* '''{{ill|Hibara Shrine (Nara)|ja|檜原神社}}''' ({{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}} '''Miwa''')
* '''{{ill|Tashinja|ja|多神社}}''' ({{ill|Shiki District, Nara|ja|磯城郡}} '''Tawaramoto Town, Ta''')
* '''{{ill|Kasanui Shrine|ja|笠縫神社}}''' (Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, '''Hazushō''', located in the '''southeastern corner of {{ill|Jinraku-ji|ja|秦楽寺}}''')
* '''{{ill|Kasayama Kōjinja|ja|笠山荒神社}}''' (Sakurai, '''Kasa''')
* '''Hime-Ōji Shrine''' (a subsidiary shrine of Tashinja)
* '''{{ill|Shiki Mikenuza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社}}''' (Sakurai, Kanaya)
* '''{{ill|Obu Tenjinja|ja|小夫天神社}}''' (Sakurai, Obu)
* '''{{ill|Anashizahyozu Shrine|ja|穴師坐兵主神社}}''' (Sakurai, Anashi)
* '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社}}''' ({{ill|Takaichi District, Nara|ja|高市郡}}, '''Asuka Village, Asuka''')
* '''Haseyamaguchi Shrine''' (Sakurai, Hase-Tejikarao){{citation needed|date=June 2024}}
Some shrines, such as '''Hibara Shrine, Kasayama Kōjinja, and Obu Tenjinja''', are '''located deep in the mountains'''. Additionally, the name '''Jinraku (秦楽)''', associated with Kasanui Shrine, is also found '''in the plains below the front part of the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun|ja|築山古墳}} in {{ill|Yamatotakada, Nara|ja|大和高田市}}''', where it was historically written as '''"神楽" (Jinraku/Kagura)'''. This could suggest some connection{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
From '''Tashinja''', looking in the direction of '''Yatsugikamino Mikoto Shrine (矢継ぎ神命神社)''' (dedicated to '''Tenmyō Toyotama no Mikoto'''), one faces the '''Jinraku and Tsukiyama Kofun direction'''. However, due to '''Yatsugi no Mori (矢継ぎの森)''', a sacred grove preserved by locals, '''the view is blocked'''.
It is also speculated that '''in ancient times, the view from Tashinja to Hime-Ōji Shrine may have included the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓}}'''. Originally, '''Hime-Ōji Shrine was located to the north (upper seat) of Tashinja''', but was possibly '''relocated southward (lower seat)'''{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
If '''Tashinja is indeed the site of Kasanui-mura''', then these kofun formations might correspond to '''the origins of the {{ill|Daijōsai|ja|大嘗祭}}'s {{ill|Yuki-den|ja|悠紀殿}}''' (which has '''cut-inward 千木 [Chigi] like {{ill|Kōtai Jingu|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}''') and '''Shuki-den (主基殿)''' (which has '''cut-outward Chigi like {{ill|Toyoukedai Jingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}}'''){{r|Heisei Daijōsai/p182}}{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
== Kasanui Island in Osaka ==
At '''{{ill|Fukae Inari Shrine|ja|深江稲荷神社}}''' ({{ill|Higashinari-ku, Osaka|ja|大阪市 東成区}} '''Fukaeminami'''), there is a tradition that '''Fukae was once the residence of the {{ill|Kasanui clan|ja|笠縫氏}}''', who migrated from '''Kasanui-mura in Yamato'''.
The '''Man'yōshū poet, {{ill|Takechi no Kurohito|ja|高市黒人}}''', wrote:
> *"Shihatsu-yama uchi-koe mireba Kasanui no shima kogi-kakuru tana nashi kobune"*
> *("Crossing over Mount Shihatsu, I see Kasanui Island, where small boats without roofs disappear.")*

The '''Kasanui clan likely lived on the island''' to secure '''materials for making straw hats (笠, kasa)'''. This suggests that '''the original Kasanui-mura in Yamato might also have been a low-lying wetland or an island-like terrain'''{{who|date=June 2024}}.
Even today, '''the straw hats used in the Daijōsai are made in Fukae and presented to the Imperial Household'''. Additionally, '''Fukae has strong historical ties to the casting of the Imperial Divine Mirror (御神鏡)''', suggesting a connection with '''{{ill|Kagamitsukuri Shrine|ja|鏡作神社}}''' in '''Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, Yao'''{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.

It is also speculated that '''in ancient times, the view from Tashinja to Hime-Ōji Shrine may have included the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓}}'''. Originally, '''Hime-Ōji Shrine was located to the north (upper seat) of Tashinja''', but was possibly '''relocated southward (lower seat)'''{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
If '''Tashinja is indeed the site of Kasanui-mura''', then these kofun formations might correspond to '''the origins of the {{ill|Daijōsai|ja|大嘗祭}}'s {{ill|Yuki-den|ja|悠紀殿}}''' (which has '''cut-inward 千木 [Chigi] like {{ill|Kōtai Jingu|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}''') and '''Shuki-den (主基殿)''' (which has '''cut-outward Chigi like {{ill|Toyoukedai Jingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}}'''){{r|Heisei Daijōsai/p182}}{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
== Kasanui Island in Osaka ==
At '''{{ill|Fukae Inari Shrine|ja|深江稲荷神社}}''' ({{ill|Higashinari-ku, Osaka|ja|大阪市 東成区}} '''Fukaeminami'''), there is a tradition that '''Fukae was once the residence of the {{ill|Kasanui clan|ja|笠縫氏}}''', who migrated from '''Kasanui-mura in Yamato'''.
The '''Man'yōshū poet, {{ill|Takechi no Kurohito|ja|高市黒人}}''', wrote:
> *"Shihatsu-yama uchi-koe mireba Kasanui no shima kogi-kakuru tana nashi kobune"*
> *("Crossing over Mount Shihatsu, I see Kasanui Island, where small boats without roofs disappear.")*
In ancient times, '''Kasanui Island''' was believed to have been an island in an inlet '''stretching from present-day Fukae to {{ill|Higashiōsaka, Osaka|ja|東大阪市}} Ashiya'''. The '''Kasanui clan likely lived on the island''' to secure '''materials for making straw hats (笠, kasa)'''. This suggests that '''the original Kasanui-mura in Yamato might also have been a low-lying wetland or an island-like terrain'''{{who|date=June 2024}}.
Even today, '''the straw hats used in the Daijōsai are made in Fukae and presented to the Imperial Household'''. Additionally, '''Fukae has strong historical ties to the casting of the Imperial Divine Mirror (御神鏡)''', suggesting a connection with '''{{ill|Kagamitsukuri Shrine|ja|鏡作神社}}''' in '''Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, Yao'''{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Motoise|ja|元伊勢}}
* {{ill|Kasanui Station|ja|笠縫駅}}

'''{{ill|Kasanui-mura|ja|笠縫邑}}''' (かさぬいむら, かさぬいのむら) is the place where '''{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}''' was '''transferred from the imperial palace in the 6th year of Emperor {{ill|Suijin|ja|崇神天皇}}'s reign''' and entrusted to '''{{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime}}''' for enshrinement. At the same time, '''{{ill|Yamato Ōkunidama no Kami|ja|倭大国魂神}}''', who was also removed from the imperial palace, was entrusted to '''{{ill|Nunakiirihime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入媛命}}''' and later enshrined at '''{{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}}'''{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
== Overview == There is a theory that '''Kasanui-mura is related to the origins of the {{ill|Daijōsai|ja|大嘗祭}} and {{ill|Toyoakari no Sechie|ja|豊明節会}}'''{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}. According to the '''25th year of Emperor {{ill|Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}'s reign''', '''Princess {{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}''' enshrined '''Amaterasu''' at '''{{ill|Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto|ja|磯城の厳橿の本}}'''. The '''Ihokashi tree''' was considered a '''sacred tree for divine spirits''', and during Emperor Suijin's reign, the '''divine pillar of {{ill|Isokashiro Shrine|ja|磯堅城の神籬}}''' was erected ('''Suijin Chronicles, 6th year'''){{who|date=June 2024}}. In '''{{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}}''', it is referred to as '''"Ishiki no Himorogi"''', suggesting that '''"Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto" and "Isokashiro no Himorogi"''' might be the same place{{who|date=June 2024}}.
=== Possible Locations=== There are various theories about the '''location of Kasanui-mura''', including: * '''{{ill|Hibara Shrine (Nara)|ja|檜原神社}}''' ({{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}} '''Miwa''') * '''{{ill|Tashinja|ja|多神社}}''' ({{ill|Shiki District, Nara|ja|磯城郡}} '''Tawaramoto Town, Ta''') * '''{{ill|Kasanui Shrine|ja|笠縫神社}}''' (Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, '''Hazushō''', located in the '''southeastern corner of {{ill|Jinraku-ji|ja|秦楽寺}}''') * '''{{ill|Kasayama Kōjinja|ja|笠山荒神社}}''' (Sakurai, '''Kasa''') * '''Hime-Ōji Shrine''' (a subsidiary shrine of Tashinja) * '''{{ill|Shiki Mikenuza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社}}''' (Sakurai, Kanaya) * '''{{ill|Obu Tenjinja|ja|小夫天神社}}''' (Sakurai, Obu) * '''{{ill|Anashizahyozu Shrine|ja|穴師坐兵主神社}}''' (Sakurai, Anashi) * '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社}}''' ({{ill|Takaichi District, Nara|ja|高市郡}}, '''Asuka Village, Asuka''') * '''Haseyamaguchi Shrine''' (Sakurai, Hase-Tejikarao){{citation needed|date=June 2024}}
Some shrines, such as '''Hibara Shrine, Kasayama Kōjinja, and Obu Tenjinja''', are '''located deep in the mountains'''. Additionally, the name '''Jinraku (秦楽)''', associated with Kasanui Shrine, is also found '''in the plains below the front part of the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun|ja|築山古墳}} in {{ill|Yamatotakada, Nara|ja|大和高田市}}''', where it was historically written as '''"神楽" (Jinraku/Kagura)'''. This could suggest some connection{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
From '''Tashinja''', looking in the direction of '''Yatsugikamino Mikoto Shrine (矢継ぎ神命神社)''' (dedicated to '''Tenmyō Toyotama no Mikoto'''), one faces the '''Jinraku and Tsukiyama Kofun direction'''. However, due to '''Yatsugi no Mori (矢継ぎの森)''', a sacred grove preserved by locals, '''the view is blocked'''.
It is also speculated that '''in ancient times, the view from Tashinja to Hime-Ōji Shrine may have included the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓}}'''. Originally, '''Hime-Ōji Shrine was located to the north (upper seat) of Tashinja''', but was possibly '''relocated southward (lower seat)'''{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
If '''Tashinja is indeed the site of Kasanui-mura''', then these kofun formations might correspond to '''the origins of the {{ill|Daijōsai|ja|大嘗祭}}'s {{ill|Yuki-den|ja|悠紀殿}}''' (which has '''cut-inward 千木 [Chigi] like {{ill|Kōtai Jingu|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}''') and '''Shuki-den (主基殿)''' (which has '''cut-outward Chigi like {{ill|Toyoukedai Jingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}}'''){{r|Heisei Daijōsai/p182}}{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
== Kasanui Island in Osaka == At '''{{ill|Fukae Inari Shrine|ja|深江稲荷神社}}''' ({{ill|Higashinari-ku, Osaka|ja|大阪市 東成区}} '''Fukaeminami'''), there is a tradition that '''Fukae was once the residence of the {{ill|Kasanui clan|ja|笠縫氏}}''', who migrated from '''Kasanui-mura in Yamato'''.
The '''Man'yōshū poet, {{ill|Takechi no Kurohito|ja|高市黒人}}''', wrote: > *"Shihatsu-yama uchi-koe mireba Kasanui no shima kogi-kakuru tana nashi kobune"* > *("Crossing over Mount Shihatsu, I see Kasanui Island, where small boats without roofs disappear.")*
In ancient times, '''Kasanui Island''' was believed to have been an island in an inlet '''stretching from present-day Fukae to {{ill|Higashiōsaka, Osaka|ja|東大阪市}} Ashiya'''. The '''Kasanui clan likely lived on the island''' to secure '''materials for making straw hats (笠, kasa)'''. This suggests that '''the original Kasanui-mura in Yamato might also have been a low-lying wetland or an island-like terrain'''{{who|date=June 2024}}.
Even today, '''the straw hats used in the Daijōsai are made in Fukae and presented to the Imperial Household'''. Additionally, '''Fukae has strong historical ties to the casting of the Imperial Divine Mirror (御神鏡)''', suggesting a connection with '''{{ill|Kagamitsukuri Shrine|ja|鏡作神社}}''' in '''Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, Yao'''{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}.
== See Also == * {{ill|Motoise|ja|元伊勢}} * {{ill|Kasanui Station|ja|笠縫駅}}
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="Heisei Daijōsai/p182">{{Citation|和書 | last = Kamata | first = Junichi | author-link = 鎌田純一 | title = Heisei Daijōsai Yōwa: Sokui-rei & Daijōsai | publisher = {{ill|Kinsei-sha|ja|錦正社}} | date = July 2003 | page = 182 | id = {{NDL|20452668}} | isbn = 4-7646-0262-8 }}</ref> }}

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2024年6月}} {{独自研究|date=2024年6月}} {{言葉を濁さない|date=2024年6月}} '''笠縫邑'''（かさぬいむら、かさぬいのむら）とは、[[崇神天皇]]6年に、宮中に奉祀していた[[天照大神]]を移し、[[豊鍬入姫命]]に託して祀らせた場所。同時に宮中を出された[[倭大国魂神]]は[[渟名城入媛命]]に託して、後に[[大和神社]]に祀ったとされる{{要出典|date=2024年6月}}。
== 概要 == 笠縫邑は[[大嘗祭]]、[[豊明節会]]の起源に関係する土地との説もある{{要出典|date=2024年6月}}。[[垂仁天皇]]紀25年3月丙申条に、[[倭姫命]]は天照大神を[[磯城の厳橿の本]]に祀ったとあり、厳橿は神霊の神木で、崇神朝に[[磯堅城の神籬]]を立てた。（崇神紀６年条）。[[古語拾遺]]には「磯城の神籬」とあり、「磯城の厳橿の本」と「磯堅城の神籬」は同所とも考えられる{{誰2|date=2024年6月}}。
比定地については、[[大神神社#伊勢神宮との関係|檜原神社]]（[[桜井市]] [[三輪]]）、[[多神社]]（[[磯城郡]] [[田原本町]]多）、[[笠縫神社]]（磯城郡田原本町秦荘、[[秦楽寺]]境内南東隅）、[[笠山荒神社]]（桜井市笠）、[[多神社]]摂社の姫皇子神社、[[志貴御県坐神社]]（桜井市金屋）、[[小夫天神社]]（桜井市小夫）、[[穴師坐兵主神社]]（桜井市穴師）、[[飛鳥坐神社]]（[[高市郡]] [[明日香村]]飛鳥）、長谷山口坐神社（桜井市初瀬手力雄）、等々{{要出典|date=2024年6月}}。
檜原神社、笠山荒神社、小夫天神社（笠山荒神社よりもさらに東の山間部）、[[穴師坐兵主神社]]等はまさに山の中である。 また、笠縫神社に関係する{{読み仮名|秦楽|じんらく}}という名が、[[築山古墳 (大和高田市)|築山古墳]]（[[大和高田市]]築山字城山）の前方部眼下に広がる平地にも{{読み仮名|神楽|じんらく}}という名で存在する。この神楽は、古くは秦楽と書いたとの説もある。何らかの関係を示唆しているかも知れない{{要出典|date=2024年6月}}。
多神社から、摂社の屋就神命神社（やつぎかみのみことじんじゃ、やつき？しんめいじんじゃ：祭神 天明豊玉命）の方角を見ると、神楽、築山古墳の方角となる。実際には、理由はよく判らないが地元住民によって大切に残された「矢継ぎの森（やつぎのもり：屋就神命神社の杜でもある）」に隠されて見えない。
また、もしかすると、昔は、多神社から姫皇子神社の方向を見ると、[[箸墓]]が見えたのかもしれない。元々、[[姫皇子神社]]は、多神社の奥（北：上座）に位置していたので、前（南：下座）に移転させられている可能性がある{{要出典|date=2024年6月}}。
[[多神社]]を笠縫邑の位置と比定するなら、これら[[古墳]]が、鏡に映した様に南北及び東西が逆になるが、[[大嘗祭]]の[[悠紀殿]]（[[千木]]は[[伊勢神宮]][[皇大神宮]]の様に内削ぎ）{{r|平成大禮要話/p182}}、主基殿（千木は伊勢神宮[[豊受大神宮]]の様に外削ぎ）{{r|平成大禮要話/p182}}の起源とも関係している可能性がある{{要出典|date=2024年6月}}。
== 大阪の笠縫島 == [[深江稲荷神社]]（[[大阪市]] [[東成区]]深江南）には、付近の深江は[[笠縫氏]]の居住地で、[[大和]]の笠縫邑から移住してきた、との伝承がある。[[万葉]][[歌人]][[高市黒人]]（たけちのくろと）が「四極山（しはつやま） うち越え見れば笠縫の島 漕ぎ隠る 棚無し小舟」と詠んだ様に、古代には、笠縫島といわれた。
笠縫島は、現在の深江から[[東大阪市]]足代にかけて、入江に浮かんだ島であった。笠の材料の確保のため、笠縫氏は島に住んだと思われるが、もともと大和の笠縫邑も、同じような低湿地か、島状の土地だったのではないかとも推測される{{誰2|date=2024年6月}}。
現在も大嘗祭に使用する笠は、この深江から[[皇室]]へ献上されている。また、深江は、皇祖の御神鏡に関係する[[鋳物]]師とも関係が深い土地とのことである。[[鏡作神社]]（磯城郡田原本町八尾 等周辺）とも関係の可能性がある。
== 関連項目 == * [[元伊勢]] * [[笠縫駅]]
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="平成大禮要話/p182">{{Citation|和書 | last = 鎌田 | first = 純一 | author-link = 鎌田純一 | title = 平成大禮要話 : 即位禮・大嘗祭 | publisher = [[錦正社]] | date = 2003-7 | page = 182 | id = {{全国書誌番号|20452668}} | isbn = 4-7646-0262-8 }}</ref> }}
{{DEFAULTSORT:かさぬいむら}} [[Category:神道]] [[Category:元伊勢]] [[Category:奈良県の歴史]] [[Category:大阪府指定史跡]]

{{出典の明記|date=2024年6月}} {{独自研究|date=2024年6月}} {{言葉を濁さない|date=2024年6月}} '''笠縫邑'''（かさぬいむら、かさぬいのむら）とは、[[崇神天皇]]6年に、宮中に奉祀していた[[天照大神]]を移し、[[豊鍬入姫命]]に託して祀らせた場所。同時に宮中を出された[[倭大国魂神]]は[[渟名城入媛命]]に託して、後に[[大和神社]]に祀ったとされる{{要出典|date=2024年6月}}。

比定地については、[[大神神社#伊勢神宮との関係|檜原神社]]（[[桜井市]] [[三輪]]）、[[多神社]]（[[磯城郡]] [[田原本町]]多）、[[笠縫神社]]（磯城郡田原本町秦荘、[[秦楽寺]]境内南東隅）、[[笠山荒神社]]（桜井市笠）、[[多神社]]摂社の姫皇子神社、[[志貴御県坐神社]]（桜井市金屋）、[[小夫天神社]]（桜井市小夫）、[[穴師坐兵主神社]]（桜井市穴師）、[[飛鳥坐神社]]（[[高市郡]] [[明日香村]]飛鳥）、長谷山口坐神社（桜井市初瀬手力雄）、等々{{要出典|date=2024年6月}}。
檜原神社、笠山荒神社、小夫天神社（笠山荒神社よりもさらに東の山間部）、[[穴師坐兵主神社]]等はまさに山の中である。 また、笠縫神社に関係する{{読み仮名|秦楽|じんらく}}という名が、[[築山古墳 (大和高田市)|築山古墳]]（[[大和高田市]]築山字城山）の前方部眼下に広がる平地にも{{読み仮名|神楽|じんらく}}という名で存在する。この神楽は、古くは秦楽と書いたとの説もある。何らかの関係を示唆しているかも知れない{{要出典|date=2024年6月}}。
