また、もしかすると、昔は、多神社から姫皇子神社の方向を見ると、[[箸墓]]が見えたのかもしれない。元々、[[姫皇子神社]]は、多神社の奥（北：上座）に位置していたので、前（南：下座）に移転させられている可能性がある{{要出典|date=2024年6月}}。
[[多神社]]を笠縫邑の位置と比定するなら、これら[[古墳]]が、鏡に映した様に南北及び東西が逆になるが、[[大嘗祭]]の[[悠紀殿]]（[[千木]]は[[伊勢神宮]][[皇大神宮]]の様に内削ぎ）{{r|平成大禮要話/p182}}、主基殿（千木は伊勢神宮[[豊受大神宮]]の様に外削ぎ）{{r|平成大禮要話/p182}}の起源とも関係している可能性がある{{要出典|date=2024年6月}}。
== 大阪の笠縫島 == [[深江稲荷神社]]（[[大阪市]] [[東成区]]深江南）には、付近の深江は[[笠縫氏]]の居住地で、[[大和]]の笠縫邑から移住してきた、との伝承がある。[[万葉]][[歌人]][[高市黒人]]（たけちのくろと）が「四極山（しはつやま） うち越え見れば笠縫の島 漕ぎ隠る 棚無し小舟」と詠んだ様に、古代には、笠縫島といわれた。
笠縫島は、現在の深江から[[東大阪市]]足代にかけて、入江に浮かんだ島であった。笠の材料の確保のため、笠縫氏は島に住んだと思われるが、もともと大和の笠縫邑も、同じような低湿地か、島状の土地だったのではないかとも推測される{{誰2|date=2024年6月}}。
現在も大嘗祭に使用する笠は、この深江から[[皇室]]へ献上されている。また、深江は、皇祖の御神鏡に関係する[[鋳物]]師とも関係が深い土地とのことである。[[鏡作神社]]（磯城郡田原本町八尾 等周辺）とも関係の可能性がある。
== 関連項目 == * [[元伊勢]] * [[笠縫駅]]
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="平成大禮要話/p182">{{Citation|和書 | last = 鎌田 | first = 純一 | author-link = 鎌田純一 | title = 平成大禮要話 : 即位禮・大嘗祭 | publisher = [[錦正社]] | date = 2003-7 | page = 182 | id = {{全国書誌番号|20452668}} | isbn = 4-7646-0262-8 }}</ref> }}
{{DEFAULTSORT:かさぬいむら}} [[Category:神道]] [[Category:元伊勢]] [[Category:奈良県の歴史]] [[Category:大阪府指定史跡]]

'''{{ill|Kasanui-mura|ja|笠縫邑|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (かさぬいむら, かさぬいのむら) is the place where '''{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' was '''transferred from the imperial palace in the 6th year of Emperor {{ill|Suijin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s reign''' and entrusted to '''{{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' for enshrinement. At the same time, '''{{ill|Yamato Ōkunidama no Kami|ja|倭大国魂神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''', who was also removed from the imperial palace, was entrusted to '''{{ill|Nunakiirihime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入媛命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' and later enshrined at '''{{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'''.
== Overview == There is a theory that '''Kasanui-mura is related to the origins of the {{ill|Daijōsai|ja|大嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Toyoakari no Sechie|ja|豊明節会|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'''. According to the '''25th year of Emperor {{ill|Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s reign''', '''Princess {{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' enshrined '''Amaterasu''' at '''{{ill|Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto|ja|磯城の厳橿の本|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'''. The '''Ihokashi tree''' was considered a '''sacred tree for divine spirits''', and during Emperor Suijin's reign, the '''divine pillar of {{ill|Isokashiro Shrine|ja|磯堅城の神籬|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' was erected ('''Suijin Chronicles, 6th year'''){{who|date=June 2024}}. In '''{{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''', it is referred to as '''"Ishiki no Himorogi"''', suggesting that '''"Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto" and "Isokashiro no Himorogi"''' might be the same place{{who|date=June 2024}}.
=== Possible Locations=== There are various theories about the '''location of Kasanui-mura''', including: * '''{{ill|Hibara Shrine (Nara)|ja|檜原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' ({{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} '''Miwa''') * '''{{ill|Tashinja|ja|多神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' ({{ill|Shiki District, Nara|ja|磯城郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} '''Tawaramoto Town, Ta''') * '''{{ill|Kasanui Shrine|ja|笠縫神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, '''Hazushō''', located in the '''southeastern corner of {{ill|Jinraku-ji|ja|秦楽寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''') * '''{{ill|Kasayama Kōjinja|ja|笠山荒神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (Sakurai, '''Kasa''') * '''Hime-Ōji Shrine''' (a subsidiary shrine of Tashinja) * '''{{ill|Shiki Mikenuza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (Sakurai, Kanaya) * '''{{ill|Obu Tenjinja|ja|小夫天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (Sakurai, Obu) * '''{{ill|Anashizahyozu Shrine|ja|穴師坐兵主神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (Sakurai, Anashi) * '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' ({{ill|Takaichi District, Nara|ja|高市郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, '''Asuka Village, Asuka''') * '''Haseyamaguchi Shrine''' (Sakurai, Hase-Tejikarao)
Some shrines, such as '''Hibara Shrine, Kasayama Kōjinja, and Obu Tenjinja''', are '''located deep in the mountains'''. Additionally, the name '''Jinraku (秦楽)''', associated with Kasanui Shrine, is also found '''in the plains below the front part of the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun|ja|築山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Yamatotakada, Nara|ja|大和高田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''', where it was historically written as '''"神楽" (Jinraku/Kagura)'''. This could suggest some connection.
From '''Tashinja''', looking in the direction of '''Yatsugikamino Mikoto Shrine (矢継ぎ神命神社)''' (dedicated to '''Tenmyō Toyotama no Mikoto'''), one faces the '''Jinraku and Tsukiyama Kofun direction'''. However, due to '''Yatsugi no Mori (矢継ぎの森)''', a sacred grove preserved by locals, '''the view is blocked'''.
It is also speculated that '''in ancient times, the view from Tashinja to Hime-Ōji Shrine may have included the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'''. Originally, '''Hime-Ōji Shrine was located to the north (upper seat) of Tashinja''', but was possibly '''relocated southward (lower seat)'''.
If '''Tashinja is indeed the site of Kasanui-mura''', then these kofun formations might correspond to '''the origins of the {{ill|Daijōsai|ja|大嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s {{ill|Yuki-den|ja|悠紀殿|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (which has '''cut-inward 千木 [Chigi] like {{ill|Kōtai Jingu|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''') and '''Shuki-den (主基殿)''' (which has '''cut-outward Chigi like {{ill|Toyoukedai Jingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'''){{r|Heisei Daijōsai/p182}}.
== Kasanui Island in Osaka == At '''{{ill|Fukae Inari Shrine|ja|深江稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' ({{ill|Higashinari-ku, Osaka|ja|大阪市 東成区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} '''Fukaeminami'''), there is a tradition that '''Fukae was once the residence of the {{ill|Kasanui clan|ja|笠縫氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''', who migrated from '''Kasanui-mura in Yamato'''.
The '''Man'yōshū poet, {{ill|Takechi no Kurohito|ja|高市黒人|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''', wrote: > *"Shihatsu-yama uchi-koe mireba Kasanui no shima kogi-kakuru tana nashi kobune"* > *("Crossing over Mount Shihatsu, I see Kasanui Island, where small boats without roofs disappear.")*
In ancient times, '''Kasanui Island''' was believed to have been an island in an inlet '''stretching from present-day Fukae to {{ill|Higashiōsaka, Osaka|ja|東大阪市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ashiya'''. The '''Kasanui clan likely lived on the island''' to secure '''materials for making straw hats (笠, kasa)'''. This suggests that '''the original Kasanui-mura in Yamato might also have been a low-lying wetland or an island-like terrain'''{{who|date=June 2024}}.
Even today, '''the straw hats used in the Daijōsai are made in Fukae and presented to the Imperial Household'''. Additionally, '''Fukae has strong historical ties to the casting of the Imperial Divine Mirror (御神鏡)''', suggesting a connection with '''{{ill|Kagamitsukuri Shrine|ja|鏡作神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' in '''Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, Yao'''.
== See Also == * {{ill|Motoise|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kasanui Station|ja|笠縫駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="Heisei Daijōsai/p182">{{Citation|和書 | last = Kamata | first = Junichi | author-link = 鎌田純一 | title = Heisei Daijōsai Yōwa: Sokui-rei & Daijōsai | publisher = {{ill|Kinsei-sha|ja|錦正社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | date = July 2003 | page = 182 | id = {{NDL|20452668}} | isbn = 4-7646-0262-8 }}</ref> }}
{{translated page|ja|笠縫邑|version=101402403|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:笠縫邑]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }}
[[sn:Kasanuimura]] [[en:Kasanuimura]] [[ja:笠縫邑]]

'''{{ill|Kasanui-mura|ja|笠縫邑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasanui-mura|qq=}}''' (かさぬいむら, かさぬいのむら) is the place where '''{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}''' was '''transferred from the imperial palace in the 6th year of Emperor {{ill|Suijin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin|qq=}}'s reign''' and entrusted to '''{{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}''' for enshrinement. At the same time, '''{{ill|Yamato Ōkunidama no Kami|ja|倭大国魂神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Ōkunidama no Kami|qq=}}''', who was also removed from the imperial palace, was entrusted to '''{{ill|Nunakiirihime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入媛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunakiirihime no Mikoto|qq=}}''' and later enshrined at '''{{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Shrine|qq=}}'''.
== Overview == There is a theory that '''Kasanui-mura is related to the origins of the {{ill|Daijōsai|ja|大嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōsai|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyoakari no Sechie|ja|豊明節会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoakari no Sechie|qq=}}'''. According to the '''25th year of Emperor {{ill|Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suinin|qq=}}'s reign''', '''Princess {{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=}}''' enshrined '''Amaterasu''' at '''{{ill|Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto|ja|磯城の厳橿の本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto|qq=}}'''. The '''Ihokashi tree''' was considered a '''sacred tree for divine spirits''', and during Emperor Suijin's reign, the '''divine pillar of {{ill|Isokashiro Shrine|ja|磯堅城の神籬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isokashiro Shrine|qq=}}''' was erected ('''Suijin Chronicles, 6th year'''){{who|date=June 2024}}. In '''{{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogo Shūi|qq=}}''', it is referred to as '''"Ishiki no Himorogi"''', suggesting that '''"Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto" and "Isokashiro no Himorogi"''' might be the same place{{who|date=June 2024}}.
=== Possible Locations=== There are various theories about the '''location of Kasanui-mura''', including: * '''{{ill|Hibara Shrine (Nara)|ja|檜原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibara Shrine (Nara)|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai, Nara|qq=}} '''Miwa''') * '''{{ill|Tashinja|ja|多神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tashinja|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Shiki District, Nara|ja|磯城郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki District, Nara|qq=}} '''Tawaramoto Town, Ta''') * '''{{ill|Kasanui Shrine|ja|笠縫神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasanui Shrine|qq=}}''' (Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, '''Hazushō''', located in the '''southeastern corner of {{ill|Jinraku-ji|ja|秦楽寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinraku-ji|qq=}}''') * '''{{ill|Kasayama Kōjinja|ja|笠山荒神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasayama Kōjinja|qq=}}''' (Sakurai, '''Kasa''') * '''Hime-Ōji Shrine''' (a subsidiary shrine of Tashinja) * '''{{ill|Shiki Mikenuza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Mikenuza Shrine|qq=}}''' (Sakurai, Kanaya) * '''{{ill|Obu Tenjinja|ja|小夫天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obu Tenjinja|qq=}}''' (Sakurai, Obu) * '''{{ill|Anashizahyozu Shrine|ja|穴師坐兵主神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anashizahyozu Shrine|qq=}}''' (Sakurai, Anashi) * '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Takaichi District, Nara|ja|高市郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaichi District, Nara|qq=}}, '''Asuka Village, Asuka''') * '''Haseyamaguchi Shrine''' (Sakurai, Hase-Tejikarao)
Some shrines, such as '''Hibara Shrine, Kasayama Kōjinja, and Obu Tenjinja''', are '''located deep in the mountains'''. Additionally, the name '''Jinraku (秦楽)''', associated with Kasanui Shrine, is also found '''in the plains below the front part of the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun|ja|築山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Yamatotakada, Nara|ja|大和高田市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatotakada, Nara|qq=}}''', where it was historically written as '''"神楽" (Jinraku/Kagura)'''. This could suggest some connection.
From '''Tashinja''', looking in the direction of '''Yatsugikamino Mikoto Shrine (矢継ぎ神命神社)''' (dedicated to '''Tenmyō Toyotama no Mikoto'''), one faces the '''Jinraku and Tsukiyama Kofun direction'''. However, due to '''Yatsugi no Mori (矢継ぎの森)''', a sacred grove preserved by locals, '''the view is blocked'''.
It is also speculated that '''in ancient times, the view from Tashinja to Hime-Ōji Shrine may have included the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashihaka Kofun|qq=}}'''. Originally, '''Hime-Ōji Shrine was located to the north (upper seat) of Tashinja''', but was possibly '''relocated southward (lower seat)'''.
If '''Tashinja is indeed the site of Kasanui-mura''', then these kofun formations might correspond to '''the origins of the {{ill|Daijōsai|ja|大嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōsai|qq=}}'s {{ill|Yuki-den|ja|悠紀殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuki-den|qq=}}''' (which has '''cut-inward 千木 [Chigi] like {{ill|Kōtai Jingu|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}}''') and '''Shuki-den (主基殿)''' (which has '''cut-outward Chigi like {{ill|Toyoukedai Jingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoukedai Jingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}}'''){{r|Heisei Daijōsai/p182}}.
== Kasanui Island in Osaka == At '''{{ill|Fukae Inari Shrine|ja|深江稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukae Inari Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Higashinari-ku, Osaka|ja|大阪市 東成区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinari-ku, Osaka|qq=}} '''Fukaeminami'''), there is a tradition that '''Fukae was once the residence of the {{ill|Kasanui clan|ja|笠縫氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasanui clan|qq=}}''', who migrated from '''Kasanui-mura in Yamato'''.
The '''Man'yōshū poet, {{ill|Takechi no Kurohito|ja|高市黒人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takechi no Kurohito|qq=}}''', wrote: > *"Shihatsu-yama uchi-koe mireba Kasanui no shima kogi-kakuru tana nashi kobune"* > *("Crossing over Mount Shihatsu, I see Kasanui Island, where small boats without roofs disappear.")*
In ancient times, '''Kasanui Island''' was believed to have been an island in an inlet '''stretching from present-day Fukae to {{ill|Higashiōsaka, Osaka|ja|東大阪市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiōsaka, Osaka|qq=}} Ashiya'''. The '''Kasanui clan likely lived on the island''' to secure '''materials for making straw hats (笠, kasa)'''. This suggests that '''the original Kasanui-mura in Yamato might also have been a low-lying wetland or an island-like terrain'''{{who|date=June 2024}}.
Even today, '''the straw hats used in the Daijōsai are made in Fukae and presented to the Imperial Household'''. Additionally, '''Fukae has strong historical ties to the casting of the Imperial Divine Mirror (御神鏡)''', suggesting a connection with '''{{ill|Kagamitsukuri Shrine|ja|鏡作神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagamitsukuri Shrine|qq=}}''' in '''Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, Yao'''.

[[sn:Kasanuimura]] [[en:Kasanuimura]] [[ja:笠縫邑]]

'''{{ill|Kasanui-mura|ja|笠縫邑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasanui-mura|qq=}}''' (かさぬいむら, かさぬいのむら) is the place where '''{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}''' was '''transferred from the imperial palace in the 6th year of Emperor {{ill|Suijin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin|qq=}}'s reign''' and entrusted to '''{{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}''' for enshrinement. At the same time, '''{{ill|Yamato Ōkunidama no Kami|ja|倭大国魂神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Ōkunidama no Kami|qq=}}''', who was also removed from the imperial palace, was entrusted to '''{{ill|Nunakiirihime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入媛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunakiirihime no Mikoto|qq=}}''' and later enshrined at '''{{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Shrine|qq=}}'''.
== Overview == There is a theory that '''Kasanui-mura is related to the origins of the {{ill|Daijosai|ja|大嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōsai|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyoakari no Sechie|ja|豊明節会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoakari no Sechie|qq=}}'''. According to the '''25th year of Emperor {{ill|Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suinin|qq=}}'s reign''', '''Princess {{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=}}''' enshrined '''Amaterasu''' at '''{{ill|Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto|ja|磯城の厳橿の本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto|qq=}}'''. The '''Ihokashi tree''' was considered a '''sacred tree for divine spirits''', and during Emperor Suijin's reign, the '''divine pillar of {{ill|Isokashiro Shrine|ja|磯堅城の神籬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isokashiro Shrine|qq=}}''' was erected ('''Suijin Chronicles, 6th year'''){{who|date=June 2024}}. In '''{{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogo Shūi|qq=}}''', it is referred to as '''"Ishiki no Himorogi"''', suggesting that '''"Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto" and "Isokashiro no Himorogi"''' might be the same place{{who|date=June 2024}}.
=== Possible Locations=== There are various theories about the '''location of Kasanui-mura''', including: * '''{{ill|Hibara Shrine (Nara)|ja|檜原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibara Shrine (Nara)|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai, Nara|qq=}} '''Miwa''') * '''{{ill|Tashinja|ja|多神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tashinja|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Shiki District, Nara|ja|磯城郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki District, Nara|qq=}} '''Tawaramoto Town, Ta''') * '''{{ill|Kasanui Shrine|ja|笠縫神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasanui Shrine|qq=}}''' (Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, '''Hazushō''', located in the '''southeastern corner of {{ill|Jinraku-ji|ja|秦楽寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinraku-ji|qq=}}''') * '''{{ill|Kasayama Kōjinja|ja|笠山荒神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasayama Kōjinja|qq=}}''' (Sakurai, '''Kasa''') * '''Hime-Ōji Shrine''' (a subsidiary shrine of Tashinja) * '''{{ill|Shiki Mikenuza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Mikenuza Shrine|qq=}}''' (Sakurai, Kanaya) * '''{{ill|Obu Tenjinja|ja|小夫天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obu Tenjinja|qq=}}''' (Sakurai, Obu) * '''{{ill|Anashizahyozu Shrine|ja|穴師坐兵主神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anashizahyozu Shrine|qq=}}''' (Sakurai, Anashi) * '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Takaichi District, Nara|ja|高市郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaichi District, Nara|qq=}}, '''Asuka Village, Asuka''') * '''Haseyamaguchi Shrine''' (Sakurai, Hase-Tejikarao)
Some shrines, such as '''Hibara Shrine, Kasayama Kōjinja, and Obu Tenjinja''', are '''located deep in the mountains'''. Additionally, the name '''Jinraku (秦楽)''', associated with Kasanui Shrine, is also found '''in the plains below the front part of the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun|ja|築山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Yamatotakada, Nara|ja|大和高田市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatotakada, Nara|qq=}}''', where it was historically written as '''"神楽" (Jinraku/Kagura)'''. This could suggest some connection.
From '''Tashinja''', looking in the direction of '''Yatsugikamino Mikoto Shrine (矢継ぎ神命神社)''' (dedicated to '''Tenmyō Toyotama no Mikoto'''), one faces the '''Jinraku and Tsukiyama Kofun direction'''. However, due to '''Yatsugi no Mori (矢継ぎの森)''', a sacred grove preserved by locals, '''the view is blocked'''.
It is also speculated that '''in ancient times, the view from Tashinja to Hime-Ōji Shrine may have included the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashihaka Kofun|qq=}}'''. Originally, '''Hime-Ōji Shrine was located to the north (upper seat) of Tashinja''', but was possibly '''relocated southward (lower seat)'''.
If '''Tashinja is indeed the site of Kasanui-mura''', then these kofun formations might correspond to '''the origins of the {{ill|Daijosai|ja|大嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōsai|qq=}}'s {{ill|Yuki-den|ja|悠紀殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuki-den|qq=}}''' (which has '''cut-inward 千木 [Chigi] like {{ill|Kōtai Jingu|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}}''') and '''Shuki-den (主基殿)''' (which has '''cut-outward Chigi like {{ill|Toyoukedai Jingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoukedai Jingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}}'''){{r|Heisei Daijōsai/p182}}.
== Kasanui Island in Osaka == At '''{{ill|Fukae Inari Shrine|ja|深江稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukae Inari Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Higashinari-ku, Osaka|ja|大阪市 東成区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinari-ku, Osaka|qq=}} '''Fukaeminami'''), there is a tradition that '''Fukae was once the residence of the {{ill|Kasanui clan|ja|笠縫氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasanui clan|qq=}}''', who migrated from '''Kasanui-mura in Yamato'''.

The '''Ihokashi tree''' was considered a '''sacred tree for divine spirits''', and during Emperor Suijin's reign, the '''divine pillar of {{ill|Isokashiro Shrine|ja|磯堅城の神籬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isokashiro Shrine|qq=}}''' was erected ('''Suijin Chronicles, 6th year'''){{who|date=June 2024}}. In '''{{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogo Shūi|qq=}}''', it is referred to as '''"Ishiki no Himorogi"''', suggesting that '''"Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto" and "Isokashiro no Himorogi"''' might be the same place{{who|date=June 2024}}.
=== Possible Locations=== There are various theories about the '''location of Kasanui-mura''', including: * '''{{ill|Hibara Shrine (Nara)|ja|檜原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibara Shrine (Nara)|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai, Nara|qq=}} '''Miwa''') * '''{{ill|Tashinja|ja|多神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tashinja|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Shiki District, Nara|ja|磯城郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki District, Nara|qq=}} '''Tawaramoto Town, Ta''') * '''{{ill|Kasanui Shrine|ja|笠縫神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasanui Shrine|qq=}}''' (Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, '''Hazushō''', located in the '''southeastern corner of {{ill|Jinraku-ji|ja|秦楽寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinraku-ji|qq=}}''') * '''{{ill|Kasayama Kōjinja|ja|笠山荒神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasayama Kōjinja|qq=}}''' (Sakurai, '''Kasa''') * '''Hime-Ōji Shrine''' (a subsidiary shrine of Tashinja) * '''{{ill|Shiki Mikenuza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Mikenuza Shrine|qq=}}''' (Sakurai, Kanaya) * '''{{ill|Obu Tenjinja|ja|小夫天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obu Tenjinja|qq=}}''' (Sakurai, Obu) * '''{{ill|Anashizahyozu Shrine|ja|穴師坐兵主神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anashizahyozu Shrine|qq=}}''' (Sakurai, Anashi) * '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Takaichi District, Nara|ja|高市郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaichi District, Nara|qq=}}, '''Asuka Village, Asuka''') * '''Haseyamaguchi Shrine''' (Sakurai, Hase-Tejikarao)
Some shrines, such as '''Hibara Shrine, Kasayama Kōjinja, and Obu Tenjinja''', are '''located deep in the mountains'''. Additionally, the name '''Jinraku (秦楽)''', associated with Kasanui Shrine, is also found '''in the plains below the front part of the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun|ja|築山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Yamatotakada, Nara|ja|大和高田市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatotakada, Nara|qq=}}''', where it was historically written as '''"神楽" (Jinraku/Kagura)'''. This could suggest some connection.
From '''Tashinja''', looking in the direction of '''Yatsugikamino Mikoto Shrine (矢継ぎ神命神社)''' (dedicated to '''Tenmyō Toyotama no Mikoto'''), one faces the '''Jinraku and Tsukiyama Kofun direction'''. However, due to '''Yatsugi no Mori (矢継ぎの森)''', a sacred grove preserved by locals, '''the view is blocked'''.
It is also speculated that '''in ancient times, the view from Tashinja to Hime-Ōji Shrine may have included the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashihaka Kofun|qq=}}'''. Originally, '''Hime-Ōji Shrine was located to the north (upper seat) of Tashinja''', but was possibly '''relocated southward (lower seat)'''.
If '''Tashinja is indeed the site of Kasanui-mura''', then these kofun formations might correspond to '''the origins of the {{ill|Daijosai|ja|大嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōsai|qq=}}'s {{ill|Yuki-den|ja|悠紀殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuki-den|qq=}}''' (which has '''cut-inward 千木 [Chigi] like {{ill|Kōtai Jingu|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}}''') and '''Shuki-den (主基殿)''' (which has '''cut-outward Chigi like {{ill|Toyoukedai Jingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoukedai Jingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}}'''){{r|Heisei Daijōsai/p182}}.
== Kasanui Island in Osaka == At '''{{ill|Fukae Inari Shrine|ja|深江稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukae Inari Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Higashinari-ku, Osaka|ja|大阪市 東成区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinari-ku, Osaka|qq=}} '''Fukaeminami'''), there is a tradition that '''Fukae was once the residence of the {{ill|Kasanui clan|ja|笠縫氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasanui clan|qq=}}''', who migrated from '''Kasanui-mura in Yamato'''.
The '''Man'yōshū poet, {{ill|Takechi no Kurohito|ja|高市黒人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takechi no Kurohito|qq=}}''', wrote: > *"Shihatsu-yama uchi-koe mireba Kasanui no shima kogi-kakuru tana nashi kobune"* > *("Crossing over Mount Shihatsu, I see Kasanui Island, where small boats without roofs disappear.")*
In ancient times, '''Kasanui Island''' was believed to have been an island in an inlet '''stretching from present-day Fukae to {{ill|Higashiōsaka, Osaka|ja|東大阪市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiōsaka, Osaka|qq=}} Ashiya'''. The '''Kasanui clan likely lived on the island''' to secure '''materials for making straw hats (笠, kasa)'''. This suggests that '''the original Kasanui-mura in Yamato might also have been a low-lying wetland or an island-like terrain'''{{who|date=June 2024}}.
Even today, '''the straw hats used in the Daijōsai are made in Fukae and presented to the Imperial Household'''. Additionally, '''Fukae has strong historical ties to the casting of the Imperial Divine Mirror (御神鏡)''', suggesting a connection with '''{{ill|Kagamitsukuri Shrine|ja|鏡作神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagamitsukuri Shrine|qq=}}''' in '''Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, Yao'''.
== See Also == * {{ill|Motoise|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motoise|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} * {{ill|Kasanui Station|ja|笠縫駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasanui Station|qq=}}
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="Heisei Daijōsai/p182">{{Citation|和書 | last = Kamata | first = Junichi | author-link = 鎌田純一 | title = Heisei Daijōsai Yōwa: Sokui-rei & Daijōsai | publisher = {{ill|Kinsei-sha|ja|錦正社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinsei-sha|qq=}} | date = July 2003 | page = 182 | id = {{NDL|20452668}} | isbn = 4-7646-0262-8 }}</ref> }}
{{translated page|ja|笠縫邑|version=101402403|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.comment: Bot: replace {{ill}} with enwiki link via Wikidata
revision.text:
'''{{ill|Kasanuimura|lt=Kasanuimura|lt=Kasanui-mura|ja|笠縫邑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasanui-mura|qq=}}''' (かさぬいむら, かさぬいのむら) is the place where '''[[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]''' was '''transferred from the imperial palace in the 6th year of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Suijin]]'s reign''' and entrusted to '''{{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}''' for enshrinement. At the same time, '''[[:en:Yamato Okunitama|Yamato Ōkunidama no Kami]]''', who was also removed from the imperial palace, was entrusted to '''{{ill|Nunakiirihime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入媛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunakiirihime no Mikoto|qq=}}''' and later enshrined at '''[[:en:Ōyamato Shrine|Ōyamato Shrine]]'''.
== Overview == There is a theory that '''Kasanui-mura is related to the origins of the [[:en:Daijosai|Daijosai]] and {{ill|Toyoakari no Sechie|ja|豊明節会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoakari no Sechie|qq=}}'''. According to the '''25th year of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Suinin]]'s reign''', '''Princess [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]''' enshrined '''Amaterasu''' at '''{{ill|Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto|ja|磯城の厳橿の本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto|qq=}}'''. The '''Ihokashi tree''' was considered a '''sacred tree for divine spirits''', and during Emperor Suijin's reign, the '''divine pillar of {{ill|Isokashiro Shrine|ja|磯堅城の神籬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isokashiro Shrine|qq=}}''' was erected ('''Suijin Chronicles, 6th year'''){{who|date=June 2024}}. In '''[[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]]''', it is referred to as '''"Ishiki no Himorogi"''', suggesting that '''"Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto" and "Isokashiro no Himorogi"''' might be the same place{{who|date=June 2024}}.
=== Possible Locations=== There are various theories about the '''location of Kasanui-mura''', including: * '''{{ill|Hibara Shrine (Nara)|ja|檜原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibara Shrine (Nara)|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]] '''Miwa''') * '''{{ill|Tashinja|ja|多神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tashinja|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Shiki District, Nara|Shiki District, Nara]] '''Tawaramoto Town, Ta''') * '''{{ill|Kasanui Shrine|ja|笠縫神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasanui Shrine|qq=}}''' (Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, '''Hazushō''', located in the '''southeastern corner of [[:en:Jinraku-ji|Jinraku-ji]]''') * '''{{ill|Kasayama Kōjinja|ja|笠山荒神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasayama Kōjinja|qq=}}''' (Sakurai, '''Kasa''') * '''Hime-Ōji Shrine''' (a subsidiary shrine of Tashinja) * '''{{ill|Shiki Mikenuza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Mikenuza Shrine|qq=}}''' (Sakurai, Kanaya) * '''{{ill|Obu Tenjinja|ja|小夫天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obu Tenjinja|qq=}}''' (Sakurai, Obu) * '''{{ill|Anashizahyozu Shrine|ja|穴師坐兵主神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anashizahyozu Shrine|qq=}}''' (Sakurai, Anashi) * '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Takaichi District, Nara|Takaichi District, Nara]], '''Asuka Village, Asuka''') * '''Haseyamaguchi Shrine''' (Sakurai, Hase-Tejikarao)
Some shrines, such as '''Hibara Shrine, Kasayama Kōjinja, and Obu Tenjinja''', are '''located deep in the mountains'''. Additionally, the name '''Jinraku (秦楽)''', associated with Kasanui Shrine, is also found '''in the plains below the front part of the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun|ja|築山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Yamatotakada, Nara|Yamatotakada, Nara]]''', where it was historically written as '''"神楽" (Jinraku/Kagura)'''. This could suggest some connection.
From '''Tashinja''', looking in the direction of '''Yatsugikamino Mikoto Shrine (矢継ぎ神命神社)''' (dedicated to '''Tenmyō Toyotama no Mikoto'''), one faces the '''Jinraku and Tsukiyama Kofun direction'''. However, due to '''Yatsugi no Mori (矢継ぎの森)''', a sacred grove preserved by locals, '''the view is blocked'''.
It is also speculated that '''in ancient times, the view from Tashinja to Hime-Ōji Shrine may have included the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashihaka Kofun|qq=}}'''. Originally, '''Hime-Ōji Shrine was located to the north (upper seat) of Tashinja''', but was possibly '''relocated southward (lower seat)'''.
If '''Tashinja is indeed the site of Kasanui-mura''', then these kofun formations might correspond to '''the origins of the [[:en:Daijosai|Daijosai]]'s {{ill|Yuki-den|ja|悠紀殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuki-den|qq=}}''' (which has '''cut-inward 千木 [Chigi] like {{ill|Kōtai Jingu|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}}''') and '''Shuki-den (主基殿)''' (which has '''cut-outward Chigi like {{ill|Toyoukedai Jingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoukedai Jingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}}'''){{r|Heisei Daijōsai/p182}}.
== Kasanui Island in Osaka == At '''{{ill|Fukae Inari Shrine|ja|深江稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukae Inari Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Higashinari-ku, Osaka|ja|大阪市 東成区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinari-ku, Osaka|qq=}} '''Fukaeminami'''), there is a tradition that '''Fukae was once the residence of the {{ill|Kasanui clan|ja|笠縫氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasanui clan|qq=}}''', who migrated from '''Kasanui-mura in Yamato'''.
The '''Man'yōshū poet, [[:en:Takechi no Kurohito|Takechi no Kurohito]]''', wrote: > *"Shihatsu-yama uchi-koe mireba Kasanui no shima kogi-kakuru tana nashi kobune"* > *("Crossing over Mount Shihatsu, I see Kasanui Island, where small boats without roofs disappear.")*
In ancient times, '''Kasanui Island''' was believed to have been an island in an inlet '''stretching from present-day Fukae to [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka, Osaka]] Ashiya'''. The '''Kasanui clan likely lived on the island''' to secure '''materials for making straw hats (笠, kasa)'''. This suggests that '''the original Kasanui-mura in Yamato might also have been a low-lying wetland or an island-like terrain'''{{who|date=June 2024}}.
Even today, '''the straw hats used in the Daijōsai are made in Fukae and presented to the Imperial Household'''. Additionally, '''Fukae has strong historical ties to the casting of the Imperial Divine Mirror (御神鏡)''', suggesting a connection with '''{{ill|Kagamitsukuri Shrine|ja|鏡作神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagamitsukuri Shrine|qq=}}''' in '''Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, Yao'''.
== See Also == * {{ill|Motoise|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motoise|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} * [[:en:Kasanui Station|Kasanui Station]]
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="Heisei Daijōsai/p182">{{Citation|和書 | last = Kamata | first = Junichi | author-link = 鎌田純一 | title = Heisei Daijōsai Yōwa: Sokui-rei & Daijōsai | publisher = {{ill|Kinsei-sha|ja|錦正社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinsei-sha|qq=}} | date = July 2003 | page = 182 | id = {{NDL|20452668}} | isbn = 4-7646-0262-8 }}</ref> }}

This could suggest some connection.

It is also speculated that '''in ancient times, the view from Tashinja to Hime-Ōji Shrine may have included the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashihaka Kofun|qq=}}'''. Originally, '''Hime-Ōji Shrine was located to the north (upper seat) of Tashinja''', but was possibly '''relocated southward (lower seat)'''.
If '''Tashinja is indeed the site of Kasanui-mura''', then these kofun formations might correspond to '''the origins of the [[:en:Daijosai|Daijosai]]'s {{ill|Yuki-den|ja|悠紀殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuki-den|qq=}}''' (which has '''cut-inward 千木 [Chigi] like {{ill|Kōtai Jingu|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}}''') and '''Shuki-den (主基殿)''' (which has '''cut-outward Chigi like {{ill|Toyoukedai Jingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoukedai Jingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}}'''){{r|Heisei Daijōsai/p182}}.
== Kasanui Island in Osaka == At '''{{ill|Fukae Inari Shrine|ja|深江稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukae Inari Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Higashinari-ku, Osaka|ja|大阪市 東成区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinari-ku, Osaka|qq=}} '''Fukaeminami'''), there is a tradition that '''Fukae was once the residence of the {{ill|Kasanui clan|ja|笠縫氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasanui clan|qq=}}''', who migrated from '''Kasanui-mura in Yamato'''.
The '''Man'yōshū poet, [[:en:Takechi no Kurohito|Takechi no Kurohito]]''', wrote: > *"Shihatsu-yama uchi-koe mireba Kasanui no shima kogi-kakuru tana nashi kobune"* > *("Crossing over Mount Shihatsu, I see Kasanui Island, where small boats without roofs disappear.")*
In ancient times, '''Kasanui Island''' was believed to have been an island in an inlet '''stretching from present-day Fukae to [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka, Osaka]] Ashiya'''. The '''Kasanui clan likely lived on the island''' to secure '''materials for making straw hats (笠, kasa)'''. This suggests that '''the original Kasanui-mura in Yamato might also have been a low-lying wetland or an island-like terrain'''{{who|date=June 2024}}.
Even today, '''the straw hats used in the Daijōsai are made in Fukae and presented to the Imperial Household'''. Additionally, '''Fukae has strong historical ties to the casting of the Imperial Divine Mirror (御神鏡)''', suggesting a connection with '''{{ill|Kagamitsukuri Shrine|ja|鏡作神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagamitsukuri Shrine|qq=}}''' in '''Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, Yao'''.
== See Also == * {{ill|Motoise|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motoise|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} * [[:en:Kasanui Station|Kasanui Station]]
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="Heisei Daijōsai/p182">{{Citation|和書 | last = Kamata | first = Junichi | author-link = 鎌田純一 | title = Heisei Daijōsai Yōwa: Sokui-rei & Daijōsai | publisher = {{ill|Kinsei-sha|ja|錦正社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinsei-sha|qq=}} | date = July 2003 | page = 182 | id = {{NDL|20452668}} | isbn = 4-7646-0262-8 }}</ref> }}

[[sn:Kasanuimura]] [[en:Kasanuimura]] [[ja:笠縫邑]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Osaka Prefecture]]

'''{{ill|Kasanuimura|lt=Kasanuimura|lt=Kasanui-mura|ja|笠縫邑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasanui-mura|qq=}}''' (かさぬいむら, かさぬいのむら) is the place where '''[[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]''' was '''transferred from the imperial palace in the 6th year of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Suijin]]'s reign''' and entrusted to '''{{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}''' for enshrinement. At the same time, '''[[:en:Yamato Okunitama|Yamato Ōkunidama no Kami]]''', who was also removed from the imperial palace, was entrusted to '''{{ill|Nunakiirihime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入媛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunakiirihime no Mikoto|qq=}}''' and later enshrined at '''[[:en:Ōyamato Shrine|Ōyamato Shrine]]'''.
== Overview == There is a theory that '''Kasanui-mura is related to the origins of the [[:en:Daijosai|Daijosai]] and {{ill|Toyoakari no Sechie|ja|豊明節会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoakari no Sechie|qq=}}'''. According to the '''25th year of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Suinin]]'s reign''', '''Princess [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]''' enshrined '''Amaterasu''' at '''{{ill|Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto|ja|磯城の厳橿の本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto|qq=}}'''. The '''Ihokashi tree''' was considered a '''sacred tree for divine spirits''', and during Emperor Suijin's reign, the '''divine pillar of {{ill|Isokashiro Shrine|ja|磯堅城の神籬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isokashiro Shrine|qq=}}''' was erected ('''Suijin Chronicles, 6th year'''){{who|date=June 2024}}. In '''[[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]]''', it is referred to as '''"Ishiki no Himorogi"''', suggesting that '''"Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto" and "Isokashiro no Himorogi"''' might be the same place{{who|date=June 2024}}.
=== Possible Locations=== There are various theories about the '''location of Kasanui-mura''', including: * '''{{ill|Hibara Shrine (Nara)|ja|檜原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibara Shrine (Nara)|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]] '''Miwa''') * '''{{ill|Tashinja|ja|多神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tashinja|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Shiki District, Nara|Shiki District, Nara]] '''Tawaramoto Town, Ta''') * '''{{ill|Kasanui Shrine|ja|笠縫神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasanui Shrine|qq=}}''' (Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, '''Hazushō''', located in the '''southeastern corner of [[:en:Jinraku-ji|Jinraku-ji]]''') * '''{{ill|Kasayama Kōjinja|ja|笠山荒神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasayama Kōjinja|qq=}}''' (Sakurai, '''Kasa''') * '''Hime-Ōji Shrine''' (a subsidiary shrine of Tashinja) * '''{{ill|Shiki Mikenuza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Mikenuza Shrine|qq=}}''' (Sakurai, Kanaya) * '''{{ill|Obu Tenjinja|ja|小夫天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obu Tenjinja|qq=}}''' (Sakurai, Obu) * '''{{ill|Anashizahyozu Shrine|ja|穴師坐兵主神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anashizahyozu Shrine|qq=}}''' (Sakurai, Anashi) * '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Takaichi District, Nara|Takaichi District, Nara]], '''Asuka Village, Asuka''') * '''Haseyamaguchi Shrine''' (Sakurai, Hase-Tejikarao)
Some shrines, such as '''Hibara Shrine, Kasayama Kōjinja, and Obu Tenjinja''', are '''located deep in the mountains'''. Additionally, the name '''Jinraku (秦楽)''', associated with Kasanui Shrine, is also found '''in the plains below the front part of the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun|ja|築山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Yamatotakada, Nara|Yamatotakada, Nara]]''', where it was historically written as '''"神楽" (Jinraku/Kagura)'''. This could suggest some connection.
From '''Tashinja''', looking in the direction of '''Yatsugikamino Mikoto Shrine (矢継ぎ神命神社)''' (dedicated to '''Tenmyō Toyotama no Mikoto'''), one faces the '''Jinraku and Tsukiyama Kofun direction'''. However, due to '''Yatsugi no Mori (矢継ぎの森)''', a sacred grove preserved by locals, '''the view is blocked'''.
It is also speculated that '''in ancient times, the view from Tashinja to Hime-Ōji Shrine may have included the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashihaka Kofun|qq=}}'''. Originally, '''Hime-Ōji Shrine was located to the north (upper seat) of Tashinja''', but was possibly '''relocated southward (lower seat)'''.
If '''Tashinja is indeed the site of Kasanui-mura''', then these kofun formations might correspond to '''the origins of the [[:en:Daijosai|Daijosai]]'s {{ill|Yuki-den|ja|悠紀殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuki-den|qq=}}''' (which has '''cut-inward 千木 [Chigi] like {{ill|Kōtai Jingu|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}}''') and '''Shuki-den (主基殿)''' (which has '''cut-outward Chigi like {{ill|Toyoukedai Jingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoukedai Jingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}}'''){{r|Heisei Daijōsai/p182}}.
== Kasanui Island in Osaka == At '''{{ill|Fukae Inari Shrine|ja|深江稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukae Inari Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Higashinari-ku, Osaka|ja|大阪市 東成区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinari-ku, Osaka|qq=}} '''Fukaeminami'''), there is a tradition that '''Fukae was once the residence of the {{ill|Kasanui clan|ja|笠縫氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasanui clan|qq=}}''', who migrated from '''Kasanui-mura in Yamato'''.
The '''Man'yōshū poet, [[:en:Takechi no Kurohito|Takechi no Kurohito]]''', wrote: > *"Shihatsu-yama uchi-koe mireba Kasanui no shima kogi-kakuru tana nashi kobune"* > *("Crossing over Mount Shihatsu, I see Kasanui Island, where small boats without roofs disappear.")*
In ancient times, '''Kasanui Island''' was believed to have been an island in an inlet '''stretching from present-day Fukae to [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka, Osaka]] Ashiya'''. The '''Kasanui clan likely lived on the island''' to secure '''materials for making straw hats (笠, kasa)'''. This suggests that '''the original Kasanui-mura in Yamato might also have been a low-lying wetland or an island-like terrain'''{{who|date=June 2024}}.
Even today, '''the straw hats used in the Daijōsai are made in Fukae and presented to the Imperial Household'''. Additionally, '''Fukae has strong historical ties to the casting of the Imperial Divine Mirror (御神鏡)''', suggesting a connection with '''{{ill|Kagamitsukuri Shrine|ja|鏡作神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagamitsukuri Shrine|qq=}}''' in '''Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, Yao'''.

At the same time, '''[[:en:Yamato Okunitama|Yamato Ōkunidama no Kami]]''', who was also removed from the imperial palace, was entrusted to '''{{ill|Nunakiirihime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入媛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunakiirihime no Mikoto|qq=}}''' and later enshrined at '''[[:en:Ōyamato Shrine|Ōyamato Shrine]]'''.

The '''Ihokashi tree''' was considered a '''sacred tree for divine spirits''', and during Emperor Suijin's reign, the '''divine pillar of {{ill|Isokashiro Shrine|ja|磯堅城の神籬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isokashiro Shrine|qq=}}''' was erected ('''Suijin Chronicles, 6th year'''){{who|date=June 2024}}. === Possible Locations=== There are various theories about the '''location of Kasanui-mura''', including: * '''{{ill|Hibara Shrine (Nara)|ja|檜原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibara Shrine (Nara)|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]] '''Miwa''') * '''{{ill|Tashinja|ja|多神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tashinja|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Shiki District, Nara|Shiki District, Nara]] '''Tawaramoto Town, Ta''') * '''{{ill|Kasanui Shrine|ja|笠縫神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasanui Shrine|qq=}}''' (Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, '''Hazushō''', located in the '''southeastern corner of [[:en:Jinraku-ji|Jinraku-ji]]''') * '''{{ill|Kasayama Kōjinja|ja|笠山荒神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasayama Kōjinja|qq=}}''' (Sakurai, '''Kasa''') * '''Hime-Ōji Shrine''' (a subsidiary shrine of Tashinja) * '''{{ill|Shiki Mikenuza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Mikenuza Shrine|qq=}}''' (Sakurai, Kanaya) * '''{{ill|Obu Tenjinja|ja|小夫天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obu Tenjinja|qq=}}''' (Sakurai, Obu) * '''{{ill|Anashizahyozu Shrine|ja|穴師坐兵主神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anashizahyozu Shrine|qq=}}''' (Sakurai, Anashi) * '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Takaichi District, Nara|Takaichi District, Nara]], '''Asuka Village, Asuka''') * '''Haseyamaguchi Shrine''' (Sakurai, Hase-Tejikarao)
Some shrines, such as '''Hibara Shrine, Kasayama Kōjinja, and Obu Tenjinja''', are '''located deep in the mountains'''. Additionally, the name '''Jinraku (秦楽)''', associated with Kasanui Shrine, is also found '''in the plains below the front part of the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun|ja|築山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Yamatotakada, Nara|Yamatotakada, Nara]]''', where it was historically written as '''"神楽" (Jinraku/Kagura)'''. This could suggest some connection.
From '''Tashinja''', looking in the direction of '''Yatsugikamino Mikoto Shrine (矢継ぎ神命神社)''' (dedicated to '''Tenmyō Toyotama no Mikoto'''), one faces the '''Jinraku and Tsukiyama Kofun direction'''. The '''Man'yōshū poet, [[:en:Takechi no Kurohito|Takechi no Kurohito]]''', wrote: > *"Shihatsu-yama uchi-koe mireba Kasanui no shima kogi-kakuru tana nashi kobune"* > *("Crossing over Mount Shihatsu, I see Kasanui Island, where small boats without roofs disappear.")*
In ancient times, '''Kasanui Island''' was believed to have been an island in an inlet '''stretching from present-day Fukae to [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka, Osaka]] Ashiya'''. The '''Kasanui clan likely lived on the island''' to secure '''materials for making straw hats (笠, kasa)'''. This suggests that '''the original Kasanui-mura in Yamato might also have been a low-lying wetland or an island-like terrain'''{{who|date=June 2024}}.
Even today, '''the straw hats used in the Daijōsai are made in Fukae and presented to the Imperial Household'''. Additionally, '''Fukae has strong historical ties to the casting of the Imperial Divine Mirror (御神鏡)''', suggesting a connection with '''{{ill|Kagamitsukuri Shrine|ja|鏡作神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagamitsukuri Shrine|qq=}}''' in '''Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, Yao'''.

[[sn:Kasanuimura]]
[[ja:笠縫邑]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Osaka Prefecture]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis-->

'''{{ill|Kasanuimura|lt=Kasanuimura|lt=Kasanui-mura|ja|笠縫邑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasanui-mura|qq=}}''' (かさぬいむら, かさぬいのむら) is the place where '''[[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]''' was '''transferred from the imperial palace in the 6th year of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Suijin]]'s reign''' and entrusted to '''{{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}''' for enshrinement. At the same time, '''[[:en:Yamato Okunitama|Yamato Ōkunidama no Kami]]''', who was also removed from the imperial palace, was entrusted to '''{{ill|Nunakiirihime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入媛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunakiirihime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 渟名城入姫命}}''' and later enshrined at '''[[:en:Ōyamato Shrine|Ōyamato Shrine]]'''.
== Overview == There is a theory that '''Kasanui-mura is related to the origins of the [[:en:Daijosai|Daijosai]] and {{ill|Toyoakari no Sechie|ja|豊明節会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoakari no Sechie|qq=}}'''. According to the '''25th year of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Suinin]]'s reign''', '''Princess [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]''' enshrined '''Amaterasu''' at '''{{ill|Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto|ja|磯城の厳橿の本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''. The '''Ihokashi tree''' was considered a '''sacred tree for divine spirits''', and during Emperor Suijin's reign, the '''divine pillar of {{ill|Isokashiro Shrine|ja|磯堅城の神籬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isokashiro Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' was erected ('''Suijin Chronicles, 6th year'''){{who|date=June 2024}}. In '''[[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]]''', it is referred to as '''"Ishiki no Himorogi"''', suggesting that '''"Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto" and "Isokashiro no Himorogi"''' might be the same place{{who|date=June 2024}}.
=== Possible Locations=== There are various theories about the '''location of Kasanui-mura''', including: * '''{{ill|Hibara Shrine (Nara)|ja|檜原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibara Shrine (Nara)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大神神社}}''' ([[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]] '''Miwa''') * '''{{ill|Tashinja|ja|多神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tashinja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 多坐弥志理都比古神社}}''' ([[:en:Shiki District, Nara|Shiki District, Nara]] '''Tawaramoto Town, Ta''') * '''{{ill|Kasanui Shrine|ja|笠縫神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasanui Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, '''Hazushō''', located in the '''southeastern corner of [[:en:Jinraku-ji|Jinraku-ji]]''') * '''{{ill|Kasayama Kōjinja|ja|笠山荒神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasayama Kōjinja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Sakurai, '''Kasa''') * '''Hime-Ōji Shrine''' (a subsidiary shrine of Tashinja) * '''{{ill|Shiki Mikenuza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Mikenuza Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志貴御縣坐神社}}''' (Sakurai, Kanaya) * '''{{ill|Obu Tenjinja|ja|小夫天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obu Tenjinja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Sakurai, Obu) * '''{{ill|Anashizahyozu Shrine|ja|穴師坐兵主神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anashizahyozu Shrine|qq=}}''' (Sakurai, Anashi) * '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Takaichi District, Nara|Takaichi District, Nara]], '''Asuka Village, Asuka''') * '''Haseyamaguchi Shrine''' (Sakurai, Hase-Tejikarao)
Some shrines, such as '''Hibara Shrine, Kasayama Kōjinja, and Obu Tenjinja''', are '''located deep in the mountains'''. Additionally, the name '''Jinraku (秦楽)''', associated with Kasanui Shrine, is also found '''in the plains below the front part of the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun|ja|築山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Yamatotakada, Nara|Yamatotakada, Nara]]''', where it was historically written as '''"神楽" (Jinraku/Kagura)'''. This could suggest some connection.
From '''Tashinja''', looking in the direction of '''Yatsugikamino Mikoto Shrine (矢継ぎ神命神社)''' (dedicated to '''Tenmyō Toyotama no Mikoto'''), one faces the '''Jinraku and Tsukiyama Kofun direction'''. However, due to '''Yatsugi no Mori (矢継ぎの森)''', a sacred grove preserved by locals, '''the view is blocked'''.
It is also speculated that '''in ancient times, the view from Tashinja to Hime-Ōji Shrine may have included the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashihaka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 箸墓古墳}}'''. Originally, '''Hime-Ōji Shrine was located to the north (upper seat) of Tashinja''', but was possibly '''relocated southward (lower seat)'''.
If '''Tashinja is indeed the site of Kasanui-mura''', then these kofun formations might correspond to '''the origins of the [[:en:Daijosai|Daijosai]]'s {{ill|Yuki-den|ja|悠紀殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuki-den|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (which has '''cut-inward 千木 [Chigi] like {{ill|Kōtai Jingu|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''') and '''Shuki-den (主基殿)''' (which has '''cut-outward Chigi like {{ill|Toyoukedai Jingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoukedai Jingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}'''){{r|Heisei Daijōsai/p182}}.
== Kasanui Island in Osaka == At '''{{ill|Fukae Inari Shrine|ja|深江稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukae Inari Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Higashinari-ku, Osaka|ja|大阪市 東成区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinari-ku, Osaka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} '''Fukaeminami'''), there is a tradition that '''Fukae was once the residence of the {{ill|Kasanui clan|ja|笠縫氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasanui clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', who migrated from '''Kasanui-mura in Yamato'''.
The '''Man'yōshū poet, [[:en:Takechi no Kurohito|Takechi no Kurohito]]''', wrote: > *"Shihatsu-yama uchi-koe mireba Kasanui no shima kogi-kakuru tana nashi kobune"* > *("Crossing over Mount Shihatsu, I see Kasanui Island, where small boats without roofs disappear.")*
In ancient times, '''Kasanui Island''' was believed to have been an island in an inlet '''stretching from present-day Fukae to [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka, Osaka]] Ashiya'''. The '''Kasanui clan likely lived on the island''' to secure '''materials for making straw hats (笠, kasa)'''. This suggests that '''the original Kasanui-mura in Yamato might also have been a low-lying wetland or an island-like terrain'''{{who|date=June 2024}}.
Even today, '''the straw hats used in the Daijōsai are made in Fukae and presented to the Imperial Household'''. Additionally, '''Fukae has strong historical ties to the casting of the Imperial Divine Mirror (御神鏡)''', suggesting a connection with '''{{ill|Kagamitsukuri Shrine|ja|鏡作神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagamitsukuri Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鏡作坐天照御魂神社}}''' in '''Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, Yao'''.
== See Also == * {{ill|Motoise|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motoise|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} * [[:en:Kasanui Station|Kasanui Station]]
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="Heisei Daijōsai/p182">{{Citation|和書 | last = Kamata | first = Junichi | author-link = 鎌田純一 | title = Heisei Daijōsai Yōwa: Sokui-rei & Daijōsai | publisher = {{ill|Kinsei-sha|ja|錦正社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinsei-sha|qq=}} | date = July 2003 | page = 182 | id = {{NDL|20452668}} | isbn = 4-7646-0262-8 }}</ref> }}
{{translated page|ja|笠縫邑|version=101402403|comment=Imported full ja history}}

The '''Ihokashi tree''' was considered a '''sacred tree for divine spirits''', and during Emperor Suijin's reign, the '''divine pillar of {{ill|Isokashiro Shrine|ja|磯堅城の神籬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isokashiro Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' was erected ('''Suijin Chronicles, 6th year'''){{who|date=June 2024}}. In '''[[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]]''', it is referred to as '''"Ishiki no Himorogi"''', suggesting that '''"Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto" and "Isokashiro no Himorogi"''' might be the same place{{who|date=June 2024}}.
=== Possible Locations=== There are various theories about the '''location of Kasanui-mura''', including: * '''{{ill|Hibara Shrine (Nara)|ja|檜原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibara Shrine (Nara)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大神神社}}''' ([[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]] '''Miwa''') * '''{{ill|Tashinja|ja|多神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tashinja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 多坐弥志理都比古神社}}''' ([[:en:Shiki District, Nara|Shiki District, Nara]] '''Tawaramoto Town, Ta''') * '''{{ill|Kasanui Shrine|ja|笠縫神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasanui Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, '''Hazushō''', located in the '''southeastern corner of [[:en:Jinraku-ji|Jinraku-ji]]''') * '''{{ill|Kasayama Kōjinja|ja|笠山荒神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasayama Kōjinja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Sakurai, '''Kasa''') * '''Hime-Ōji Shrine''' (a subsidiary shrine of Tashinja) * '''{{ill|Shiki Mikenuza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Mikenuza Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志貴御縣坐神社}}''' (Sakurai, Kanaya) * '''{{ill|Obu Tenjinja|ja|小夫天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obu Tenjinja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Sakurai, Obu) * '''{{ill|Anashizahyozu Shrine|ja|穴師坐兵主神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anashizahyozu Shrine|qq=}}''' (Sakurai, Anashi) * '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Takaichi District, Nara|Takaichi District, Nara]], '''Asuka Village, Asuka''') * '''Haseyamaguchi Shrine''' (Sakurai, Hase-Tejikarao)
Some shrines, such as '''Hibara Shrine, Kasayama Kōjinja, and Obu Tenjinja''', are '''located deep in the mountains'''. Additionally, the name '''Jinraku (秦楽)''', associated with Kasanui Shrine, is also found '''in the plains below the front part of the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun|ja|築山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Yamatotakada, Nara|Yamatotakada, Nara]]''', where it was historically written as '''"神楽" (Jinraku/Kagura)'''. This could suggest some connection.
From '''Tashinja''', looking in the direction of '''Yatsugikamino Mikoto Shrine (矢継ぎ神命神社)''' (dedicated to '''Tenmyō Toyotama no Mikoto'''), one faces the '''Jinraku and Tsukiyama Kofun direction'''. However, due to '''Yatsugi no Mori (矢継ぎの森)''', a sacred grove preserved by locals, '''the view is blocked'''.
It is also speculated that '''in ancient times, the view from Tashinja to Hime-Ōji Shrine may have included the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashihaka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 箸墓古墳}}'''. Originally, '''Hime-Ōji Shrine was located to the north (upper seat) of Tashinja''', but was possibly '''relocated southward (lower seat)'''.
If '''Tashinja is indeed the site of Kasanui-mura''', then these kofun formations might correspond to '''the origins of the [[:en:Daijosai|Daijosai]]'s {{ill|Yuki-den|ja|悠紀殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuki-den|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (which has '''cut-inward 千木 [Chigi] like {{ill|Kōtai Jingu|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''') and '''Shuki-den (主基殿)''' (which has '''cut-outward Chigi like {{ill|Toyoukedai Jingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoukedai Jingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}'''){{r|Heisei Daijōsai/p182}}.
== Kasanui Island in Osaka == At '''{{ill|Fukae Inari Shrine|ja|深江稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukae Inari Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Higashinari-ku, Osaka|ja|大阪市 東成区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinari-ku, Osaka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} '''Fukaeminami'''), there is a tradition that '''Fukae was once the residence of the {{ill|Kasanui clan|ja|笠縫氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasanui clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', who migrated from '''Kasanui-mura in Yamato'''.
The '''Man'yōshū poet, [[:en:Takechi no Kurohito|Takechi no Kurohito]]''', wrote: > *"Shihatsu-yama uchi-koe mireba Kasanui no shima kogi-kakuru tana nashi kobune"* > *("Crossing over Mount Shihatsu, I see Kasanui Island, where small boats without roofs disappear.")*
In ancient times, '''Kasanui Island''' was believed to have been an island in an inlet '''stretching from present-day Fukae to [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka, Osaka]] Ashiya'''. The '''Kasanui clan likely lived on the island''' to secure '''materials for making straw hats (笠, kasa)'''. This suggests that '''the original Kasanui-mura in Yamato might also have been a low-lying wetland or an island-like terrain'''{{who|date=June 2024}}.
Even today, '''the straw hats used in the Daijōsai are made in Fukae and presented to the Imperial Household'''. Additionally, '''Fukae has strong historical ties to the casting of the Imperial Divine Mirror (御神鏡)''', suggesting a connection with '''{{ill|Kagamitsukuri Shrine|ja|鏡作神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagamitsukuri Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鏡作坐天照御魂神社}}''' in '''Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, Yao'''.
== See Also == * {{ill|Motoise|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motoise|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} * [[:en:Kasanui Station|Kasanui Station]]
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="Heisei Daijōsai/p182">{{Citation|和書 | last = Kamata | first = Junichi | author-link = 鎌田純一 | title = Heisei Daijōsai Yōwa: Sokui-rei & Daijōsai | publisher = {{ill|Kinsei-sha|ja|錦正社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinsei-sha|qq=}} | date = July 2003 | page = 182 | id = {{NDL|20452668}} | isbn = 4-7646-0262-8 }}</ref> }}
{{translated page|ja|笠縫邑|version=101402403|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11599947]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11599947]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''{{ill|Kasanuimura|lt=Kasanuimura|lt=Kasanui-mura|ja|笠縫邑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasanui-mura|qq=}}''' (かさぬいむら, かさぬいのむら) is the place where '''[[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]''' was '''transferred from the imperial palace in the 6th year of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Suijin]]'s reign''' and entrusted to '''{{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}''' for enshrinement. At the same time, '''[[:en:Yamato Okunitama|Yamato Ōkunidama no Kami]]''', who was also removed from the imperial palace, was entrusted to '''{{ill|Nunakiirihime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入媛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunakiirihime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 渟名城入姫命}}''' and later enshrined at '''[[:en:Ōyamato Shrine|Ōyamato Shrine]]'''.
== Overview == There is a theory that '''Kasanui-mura is related to the origins of the [[:en:Daijosai|Daijosai]] and {{ill|Toyoakari no Sechie|ja|豊明節会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoakari no Sechie|qq=}}'''. According to the '''25th year of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Suinin]]'s reign''', '''Princess [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]''' enshrined '''Amaterasu''' at '''{{ill|Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto|ja|磯城の厳橿の本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''. The '''Ihokashi tree''' was considered a '''sacred tree for divine spirits''', and during Emperor Suijin's reign, the '''divine pillar of {{ill|Isokashiro Shrine|ja|磯堅城の神籬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isokashiro Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' was erected ('''Suijin Chronicles, 6th year'''){{who|date=June 2024}}. In '''[[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]]''', it is referred to as '''"Ishiki no Himorogi"''', suggesting that '''"Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto" and "Isokashiro no Himorogi"''' might be the same place{{who|date=June 2024}}.
=== Possible Locations=== There are various theories about the '''location of Kasanui-mura''', including: * '''{{ill|Hibara Shrine (Nara)|ja|檜原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibara Shrine (Nara)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大神神社}}''' ([[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]] '''Miwa''') * '''{{ill|Tashinja|ja|多神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tashinja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 多坐弥志理都比古神社}}''' ([[:en:Shiki District, Nara|Shiki District, Nara]] '''Tawaramoto Town, Ta''') * '''{{ill|Kasanui Shrine|ja|笠縫神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasanui Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, '''Hazushō''', located in the '''southeastern corner of [[:en:Jinraku-ji|Jinraku-ji]]''') * '''{{ill|Kasayama Kōjinja|ja|笠山荒神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasayama Kōjinja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Sakurai, '''Kasa''') * '''Hime-Ōji Shrine''' (a subsidiary shrine of Tashinja) * '''{{ill|Shiki Mikenuza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Mikenuza Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志貴御縣坐神社}}''' (Sakurai, Kanaya) * '''{{ill|Obu Tenjinja|ja|小夫天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obu Tenjinja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Sakurai, Obu) * '''{{ill|Anashizahyozu Shrine|ja|穴師坐兵主神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anashizahyozu Shrine|qq=}}''' (Sakurai, Anashi) * '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Takaichi District, Nara|Takaichi District, Nara]], '''Asuka Village, Asuka''') * '''Haseyamaguchi Shrine''' (Sakurai, Hase-Tejikarao)
Some shrines, such as '''Hibara Shrine, Kasayama Kōjinja, and Obu Tenjinja''', are '''located deep in the mountains'''. Additionally, the name '''Jinraku (秦楽)''', associated with Kasanui Shrine, is also found '''in the plains below the front part of the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun|ja|築山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Yamatotakada, Nara|Yamatotakada, Nara]]''', where it was historically written as '''"神楽" (Jinraku/Kagura)'''. This could suggest some connection.
From '''Tashinja''', looking in the direction of '''Yatsugikamino Mikoto Shrine (矢継ぎ神命神社)''' (dedicated to '''Tenmyō Toyotama no Mikoto'''), one faces the '''Jinraku and Tsukiyama Kofun direction'''. However, due to '''Yatsugi no Mori (矢継ぎの森)''', a sacred grove preserved by locals, '''the view is blocked'''.
It is also speculated that '''in ancient times, the view from Tashinja to Hime-Ōji Shrine may have included the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashihaka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 箸墓古墳}}'''. The '''Man'yōshū poet, [[:en:Takechi no Kurohito|Takechi no Kurohito]]''', wrote: > *"Shihatsu-yama uchi-koe mireba Kasanui no shima kogi-kakuru tana nashi kobune"* > *("Crossing over Mount Shihatsu, I see Kasanui Island, where small boats without roofs disappear.")*

In '''[[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]]''', it is referred to as '''"Ishiki no Himorogi"''', suggesting that '''"Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto" and "Isokashiro no Himorogi"''' might be the same place{{who|date=June 2024}}.

Shrine (Nara)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大神神社|1=Ōmiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q705542}}''' ([[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]] '''Miwa''') * '''{{ill|Tashinja|ja|多神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tashinja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 多坐弥志理都比古神社|1=Oonimasumishiritsuhiko-jinja|comment=changed title based on Kōjinja|ja|笠山荒神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasayama Kōjinja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Sakurai, '''Kasa''') * '''Hime-Ōji Shrine''' (a subsidiary shrine of Tashinja) * '''{{ill|Shiki Mikenuza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Mikenuza Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志貴御縣坐神社|1=Shiki no Miagata Nimasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q63981812}}''' (Sakurai, Kanaya) * '''{{ill|Obu Tenjinja|ja|小夫天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obu Tenjinja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Sakurai, Obu) * '''{{ill|Anashizahyozu Shrine|ja|穴師坐兵主神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anashizahyozu Shrine|qq=|1=Anashi-Nimasu Hyōzu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597229}}''' (Sakurai, Anashi) * '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Takaichi District, Nara|Takaichi District, Nara]], '''Asuka Village, Asuka''') * '''Haseyamaguchi Shrine''' (Sakurai, Hase-Tejikarao)
Some shrines, such as '''Hibara Shrine, Kasayama Kōjinja, and Obu Tenjinja''', are '''located deep in the mountains'''. Additionally, the name '''Jinraku (秦楽)''', associated with Kasanui Shrine, is also found '''in the plains below the front part of the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun|ja|築山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Yamatotakada, Nara|Yamatotakada, Nara]]''', where it was historically written as '''"神楽" (Jinraku/Kagura)'''. This could suggest some connection.
From '''Tashinja''', looking in the direction of '''Yatsugikamino Mikoto Shrine (矢継ぎ神命神社)''' (dedicated to '''Tenmyō Toyotama no Mikoto'''), one faces the '''Jinraku and Tsukiyama Kofun direction'''. However, due to '''Yatsugi no Mori (矢継ぎの森)''', a sacred grove preserved by locals, '''the view is blocked'''.
It is also speculated that '''in ancient times, the view from Tashinja to Hime-Ōji Shrine may have included the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashihaka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 箸墓古墳|1=Hashihaka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603420}}'''. Originally, '''Hime-Ōji Shrine was located to the north (upper seat) of Tashinja''', but was possibly '''relocated southward (lower seat)'''.
If '''Tashinja is indeed the site of Kasanui-mura''', then these kofun formations might correspond to '''the origins of the [[:en:Daijosai|Daijosai]]'s {{ill|Yuki-den|ja|悠紀殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuki-den|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (which has '''cut-inward 千木 [Chigi] like {{ill|Kōtai Jingu|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''') and '''Shuki-den (主基殿)''' (which has '''cut-outward Chigi like {{ill|Toyoukedai Jingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoukedai Jingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}'''){{r|Heisei Daijōsai/p182}}.
== Kasanui Island in Osaka == At '''{{ill|Fukae Inari Shrine|ja|深江稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukae Inari Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Higashinari-ku, Osaka|ja|大阪市 東成区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinari-ku, Osaka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} '''Fukaeminami'''), there is a tradition that '''Fukae was once the residence of the {{ill|Kasanui clan|ja|笠縫氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasanui clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', who migrated from '''Kasanui-mura in Yamato'''.
The '''Man'yōshū poet, [[:en:Takechi no Kurohito|Takechi no Kurohito]]''', wrote: > *"Shihatsu-yama uchi-koe mireba Kasanui no shima kogi-kakuru tana nashi kobune"* > *("Crossing over Mount Shihatsu, I see Kasanui Island, where small boats without roofs disappear.")*
In ancient times, '''Kasanui Island''' was believed to have been an island in an inlet '''stretching from present-day Fukae to [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka, Osaka]] Ashiya'''. The '''Kasanui clan likely lived on the island''' to secure '''materials for making straw hats (笠, kasa)'''. This suggests that '''the original Kasanui-mura in Yamato might also have been a low-lying wetland or an island-like terrain'''{{who|date=June 2024}}.
Even today, '''the straw hats used in the Daijōsai are made in Fukae and presented to the Imperial Household'''. Additionally, '''Fukae has strong historical ties to the casting of the Imperial Divine Mirror (御神鏡)''', suggesting a connection with '''{{ill|Kagamitsukuri Shrine|ja|鏡作神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagamitsukuri Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鏡作坐天照御魂神社|1=Kagamitsukuri nimasu amateru mitama-jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q38278496}}''' in '''Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, Yao'''.
== See Also == * {{ill|Motoise|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motoise|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} * [[:en:Kasanui Station|Kasanui Station]]
