[[ja:笠縫邑]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Osaka Prefecture]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11599947}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:笠縫邑]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11599947]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11599947]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''{{ill|Kasanuimura|lt=Kasanuimura|lt=Kasanui-mura|ja|笠縫邑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasanui-mura|qq=}}''' (かさぬいむら, かさぬいのむら) is the place where '''[[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]''' was '''transferred from the imperial palace in the 6th year of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Suijin]]'s reign''' and entrusted to '''{{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}''' for enshrinement. At the same time, '''[[:en:Yamato Okunitama|Yamato Ōkunidama no Kami]]''', who was also removed from the imperial palace, was entrusted to '''{{ill|Nunakiirihime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入媛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunakiirihime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 渟名城入姫命|1=Princess Nunakiirihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561867}}''' and later enshrined at '''[[:en:Ōyamato Shrine|Ōyamato Shrine]]'''.
== Overview == There is a theory that '''Kasanui-mura is related to the origins of the [[:en:Daijosai|Daijosai]] and {{ill|Toyoakari no Sechie|ja|豊明節会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoakari no Sechie|1=Toyo no Akari no Sechie|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633732}}'''. According to the '''25th year of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Suinin]]'s reign''', '''Princess [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]''' enshrined '''Amaterasu''' at '''{{ill|Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto|ja|磯城の厳橿の本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''. The '''Ihokashi tree''' was considered a '''sacred tree for divine spirits''', and during Emperor Suijin's reign, the '''divine pillar of {{ill|Isokashiro Shrine|ja|磯堅城の神籬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isokashiro Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' was erected ('''Suijin Chronicles, 6th year'''){{who|date=June 2024}}. In '''[[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]]''', it is referred to as '''"Ishiki no Himorogi"''', suggesting that '''"Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto" and "Isokashiro no Himorogi"''' might be the same place{{who|date=June 2024}}.
=== Possible Locations=== There are various theories about the '''location of Kasanui-mura''', including: * '''{{ill|Hibara Shrine (Nara)|ja|檜原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibara Shrine (Nara)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大神神社|1=Ōmiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q705542}}''' ([[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]] '''Miwa''') * '''{{ill|Tashinja|ja|多神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tashinja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 多坐弥志理都比古神社|1=Oonimasumishiritsuhiko Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430652}}''' ([[:en:Shiki District, Nara|Shiki District, Nara]] '''Tawaramoto Town, Ta''') * '''{{ill|Kasanui Shrine|ja|笠縫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasanui Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, '''Hazushō''', located in the '''southeastern corner of [[:en:Jinraku-ji|Jinraku-ji]]''') * '''{{ill|Kasayama Kōjinja|ja|笠山荒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasayama Kōjinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Sakurai, '''Kasa''') * '''Hime-Ōji Shrine''' (a subsidiary shrine of Tashinja) * '''{{ill|Shiki Mikenuza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Mikenuza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志貴御縣坐神社|1=Shiki no Miagata Nimasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q63981812}}''' (Sakurai, Kanaya) * '''{{ill|Obu Tenjinsha|ja|小夫天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obu Tenjinsha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Sakurai, Obu) * '''{{ill|Anashizahyozu Shrine|ja|穴師坐兵主神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anashizahyozu Shrine|1=Anashi-Nimasu Hyōzu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597229}}''' (Sakurai, Anashi) * '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Takaichi District, Nara|Takaichi District, Nara]], '''Asuka Village, Asuka''') * '''Haseyamaguchi Shrine''' (Sakurai, Hase-Tejikarao)
Some shrines, such as '''Hibara Shrine, Kasayama Kōjinja, and Obu Tenjinsha''', are '''located deep in the mountains'''. Additionally, the name '''Jinraku (秦楽)''', associated with Kasanui Shrine, is also found '''in the plains below the front part of the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun|ja|築山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Yamatotakada, Nara|Yamatotakada, Nara]]''', where it was historically written as '''"神楽" (Jinraku/Kagura)'''. This could suggest some connection.
From '''Tashinja''', looking in the direction of '''Yatsugikamino Mikoto Shrine (矢継ぎ神命神社)''' (dedicated to '''Tenmyō Toyotama no Mikoto'''), one faces the '''Jinraku and Tsukiyama Kofun direction'''. However, due to '''Yatsugi no Mori (矢継ぎの森)''', a sacred grove preserved by locals, '''the view is blocked'''.
It is also speculated that '''in ancient times, the view from Tashinja to Hime-Ōji Shrine may have included the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashihaka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 箸墓古墳|1=Hashihaka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603420}}'''. Originally, '''Hime-Ōji Shrine was located to the north (upper seat) of Tashinja''', but was possibly '''relocated southward (lower seat)'''.
If '''Tashinja is indeed the site of Kasanui-mura''', then these kofun formations might correspond to '''the origins of the [[:en:Daijosai|Daijosai]]'s {{ill|Yuki-den|ja|悠紀殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuki-den|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (which has '''cut-inward 千木 [Chigi] like {{ill|Kōtai Jingu|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''') and '''Shuki-den (主基殿)''' (which has '''cut-outward Chigi like {{ill|Toyoukedai Jingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoukedai Jingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}'''){{r|Heisei Daijōsai/p182}}.
== Kasanui Island in Osaka == At '''{{ill|Fukae Inari Shrine|ja|深江稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukae Inari Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Higashinari-ku, Osaka|ja|大阪市 東成区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinari-ku, Osaka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} '''Fukaeminami'''), there is a tradition that '''Fukae was once the residence of the {{ill|Kasanui clan|ja|笠縫氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasanui clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', who migrated from '''Kasanui-mura in Yamato'''.
The '''Man'yōshū poet, [[:en:Takechi no Kurohito|Takechi no Kurohito]]''', wrote: > *"Shihatsu-yama uchi-koe mireba Kasanui no shima kogi-kakuru tana nashi kobune"* > *("Crossing over Mount Shihatsu, I see Kasanui Island, where small boats without roofs disappear.")*
In ancient times, '''Kasanui Island''' was believed to have been an island in an inlet '''stretching from present-day Fukae to [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka, Osaka]] Ashiya'''. The '''Kasanui clan likely lived on the island''' to secure '''materials for making straw hats (笠, kasa)'''. This suggests that '''the original Kasanui-mura in Yamato might also have been a low-lying wetland or an island-like terrain'''{{who|date=June 2024}}.
Even today, '''the straw hats used in the Daijōsai are made in Fukae and presented to the Imperial Household'''. Additionally, '''Fukae has strong historical ties to the casting of the Imperial Divine Mirror (御神鏡)''', suggesting a connection with '''{{ill|Kagamitsukuri Shrine|ja|鏡作神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagamitsukuri Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鏡作坐天照御魂神社|1=Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q38278496}}''' in '''Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, Yao'''.

[[ja:笠縫邑]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Osaka Prefecture]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11599947}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:笠縫邑]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11599947]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11599947]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''{{ill|Kasanuimura|lt=Kasanuimura|lt=Kasanui-mura|ja|笠縫邑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasanui-mura|qq=}}''' (かさぬいむら, かさぬいのむら) is the place where '''[[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]''' was '''transferred from the imperial palace in the 6th year of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Suijin]]'s reign''' and entrusted to '''{{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}''' for enshrinement. At the same time, '''[[:en:Yamato Okunitama|Yamato Ōkunidama no Kami]]''', who was also removed from the imperial palace, was entrusted to '''{{ill|Nunakiirihime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入媛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunakiirihime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 渟名城入姫命|1=Princess Nunakiirihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561867}}''' and later enshrined at '''[[:en:Ōyamato Shrine|Ōyamato Shrine]]'''.
== Overview == There is a theory that '''Kasanui-mura is related to the origins of the [[:en:Daijosai|Daijosai]] and {{ill|Toyoakari no Sechie|ja|豊明節会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoakari no Sechie|1=Toyo no Akari no Sechie|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633732}}'''. According to the '''25th year of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Suinin]]'s reign''', '''Princess [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]''' enshrined '''Amaterasu''' at '''{{ill|Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto|ja|磯城の厳橿の本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''. The '''Ihokashi tree''' was considered a '''sacred tree for divine spirits''', and during Emperor Suijin's reign, the '''divine pillar of {{ill|Isokashiro Shrine|ja|磯堅城の神籬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isokashiro Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' was erected ('''Suijin Chronicles, 6th year'''){{who|date=June 2024}}. In '''[[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]]''', it is referred to as '''"Ishiki no Himorogi"''', suggesting that '''"Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto" and "Isokashiro no Himorogi"''' might be the same place{{who|date=June 2024}}.
=== Possible Locations=== There are various theories about the '''location of Kasanui-mura''', including: * '''{{ill|Hibara Shrine (Nara)|ja|檜原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibara Shrine (Nara)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大神神社|1=Ōmiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q705542}}''' ([[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]] '''Miwa''') * '''{{ill|Tashinja|ja|多神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tashinja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 多坐弥志理都比古神社|1=Oonimasumishiritsuhiko Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430652}}''' ([[:en:Shiki District, Nara|Shiki District, Nara]] '''Tawaramoto Town, Ta''') * '''{{ill|Kasanui Shrine|ja|笠縫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasanui Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, '''Hazushō''', located in the '''southeastern corner of [[:en:Jinraku-ji|Jinraku-ji]]''') * '''{{ill|Kasayama Kōjinja|ja|笠山荒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasayama Kōjinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Sakurai, '''Kasa''') * '''Hime-Ōji Shrine''' (a subsidiary shrine of Tashinja) * '''{{ill|Shiki Mikenuza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Mikenuza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志貴御縣坐神社|1=Shiki no Miagata Nimasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q63981812}}''' (Sakurai, Kanaya) * '''{{ill|Obu Tenjinsha|ja|小夫天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obu Tenjinsha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Sakurai, Obu) * '''{{ill|Anashizahyozu Shrine|ja|穴師坐兵主神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anashizahyozu Shrine|1=Anashi-Nimasu Hyōzu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597229}}''' (Sakurai, Anashi) * '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Takaichi District, Nara|Takaichi District, Nara]], '''Asuka Village, Asuka''') * '''Haseyamaguchi Shrine''' (Sakurai, Hase-Tejikarao)
Some shrines, such as '''Hibara Shrine, Kasayama Kōjinja, and Obu Tenjinsha''', are '''located deep in the mountains'''. Additionally, the name '''Jinraku (秦楽)''', associated with Kasanui Shrine, is also found '''in the plains below the front part of the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun|ja|築山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Yamatotakada, Nara|Yamatotakada, Nara]]''', where it was historically written as '''"神楽" (Jinraku/Kagura)'''. This could suggest some connection.
From '''Tashinja''', looking in the direction of '''Yatsugikamino Mikoto Shrine (矢継ぎ神命神社)''' (dedicated to '''Tenmyō Toyotama no Mikoto'''), one faces the '''Jinraku and Tsukiyama Kofun direction'''. However, due to '''Yatsugi no Mori (矢継ぎの森)''', a sacred grove preserved by locals, '''the view is blocked'''.
It is also speculated that '''in ancient times, the view from Tashinja to Hime-Ōji Shrine may have included the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashihaka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 箸墓古墳|1=Hashihaka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603420}}'''. Originally, '''Hime-Ōji Shrine was located to the north (upper seat) of Tashinja''', but was possibly '''relocated southward (lower seat)'''.
If '''Tashinja is indeed the site of Kasanui-mura''', then these kofun formations might correspond to '''the origins of the [[:en:Daijosai|Daijosai]]'s {{ill|Yuki-den|ja|悠紀殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuki-den|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (which has '''cut-inward 千木 [Chigi] like {{ill|Kōtai Jingu|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''') and '''Shuki-den (主基殿)''' (which has '''cut-outward Chigi like {{ill|Toyoukedai Jingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoukedai Jingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}'''){{r|Heisei Daijōsai/p182}}.
== Kasanui Island in Osaka == At '''{{ill|Fukae Inari Shrine|ja|深江稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukae Inari Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Higashinari-ku, Osaka|ja|大阪市 東成区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinari-ku, Osaka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} '''Fukaeminami'''), there is a tradition that '''Fukae was once the residence of the {{ill|Kasanui clan|ja|笠縫氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasanui clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', who migrated from '''Kasanui-mura in Yamato'''.
The '''Man'yōshū poet, [[:en:Takechi no Kurohito|Takechi no Kurohito]]''', wrote: > *"Shihatsu-yama uchi-koe mireba Kasanui no shima kogi-kakuru tana nashi kobune"* > *("Crossing over Mount Shihatsu, I see Kasanui Island, where small boats without roofs disappear.")*
In ancient times, '''Kasanui Island''' was believed to have been an island in an inlet '''stretching from present-day Fukae to [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka, Osaka]] Ashiya'''. The '''Kasanui clan likely lived on the island''' to secure '''materials for making straw hats (笠, kasa)'''. This suggests that '''the original Kasanui-mura in Yamato might also have been a low-lying wetland or an island-like terrain'''{{who|date=June 2024}}.
Even today, '''the straw hats used in the Daijōsai are made in Fukae and presented to the Imperial Household'''. Additionally, '''Fukae has strong historical ties to the casting of the Imperial Divine Mirror (御神鏡)''', suggesting a connection with '''{{ill|Kagamitsukuri Shrine|ja|鏡作神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagamitsukuri Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鏡作坐天照御魂神社|1=Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q38278496}}''' in '''Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, Yao'''.
== See Also == * {{ill|Motoise|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motoise|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} * [[:en:Kasanui Station|Kasanui Station]]
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="Heisei Daijōsai/p182">{{Citation|和書 | last = Kamata | first = Junichi | author-link = 鎌田純一 | title = Heisei Daijōsai Yōwa: Sokui-rei & Daijōsai | publisher = {{ill|Kinsei-sha|ja|錦正社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinsei-sha|1=Kinsei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650455}} | date = July 2003 | page = 182 | id = {{NDL|20452668}} | isbn = 4-7646-0262-8 }}</ref> }}
{{translated page|ja|笠縫邑|version=101402403|comment=Imported full ja history}}

The '''Man'yōshū poet, [[:en:Takechi no Kurohito|Takechi no Kurohito]]''', wrote: > *"Shihatsu-yama uchi-koe mireba Kasanui no shima kogi-kakuru tana nashi kobune"* > *("Crossing over Mount Shihatsu, I see Kasanui Island, where small boats without roofs disappear.")*
In ancient times, '''Kasanui Island''' was believed to have been an island in an inlet '''stretching from present-day Fukae to [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka, Osaka]] Ashiya'''. The '''Kasanui clan likely lived on the island''' to secure '''materials for making straw hats (笠, kasa)'''. This suggests that '''the original Kasanui-mura in Yamato might also have been a low-lying wetland or an island-like terrain'''{{who|date=June 2024}}.
Even today, '''the straw hats used in the Daijōsai are made in Fukae and presented to the Imperial Household'''. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:笠縫邑]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:笠縫邑]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:笠縫邑]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11599947]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11599947]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''{{ill|Kasanuimura|lt=Kasanuimura|lt=Kasanui-mura|ja|笠縫邑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasanui-mura|qq=}}''' (かさぬいむら, かさぬいのむら) is the place where '''[[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]''' was '''transferred from the imperial palace in the 6th year of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Suijin]]'s reign''' and entrusted to '''{{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}''' for enshrinement. At the same time, '''[[:en:Yamato Okunitama|Yamato Ōkunidama no Kami]]''', who was also removed from the imperial palace, was entrusted to '''{{ill|Nunakiirihime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入媛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunakiirihime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 渟名城入姫命|1=Princess Nunakiirihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561867}}''' and later enshrined at '''[[:en:Ōyamato Shrine|Ōyamato Shrine]]'''.
== Overview == There is a theory that '''Kasanui-mura is related to the origins of the [[:en:Daijosai|Daijosai]] and {{ill|Toyoakari no Sechie|ja|豊明節会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoakari no Sechie|1=Toyo no Akari no Sechie|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633732}}'''. According to the '''25th year of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Suinin]]'s reign''', '''Princess [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]''' enshrined '''Amaterasu''' at '''{{ill|Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto|ja|磯城の厳橿の本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''. The '''Ihokashi tree''' was considered a '''sacred tree for divine spirits''', and during Emperor Suijin's reign, the '''divine pillar of {{ill|Isokashiro Shrine|ja|磯堅城の神籬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isokashiro Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' was erected ('''Suijin Chronicles, 6th year'''){{who|date=June 2024}}. In '''[[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]]''', it is referred to as '''"Ishiki no Himorogi"''', suggesting that '''"Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto" and "Isokashiro no Himorogi"''' might be the same place{{who|date=June 2024}}.
=== Possible Locations=== There are various theories about the '''location of Kasanui-mura''', including: * '''{{ill|Hibara Shrine (Nara)|ja|檜原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibara Shrine (Nara)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大神神社|1=Ōmiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q705542}}''' ([[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]] '''Miwa''') * '''{{ill|Tashinja|ja|多神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tashinja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 多坐弥志理都比古神社|1=Oonimasumishiritsuhiko Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430652}}''' ([[:en:Shiki District, Nara|Shiki District, Nara]] '''Tawaramoto Town, Ta''') * '''{{ill|Kasanui Shrine|ja|笠縫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasanui Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, '''Hazushō''', located in the '''southeastern corner of [[:en:Jinraku-ji|Jinraku-ji]]''') * '''{{ill|Kasayama Kōjinja|ja|笠山荒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasayama Kōjinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Sakurai, '''Kasa''') * '''Hime-Ōji Shrine''' (a subsidiary shrine of Tashinja) * '''{{ill|Shiki Mikenuza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Mikenuza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志貴御縣坐神社|1=Shiki no Miagata Nimasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q63981812}}''' (Sakurai, Kanaya) * '''{{ill|Obu Tenjinsha|ja|小夫天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obu Tenjinsha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Sakurai, Obu) * '''{{ill|Anashizahyozu Shrine|ja|穴師坐兵主神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anashizahyozu Shrine|1=Anashi-Nimasu Hyōzu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597229}}''' (Sakurai, Anashi) * '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Takaichi District, Nara|Takaichi District, Nara]], '''Asuka Village, Asuka''') * '''Haseyamaguchi Shrine''' (Sakurai, Hase-Tejikarao)
Some shrines, such as '''Hibara Shrine, Kasayama Kōjinja, and Obu Tenjinsha''', are '''located deep in the mountains'''. Additionally, the name '''Jinraku (秦楽)''', associated with Kasanui Shrine, is also found '''in the plains below the front part of the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun|ja|築山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Yamatotakada, Nara|Yamatotakada, Nara]]''', where it was historically written as '''"神楽" (Jinraku/Kagura)'''. This could suggest some connection.
From '''Tashinja''', looking in the direction of '''Yatsugikamino Mikoto Shrine (矢継ぎ神命神社)''' (dedicated to '''Tenmyō Toyotama no Mikoto'''), one faces the '''Jinraku and Tsukiyama Kofun direction'''. However, due to '''Yatsugi no Mori (矢継ぎの森)''', a sacred grove preserved by locals, '''the view is blocked'''.
It is also speculated that '''in ancient times, the view from Tashinja to Hime-Ōji Shrine may have included the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashihaka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 箸墓古墳|1=Hashihaka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603420}}'''. Originally, '''Hime-Ōji Shrine was located to the north (upper seat) of Tashinja''', but was possibly '''relocated southward (lower seat)'''.
If '''Tashinja is indeed the site of Kasanui-mura''', then these kofun formations might correspond to '''the origins of the [[:en:Daijosai|Daijosai]]'s {{ill|Yuki-den|ja|悠紀殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuki-den|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (which has '''cut-inward 千木 [Chigi] like {{ill|Kōtai Jingu|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''') and '''Shuki-den (主基殿)''' (which has '''cut-outward Chigi like {{ill|Toyoukedai Jingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoukedai Jingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}'''){{r|Heisei Daijōsai/p182}}.
== Kasanui Island in Osaka == At '''{{ill|Fukae Inari Shrine|ja|深江稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukae Inari Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Higashinari-ku, Osaka|ja|大阪市 東成区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinari-ku, Osaka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} '''Fukaeminami'''), there is a tradition that '''Fukae was once the residence of the {{ill|Kasanui clan|ja|笠縫氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasanui clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', who migrated from '''Kasanui-mura in Yamato'''.
The '''Man'yōshū poet, [[:en:Takechi no Kurohito|Takechi no Kurohito]]''', wrote: > *"Shihatsu-yama uchi-koe mireba Kasanui no shima kogi-kakuru tana nashi kobune"* > *("Crossing over Mount Shihatsu, I see Kasanui Island, where small boats without roofs disappear.")*
In ancient times, '''Kasanui Island''' was believed to have been an island in an inlet '''stretching from present-day Fukae to [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka, Osaka]] Ashiya'''. The '''Kasanui clan likely lived on the island''' to secure '''materials for making straw hats (笠, kasa)'''. This suggests that '''the original Kasanui-mura in Yamato might also have been a low-lying wetland or an island-like terrain'''{{who|date=June 2024}}.
Even today, '''the straw hats used in the Daijōsai are made in Fukae and presented to the Imperial Household'''. Additionally, '''Fukae has strong historical ties to the casting of the Imperial Divine Mirror (御神鏡)''', suggesting a connection with '''{{ill|Kagamitsukuri Shrine|ja|鏡作神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagamitsukuri Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鏡作坐天照御魂神社|1=Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q38278496}}''' in '''Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, Yao'''.
== See Also == * {{ill|Motoise|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motoise|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} * [[:en:Kasanui Station|Kasanui Station]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:笠縫邑]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:笠縫邑]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:笠縫邑]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:笠縫邑]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11599947]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11599947]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''{{ill|Kasanuimura|lt=Kasanuimura|lt=Kasanui-mura|ja|笠縫邑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasanui-mura|qq=}}''' (かさぬいむら, かさぬいのむら) is the place where '''[[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]''' was '''transferred from the imperial palace in the 6th year of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Suijin]]'s reign''' and entrusted to '''{{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}''' for enshrinement. At the same time, '''[[:en:Yamato Okunitama|Yamato Ōkunidama no Kami]]''', who was also removed from the imperial palace, was entrusted to '''{{ill|Nunakiirihime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入媛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunakiirihime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 渟名城入姫命|1=Princess Nunakiirihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561867}}''' and later enshrined at '''[[:en:Ōyamato Shrine|Ōyamato Shrine]]'''.
== Overview == There is a theory that '''Kasanui-mura is related to the origins of the [[:en:Daijosai|Daijosai]] and {{ill|Toyoakari no Sechie|ja|豊明節会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoakari no Sechie|1=Toyo no Akari no Sechie|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633732}}'''. According to the '''25th year of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Suinin]]'s reign''', '''Princess [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]''' enshrined '''Amaterasu''' at '''{{ill|Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto|ja|磯城の厳橿の本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''. The '''Ihokashi tree''' was considered a '''sacred tree for divine spirits''', and during Emperor Suijin's reign, the '''divine pillar of {{ill|Isokashiro Shrine|ja|磯堅城の神籬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isokashiro Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' was erected ('''Suijin Chronicles, 6th year'''){{who|date=June 2024}}. In '''[[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]]''', it is referred to as '''"Ishiki no Himorogi"''', suggesting that '''"Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto" and "Isokashiro no Himorogi"''' might be the same place{{who|date=June 2024}}.
=== Possible Locations=== There are various theories about the '''location of Kasanui-mura''', including: * '''{{ill|Hibara Shrine (Nara)|ja|檜原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibara Shrine (Nara)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大神神社|1=Ōmiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q705542}}''' ([[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]] '''Miwa''') * '''{{ill|Tashinja|ja|多神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tashinja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 多坐弥志理都比古神社|1=Oonimasumishiritsuhiko Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430652}}''' ([[:en:Shiki District, Nara|Shiki District, Nara]] '''Tawaramoto Town, Ta''') * '''{{ill|Kasanui Shrine|ja|笠縫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasanui Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, '''Hazushō''', located in the '''southeastern corner of [[:en:Jinraku-ji|Jinraku-ji]]''') * '''{{ill|Kasayama Kōjinja|ja|笠山荒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasayama Kōjinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Sakurai, '''Kasa''') * '''Hime-Ōji Shrine''' (a subsidiary shrine of Tashinja) * '''{{ill|Shiki Mikenuza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Mikenuza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志貴御縣坐神社|1=Shiki no Miagata Nimasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q63981812}}''' (Sakurai, Kanaya) * '''{{ill|Obu Tenjinsha|ja|小夫天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obu Tenjinsha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Sakurai, Obu) * '''{{ill|Anashizahyozu Shrine|ja|穴師坐兵主神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anashizahyozu Shrine|1=Anashi-Nimasu Hyōzu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597229}}''' (Sakurai, Anashi) * '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Takaichi District, Nara|Takaichi District, Nara]], '''Asuka Village, Asuka''') * '''Haseyamaguchi Shrine''' (Sakurai, Hase-Tejikarao)
Some shrines, such as '''Hibara Shrine, Kasayama Kōjinja, and Obu Tenjinsha''', are '''located deep in the mountains'''. Additionally, the name '''Jinraku (秦楽)''', associated with Kasanui Shrine, is also found '''in the plains below the front part of the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun|ja|築山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Yamatotakada, Nara|Yamatotakada, Nara]]''', where it was historically written as '''"神楽" (Jinraku/Kagura)'''. This could suggest some connection.
From '''Tashinja''', looking in the direction of '''Yatsugikamino Mikoto Shrine (矢継ぎ神命神社)''' (dedicated to '''Tenmyō Toyotama no Mikoto'''), one faces the '''Jinraku and Tsukiyama Kofun direction'''. However, due to '''Yatsugi no Mori (矢継ぎの森)''', a sacred grove preserved by locals, '''the view is blocked'''.
It is also speculated that '''in ancient times, the view from Tashinja to Hime-Ōji Shrine may have included the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashihaka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 箸墓古墳|1=Hashihaka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603420}}'''. Originally, '''Hime-Ōji Shrine was located to the north (upper seat) of Tashinja''', but was possibly '''relocated southward (lower seat)'''.
If '''Tashinja is indeed the site of Kasanui-mura''', then these kofun formations might correspond to '''the origins of the [[:en:Daijosai|Daijosai]]'s {{ill|Yuki-den|ja|悠紀殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuki-den|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (which has '''cut-inward 千木 [Chigi] like {{ill|Kōtai Jingu|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''') and '''Shuki-den (主基殿)''' (which has '''cut-outward Chigi like {{ill|Toyoukedai Jingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoukedai Jingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}'''){{r|Heisei Daijōsai/p182}}.
== Kasanui Island in Osaka == At '''{{ill|Fukae Inari Shrine|ja|深江稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukae Inari Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Higashinari-ku, Osaka|ja|大阪市 東成区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinari-ku, Osaka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} '''Fukaeminami'''), there is a tradition that '''Fukae was once the residence of the {{ill|Kasanui clan|ja|笠縫氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasanui clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', who migrated from '''Kasanui-mura in Yamato'''.
The '''Man'yōshū poet, [[:en:Takechi no Kurohito|Takechi no Kurohito]]''', wrote: > *"Shihatsu-yama uchi-koe mireba Kasanui no shima kogi-kakuru tana nashi kobune"* > *("Crossing over Mount Shihatsu, I see Kasanui Island, where small boats without roofs disappear.")*
In ancient times, '''Kasanui Island''' was believed to have been an island in an inlet '''stretching from present-day Fukae to [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka, Osaka]] Ashiya'''. The '''Kasanui clan likely lived on the island''' to secure '''materials for making straw hats (笠, kasa)'''. This suggests that '''the original Kasanui-mura in Yamato might also have been a low-lying wetland or an island-like terrain'''{{who|date=June 2024}}.
Even today, '''the straw hats used in the Daijōsai are made in Fukae and presented to the Imperial Household'''. Additionally, '''Fukae has strong historical ties to the casting of the Imperial Divine Mirror (御神鏡)''', suggesting a connection with '''{{ill|Kagamitsukuri Shrine|ja|鏡作神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagamitsukuri Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鏡作坐天照御魂神社|1=Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q38278496}}''' in '''Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, Yao'''.
== See Also == * {{ill|Motoise|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motoise|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} * [[:en:Kasanui Station|Kasanui Station]]
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="Heisei Daijōsai/p182">{{Citation|和書 | last = Kamata | first = Junichi | author-link = 鎌田純一 | title = Heisei Daijōsai Yōwa: Sokui-rei & Daijōsai | publisher = {{ill|Kinsei-sha|ja|錦正社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinsei-sha|1=Kinsei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650455}} | date = July 2003 | page = 182 | id = {{NDL|20452668}} | isbn = 4-7646-0262-8 }}</ref> }}
{{translated page|ja|笠縫邑|version=101402403|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:笠縫邑]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:笠縫邑]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:笠縫邑]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:笠縫邑]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11599947]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11599947]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''{{ill|Kasanuimura|lt=Kasanuimura|lt=Kasanui-mura|ja|笠縫邑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasanui-mura|qq=}}''' (かさぬいむら, かさぬいのむら) is the place where '''[[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]''' was '''transferred from the imperial palace in the 6th year of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Suijin]]'s reign''' and entrusted to '''{{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}''' for enshrinement. At the same time, '''[[:en:Yamato Okunitama|Yamato Ōkunidama no Kami]]''', who was also removed from the imperial palace, was entrusted to '''{{ill|Nunakiirihime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入媛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunakiirihime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 渟名城入姫命|1=Princess Nunakiirihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561867}}''' and later enshrined at '''[[:en:Ōyamato Shrine|Ōyamato Shrine]]'''.
== Overview == There is a theory that '''Kasanui-mura is related to the origins of the [[:en:Daijosai|Daijosai]] and {{ill|Toyoakari no Sechie|ja|豊明節会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoakari no Sechie|1=Toyo no Akari no Sechie|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633732}}'''. According to the '''25th year of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Suinin]]'s reign''', '''Princess [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]''' enshrined '''Amaterasu''' at '''{{ill|Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto|ja|磯城の厳橿の本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''. The '''Ihokashi tree''' was considered a '''sacred tree for divine spirits''', and during Emperor Suijin's reign, the '''divine pillar of {{ill|Isokashiro Shrine|ja|磯堅城の神籬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isokashiro Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' was erected ('''Suijin Chronicles, 6th year'''){{who|date=June 2024}}. In '''[[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]]''', it is referred to as '''"Ishiki no Himorogi"''', suggesting that '''"Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto" and "Isokashiro no Himorogi"''' might be the same place{{who|date=June 2024}}.
=== Possible Locations=== There are various theories about the '''location of Kasanui-mura''', including: * '''{{ill|Hibara Shrine (Nara)|ja|檜原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibara Shrine (Nara)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大神神社|1=Ōmiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q705542}}''' ([[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]] '''Miwa''') * '''{{ill|Tashinja|ja|多神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tashinja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 多坐弥志理都比古神社|1=Oonimasumishiritsuhiko Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430652}}''' ([[:en:Shiki District, Nara|Shiki District, Nara]] '''Tawaramoto Town, Ta''') * '''{{ill|Kasanui Shrine|ja|笠縫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasanui Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, '''Hazushō''', located in the '''southeastern corner of [[:en:Jinraku-ji|Jinraku-ji]]''') * '''{{ill|Kasayama Kōjinja|ja|笠山荒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasayama Kōjinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Sakurai, '''Kasa''') * '''Hime-Ōji Shrine''' (a subsidiary shrine of Tashinja) * '''{{ill|Shiki Mikenuza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Mikenuza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志貴御縣坐神社|1=Shiki no Miagata Nimasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q63981812}}''' (Sakurai, Kanaya) * '''{{ill|Obu Tenjinsha|ja|小夫天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obu Tenjinsha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Sakurai, Obu) * '''{{ill|Anashizahyozu Shrine|ja|穴師坐兵主神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anashizahyozu Shrine|1=Anashi-Nimasu Hyōzu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597229}}''' (Sakurai, Anashi) * '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Takaichi District, Nara|Takaichi District, Nara]], '''Asuka Village, Asuka''') * '''Haseyamaguchi Shrine''' (Sakurai, Hase-Tejikarao)
Some shrines, such as '''Hibara Shrine, Kasayama Kōjinja, and Obu Tenjinsha''', are '''located deep in the mountains'''. Additionally, the name '''Jinraku (秦楽)''', associated with Kasanui Shrine, is also found '''in the plains below the front part of the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun|ja|築山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Yamatotakada, Nara|Yamatotakada, Nara]]''', where it was historically written as '''"神楽" (Jinraku/Kagura)'''. This could suggest some connection.
From '''Tashinja''', looking in the direction of '''Yatsugikamino Mikoto Shrine (矢継ぎ神命神社)''' (dedicated to '''Tenmyō Toyotama no Mikoto'''), one faces the '''Jinraku and Tsukiyama Kofun direction'''. However, due to '''Yatsugi no Mori (矢継ぎの森)''', a sacred grove preserved by locals, '''the view is blocked'''.
It is also speculated that '''in ancient times, the view from Tashinja to Hime-Ōji Shrine may have included the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashihaka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 箸墓古墳|1=Hashihaka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603420}}'''. Originally, '''Hime-Ōji Shrine was located to the north (upper seat) of Tashinja''', but was possibly '''relocated southward (lower seat)'''.
If '''Tashinja is indeed the site of Kasanui-mura''', then these kofun formations might correspond to '''the origins of the [[:en:Daijosai|Daijosai]]'s {{ill|Yuki-den|ja|悠紀殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuki-den|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (which has '''cut-inward 千木 [Chigi] like {{ill|Kōtai Jingu|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''') and '''Shuki-den (主基殿)''' (which has '''cut-outward Chigi like {{ill|Toyoukedai Jingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoukedai Jingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}'''){{r|Heisei Daijōsai/p182}}.
== Kasanui Island in Osaka == At '''{{ill|Fukae Inari Shrine|ja|深江稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukae Inari Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Higashinari-ku, Osaka|ja|大阪市 東成区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinari-ku, Osaka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} '''Fukaeminami'''), there is a tradition that '''Fukae was once the residence of the {{ill|Kasanui clan|ja|笠縫氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasanui clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', who migrated from '''Kasanui-mura in Yamato'''.
The '''Man'yōshū poet, [[:en:Takechi no Kurohito|Takechi no Kurohito]]''', wrote: > *"Shihatsu-yama uchi-koe mireba Kasanui no shima kogi-kakuru tana nashi kobune"* > *("Crossing over Mount Shihatsu, I see Kasanui Island, where small boats without roofs disappear.")*
In ancient times, '''Kasanui Island''' was believed to have been an island in an inlet '''stretching from present-day Fukae to [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka, Osaka]] Ashiya'''. The '''Kasanui clan likely lived on the island''' to secure '''materials for making straw hats (笠, kasa)'''. This suggests that '''the original Kasanui-mura in Yamato might also have been a low-lying wetland or an island-like terrain'''{{who|date=June 2024}}.
Even today, '''the straw hats used in the Daijōsai are made in Fukae and presented to the Imperial Household'''. Additionally, '''Fukae has strong historical ties to the casting of the Imperial Divine Mirror (御神鏡)''', suggesting a connection with '''{{ill|Kagamitsukuri Shrine|ja|鏡作神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagamitsukuri Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鏡作坐天照御魂神社|1=Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q38278496}}''' in '''Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, Yao'''.
== See Also == * {{ill|Motoise|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motoise|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} * [[:en:Kasanui Station|Kasanui Station]]
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="Heisei Daijōsai/p182">{{Citation|和書 | last = Kamata | first = Junichi | author-link = 鎌田純一 | title = Heisei Daijōsai Yōwa: Sokui-rei & Daijōsai | publisher = {{ill|Kinsei-sha|ja|錦正社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinsei-sha|1=Kinsei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650455}} | date = July 2003 | page = 182 | id = {{NDL|20452668}} | isbn = 4-7646-0262-8 }}</ref> }}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:笠縫邑]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:笠縫邑]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:笠縫邑]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:笠縫邑]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11599947]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11599947]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''{{ill|Kasanuimura|lt=Kasanuimura|lt=Kasanui-mura|ja|笠縫邑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasanui-mura|qq=}}''' (かさぬいむら, かさぬいのむら) is the place where '''{{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}''' was '''transferred from the imperial palace in the 6th year of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Suijin}}'s reign''' and entrusted to '''{{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}''' for enshrinement. At the same time, '''{{ill|Yamato Okunitama|en|Yamato Okunitama|WD=Q11387080|lt=Yamato Ōkunidama no Kami}}''', who was also removed from the imperial palace, was entrusted to '''{{ill|Nunakiirihime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入媛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunakiirihime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 渟名城入姫命|1=Princess Nunakiirihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561867}}''' and later enshrined at '''{{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|en|Ōyamato Shrine|WD=Q245731|lt=Ōyamato Shrine}}'''.
== Overview == There is a theory that '''Kasanui-mura is related to the origins of the {{ill|Daijosai|en|Daijosai|WD=Q11433653|lt=Daijosai}} and {{ill|Toyoakari no Sechie|ja|豊明節会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoakari no Sechie|1=Toyo no Akari no Sechie|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633732}}'''. According to the '''25th year of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Suinin}}'s reign''', '''Princess {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}''' enshrined '''Amaterasu''' at '''{{ill|Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto|ja|磯城の厳橿の本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''. The '''Ihokashi tree''' was considered a '''sacred tree for divine spirits''', and during Emperor Suijin's reign, the '''divine pillar of {{ill|Isokashiro Shrine|ja|磯堅城の神籬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isokashiro Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' was erected ('''Suijin Chronicles, 6th year'''){{who|date=June 2024}}. In '''{{ill|Kogo Shūi|en|Kogo Shūi|WD=Q531555|lt=Kogo Shūi}}''', it is referred to as '''"Ishiki no Himorogi"''', suggesting that '''"Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto" and "Isokashiro no Himorogi"''' might be the same place{{who|date=June 2024}}.
=== Possible Locations=== There are various theories about the '''location of Kasanui-mura''', including: * '''{{ill|Hibara Shrine (Nara)|ja|檜原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibara Shrine (Nara)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大神神社|1=Ōmiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q705542}}''' ({{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|Sakurai, Nara|WD=Q127026|lt=Sakurai, Nara}} '''Miwa''') * '''{{ill|Tashinja|ja|多神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tashinja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 多坐弥志理都比古神社|1=Oonimasumishiritsuhiko Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430652}}''' ({{ill|Shiki District, Nara|en|Shiki District, Nara|WD=Q1155036|lt=Shiki District, Nara}} '''Tawaramoto Town, Ta''') * '''{{ill|Kasanui Shrine|ja|笠縫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasanui Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, '''Hazushō''', located in the '''southeastern corner of {{ill|Jinraku-ji|en|Jinraku-ji|WD=Q3179237|lt=Jinraku-ji}}''') * '''{{ill|Kasayama Kōjinja|ja|笠山荒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasayama Kōjinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Sakurai, '''Kasa''') * '''Hime-Ōji Shrine''' (a subsidiary shrine of Tashinja) * '''{{ill|Shiki Mikenuza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Mikenuza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志貴御縣坐神社|1=Shiki no Miagata Nimasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q63981812}}''' (Sakurai, Kanaya) * '''{{ill|Obu Tenjinsha|ja|小夫天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obu Tenjinsha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Sakurai, Obu) * '''{{ill|Anashizahyozu Shrine|ja|穴師坐兵主神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anashizahyozu Shrine|1=Anashi-Nimasu Hyōzu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597229}}''' (Sakurai, Anashi) * '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Takaichi District, Nara|en|Takaichi District, Nara|WD=Q1117901|lt=Takaichi District, Nara}}, '''Asuka Village, Asuka''') * '''Haseyamaguchi Shrine''' (Sakurai, Hase-Tejikarao)
Some shrines, such as '''Hibara Shrine, Kasayama Kōjinja, and Obu Tenjinsha''', are '''located deep in the mountains'''. Additionally, the name '''Jinraku (秦楽)''', associated with Kasanui Shrine, is also found '''in the plains below the front part of the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun|ja|築山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Yamatotakada, Nara|en|Yamatotakada, Nara|WD=Q838669|lt=Yamatotakada, Nara}}''', where it was historically written as '''"神楽" (Jinraku/Kagura)'''. This could suggest some connection.
From '''Tashinja''', looking in the direction of '''Yatsugikamino Mikoto Shrine (矢継ぎ神命神社)''' (dedicated to '''Tenmyō Toyotama no Mikoto'''), one faces the '''Jinraku and Tsukiyama Kofun direction'''. However, due to '''Yatsugi no Mori (矢継ぎの森)''', a sacred grove preserved by locals, '''the view is blocked'''.
It is also speculated that '''in ancient times, the view from Tashinja to Hime-Ōji Shrine may have included the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashihaka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 箸墓古墳|1=Hashihaka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603420}}'''. Originally, '''Hime-Ōji Shrine was located to the north (upper seat) of Tashinja''', but was possibly '''relocated southward (lower seat)'''.
If '''Tashinja is indeed the site of Kasanui-mura''', then these kofun formations might correspond to '''the origins of the {{ill|Daijosai|en|Daijosai|WD=Q11433653|lt=Daijosai}}'s {{ill|Yuki-den|ja|悠紀殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuki-den|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (which has '''cut-inward 千木 [Chigi] like {{ill|Kōtai Jingu|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''') and '''Shuki-den (主基殿)''' (which has '''cut-outward Chigi like {{ill|Toyoukedai Jingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoukedai Jingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}'''){{r|Heisei Daijōsai/p182}}.
== Kasanui Island in Osaka == At '''{{ill|Fukae Inari Shrine|ja|深江稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukae Inari Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Higashinari-ku, Osaka|ja|大阪市 東成区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinari-ku, Osaka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} '''Fukaeminami'''), there is a tradition that '''Fukae was once the residence of the {{ill|Kasanui clan|ja|笠縫氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasanui clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', who migrated from '''Kasanui-mura in Yamato'''.
The '''Man'yōshū poet, {{ill|Takechi no Kurohito|en|Takechi no Kurohito|WD=Q2389389|lt=Takechi no Kurohito}}''', wrote: > *"Shihatsu-yama uchi-koe mireba Kasanui no shima kogi-kakuru tana nashi kobune"* > *("Crossing over Mount Shihatsu, I see Kasanui Island, where small boats without roofs disappear.")*
In ancient times, '''Kasanui Island''' was believed to have been an island in an inlet '''stretching from present-day Fukae to {{ill|Higashiōsaka|en|Higashiōsaka|WD=Q243863|lt=Higashiōsaka, Osaka}} Ashiya'''. The '''Kasanui clan likely lived on the island''' to secure '''materials for making straw hats (笠, kasa)'''. This suggests that '''the original Kasanui-mura in Yamato might also have been a low-lying wetland or an island-like terrain'''{{who|date=June 2024}}.
Even today, '''the straw hats used in the Daijōsai are made in Fukae and presented to the Imperial Household'''. Additionally, '''Fukae has strong historical ties to the casting of the Imperial Divine Mirror (御神鏡)''', suggesting a connection with '''{{ill|Kagamitsukuri Shrine|ja|鏡作神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagamitsukuri Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鏡作坐天照御魂神社|1=Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q38278496}}''' in '''Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, Yao'''.
== See Also == * {{ill|Motoise|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motoise|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} * {{ill|Kasanui Station|en|Kasanui Station|WD=Q6374013|lt=Kasanui Station}}
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="Heisei Daijōsai/p182">{{Citation|和書 | last = Kamata | first = Junichi | author-link = 鎌田純一 | title = Heisei Daijōsai Yōwa: Sokui-rei & Daijōsai | publisher = {{ill|Kinsei-sha|ja|錦正社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinsei-sha|1=Kinsei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650455}} | date = July 2003 | page = 182 | id = {{NDL|20452668}} | isbn = 4-7646-0262-8 }}</ref> }}
{{translated page|ja|笠縫邑|version=101402403|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:笠縫邑]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:笠縫邑]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:笠縫邑]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:笠縫邑]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:笠縫邑]]
'''{{ill|Kasanuimura|lt=Kasanuimura|lt=Kasanui-mura|ja|笠縫邑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasanui-mura|qq=}}''' (かさぬいむら, かさぬいのむら) is the place where '''{{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}''' was '''transferred from the imperial palace in the 6th year of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Suijin}}'s reign''' and entrusted to '''{{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}''' for enshrinement. At the same time, '''{{ill|Yamato Okunitama|en|Yamato Okunitama|WD=Q11387080|lt=Yamato Ōkunidama no Kami}}''', who was also removed from the imperial palace, was entrusted to '''{{ill|Nunakiirihime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入媛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunakiirihime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 渟名城入姫命|1=Princess Nunakiirihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561867}}''' and later enshrined at '''{{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|en|Ōyamato Shrine|WD=Q245731|lt=Ōyamato Shrine}}'''.
== Overview == There is a theory that '''Kasanui-mura is related to the origins of the {{ill|Daijosai|en|Daijosai|WD=Q11433653|lt=Daijosai}} and {{ill|Toyoakari no Sechie|ja|豊明節会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoakari no Sechie|1=Toyo no Akari no Sechie|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633732}}'''. According to the '''25th year of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Suinin}}'s reign''', '''Princess {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}''' enshrined '''Amaterasu''' at '''{{ill|Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto|ja|磯城の厳橿の本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''. The '''Ihokashi tree''' was considered a '''sacred tree for divine spirits''', and during Emperor Suijin's reign, the '''divine pillar of {{ill|Isokashiro Shrine|ja|磯堅城の神籬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isokashiro Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' was erected ('''Suijin Chronicles, 6th year'''){{who|date=June 2024}}. In '''{{ill|Kogo Shūi|en|Kogo Shūi|WD=Q531555|lt=Kogo Shūi}}''', it is referred to as '''"Ishiki no Himorogi"''', suggesting that '''"Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto" and "Isokashiro no Himorogi"''' might be the same place{{who|date=June 2024}}.
=== Possible Locations=== There are various theories about the '''location of Kasanui-mura''', including: * '''{{ill|Hibara Shrine (Nara)|ja|檜原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibara Shrine (Nara)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大神神社|1=Ōmiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q705542}}''' ({{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|Sakurai, Nara|WD=Q127026|lt=Sakurai, Nara}} '''Miwa''') * '''{{ill|Tashinja|ja|多神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tashinja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 多坐弥志理都比古神社|1=Oonimasumishiritsuhiko Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430652}}''' ({{ill|Shiki District, Nara|en|Shiki District, Nara|WD=Q1155036|lt=Shiki District, Nara}} '''Tawaramoto Town, Ta''') * '''{{ill|Kasanui Shrine|ja|笠縫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasanui Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, '''Hazushō''', located in the '''southeastern corner of {{ill|Jinraku-ji|en|Jinraku-ji|WD=Q3179237|lt=Jinraku-ji}}''') * '''{{ill|Kasayama Kōjinja|ja|笠山荒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasayama Kōjinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Sakurai, '''Kasa''') * '''Hime-Ōji Shrine''' (a subsidiary shrine of Tashinja) * '''{{ill|Shiki Mikenuza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Mikenuza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志貴御縣坐神社|1=Shiki no Miagata Nimasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q63981812}}''' (Sakurai, Kanaya) * '''{{ill|Obu Tenjinsha|ja|小夫天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obu Tenjinsha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Sakurai, Obu) * '''{{ill|Anashizahyozu Shrine|ja|穴師坐兵主神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anashizahyozu Shrine|1=Anashi-Nimasu Hyōzu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597229}}''' (Sakurai, Anashi) * '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Takaichi District, Nara|en|Takaichi District, Nara|WD=Q1117901|lt=Takaichi District, Nara}}, '''Asuka Village, Asuka''') * '''Haseyamaguchi Shrine''' (Sakurai, Hase-Tejikarao)
Some shrines, such as '''Hibara Shrine, Kasayama Kōjinja, and Obu Tenjinsha''', are '''located deep in the mountains'''. Additionally, the name '''Jinraku (秦楽)''', associated with Kasanui Shrine, is also found '''in the plains below the front part of the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun|ja|築山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Yamatotakada, Nara|en|Yamatotakada, Nara|WD=Q838669|lt=Yamatotakada, Nara}}''', where it was historically written as '''"神楽" (Jinraku/Kagura)'''. This could suggest some connection.
From '''Tashinja''', looking in the direction of '''Yatsugikamino Mikoto Shrine (矢継ぎ神命神社)''' (dedicated to '''Tenmyō Toyotama no Mikoto'''), one faces the '''Jinraku and Tsukiyama Kofun direction'''. However, due to '''Yatsugi no Mori (矢継ぎの森)''', a sacred grove preserved by locals, '''the view is blocked'''.
It is also speculated that '''in ancient times, the view from Tashinja to Hime-Ōji Shrine may have included the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashihaka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 箸墓古墳|1=Hashihaka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603420}}'''. Originally, '''Hime-Ōji Shrine was located to the north (upper seat) of Tashinja''', but was possibly '''relocated southward (lower seat)'''.
If '''Tashinja is indeed the site of Kasanui-mura''', then these kofun formations might correspond to '''the origins of the {{ill|Daijosai|en|Daijosai|WD=Q11433653|lt=Daijosai}}'s {{ill|Yuki-den|ja|悠紀殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuki-den|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (which has '''cut-inward 千木 [Chigi] like {{ill|Kōtai Jingu|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''') and '''Shuki-den (主基殿)''' (which has '''cut-outward Chigi like {{ill|Toyoukedai Jingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoukedai Jingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}'''){{r|Heisei Daijōsai/p182}}.
== Kasanui Island in Osaka == At '''{{ill|Fukae Inari Shrine|ja|深江稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukae Inari Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Higashinari-ku, Osaka|ja|大阪市 東成区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinari-ku, Osaka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} '''Fukaeminami'''), there is a tradition that '''Fukae was once the residence of the {{ill|Kasanui clan|ja|笠縫氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasanui clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', who migrated from '''Kasanui-mura in Yamato'''.
The '''Man'yōshū poet, {{ill|Takechi no Kurohito|en|Takechi no Kurohito|WD=Q2389389|lt=Takechi no Kurohito}}''', wrote: > *"Shihatsu-yama uchi-koe mireba Kasanui no shima kogi-kakuru tana nashi kobune"* > *("Crossing over Mount Shihatsu, I see Kasanui Island, where small boats without roofs disappear.")*
In ancient times, '''Kasanui Island''' was believed to have been an island in an inlet '''stretching from present-day Fukae to {{ill|Higashiōsaka|en|Higashiōsaka|WD=Q243863|lt=Higashiōsaka, Osaka}} Ashiya'''. The '''Kasanui clan likely lived on the island''' to secure '''materials for making straw hats (笠, kasa)'''. This suggests that '''the original Kasanui-mura in Yamato might also have been a low-lying wetland or an island-like terrain'''{{who|date=June 2024}}.
Even today, '''the straw hats used in the Daijōsai are made in Fukae and presented to the Imperial Household'''. Additionally, '''Fukae has strong historical ties to the casting of the Imperial Divine Mirror (御神鏡)''', suggesting a connection with '''{{ill|Kagamitsukuri Shrine|ja|鏡作神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagamitsukuri Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鏡作坐天照御魂神社|1=Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q38278496}}''' in '''Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, Yao'''.
== See Also == * {{ill|Motoise|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motoise|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} * {{ill|Kasanui Station|en|Kasanui Station|WD=Q6374013|lt=Kasanui Station}}
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="Heisei Daijōsai/p182">{{Citation|和書 | last = Kamata | first = Junichi | author-link = 鎌田純一 | title = Heisei Daijōsai Yōwa: Sokui-rei & Daijōsai | publisher = {{ill|Kinsei-sha|ja|錦正社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinsei-sha|1=Kinsei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650455}} | date = July 2003 | page = 182 | id = {{NDL|20452668}} | isbn = 4-7646-0262-8 }}</ref> }}
{{translated page|ja|笠縫邑|version=101402403|comment=Imported full ja history}}

If '''Tashinja is indeed the site of Kasanui-mura''', then these kofun formations might correspond to '''the origins of the {{ill|Daijosai|en|Daijosai|WD=Q11433653|lt=Daijosai}}'s {{ill|Yuki-den|ja|悠紀殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuki-den|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (which has '''cut-inward 千木 [Chigi] like {{ill|Kōtai Jingu|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''') and '''Shuki-den (主基殿)''' (which has '''cut-outward Chigi like {{ill|Toyoukedai Jingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoukedai Jingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}'''){{r|Heisei Daijōsai/p182}}.

Even today, '''the straw hats used in the Daijōsai are made in Fukae and presented to the Imperial Household'''. Additionally, '''Fukae has strong historical ties to the casting of the Imperial Divine Mirror (御神鏡)''', suggesting a connection with '''{{ill|Kagamitsukuri Shrine|ja|鏡作神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagamitsukuri Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鏡作坐天照御魂神社|1=Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q38278496}}''' in '''Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, Yao'''.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:笠縫邑]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:笠縫邑]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:笠縫邑]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:笠縫邑]]
'''{{ill|Kasanuimura|lt=Kasanuimura|lt=Kasanui-mura|ja|笠縫邑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasanui-mura|qq=}}''' (かさぬいむら, かさぬいのむら) is the place where '''{{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}''' was '''transferred from the imperial palace in the 6th year of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Suijin}}'s reign''' and entrusted to '''{{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}''' for enshrinement. At the same time, '''{{ill|Yamato Okunitama|en|Yamato Okunitama|WD=Q11387080|lt=Yamato Ōkunidama no Kami}}''', who was also removed from the imperial palace, was entrusted to '''{{ill|Nunakiirihime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入媛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunakiirihime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 渟名城入姫命|1=Princess Nunakiirihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561867}}''' and later enshrined at '''{{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|en|Ōyamato Shrine|WD=Q245731|lt=Ōyamato Shrine}}'''.
== Overview == There is a theory that '''Kasanui-mura is related to the origins of the {{ill|Daijosai|en|Daijosai|WD=Q11433653|lt=Daijosai}} and {{ill|Toyoakari no Sechie|ja|豊明節会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoakari no Sechie|1=Toyo no Akari no Sechie|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633732}}'''. According to the '''25th year of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Suinin}}'s reign''', '''Princess {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}''' enshrined '''Amaterasu''' at '''{{ill|Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto|ja|磯城の厳橿の本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''. The '''Ihokashi tree''' was considered a '''sacred tree for divine spirits''', and during Emperor Suijin's reign, the '''divine pillar of {{ill|Isokashiro Shrine|ja|磯堅城の神籬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isokashiro Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' was erected ('''Suijin Chronicles, 6th year'''){{who|date=June 2024}}. In '''{{ill|Kogo Shūi|en|Kogo Shūi|WD=Q531555|lt=Kogo Shūi}}''', it is referred to as '''"Ishiki no Himorogi"''', suggesting that '''"Ishiki no Ihokashi no Moto" and "Isokashiro no Himorogi"''' might be the same place{{who|date=June 2024}}.
=== Possible Locations=== There are various theories about the '''location of Kasanui-mura''', including: * '''{{ill|Hibara Shrine (Nara)|ja|檜原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibara Shrine (Nara)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大神神社|1=Ōmiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q705542}}''' ({{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|Sakurai, Nara|WD=Q127026|lt=Sakurai, Nara}} '''Miwa''') * '''{{ill|Tashinja|ja|多神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tashinja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 多坐弥志理都比古神社|1=Oonimasumishiritsuhiko Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430652}}''' ({{ill|Shiki District, Nara|en|Shiki District, Nara|WD=Q1155036|lt=Shiki District, Nara}} '''Tawaramoto Town, Ta''') * '''{{ill|Kasanui Shrine|ja|笠縫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasanui Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, '''Hazushō''', located in the '''southeastern corner of {{ill|Jinraku-ji|en|Jinraku-ji|WD=Q3179237|lt=Jinraku-ji}}''') * '''{{ill|Kasayama Kōjinja|ja|笠山荒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasayama Kōjinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Sakurai, '''Kasa''') * '''Hime-Ōji Shrine''' (a subsidiary shrine of Tashinja) * '''{{ill|Shiki Mikenuza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Mikenuza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志貴御縣坐神社|1=Shiki no Miagata Nimasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q63981812}}''' (Sakurai, Kanaya) * '''{{ill|Obu Tenjinsha|ja|小夫天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obu Tenjinsha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Sakurai, Obu) * '''{{ill|Anashizahyozu Shrine|ja|穴師坐兵主神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anashizahyozu Shrine|1=Anashi-Nimasu Hyōzu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597229}}''' (Sakurai, Anashi) * '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Takaichi District, Nara|en|Takaichi District, Nara|WD=Q1117901|lt=Takaichi District, Nara}}, '''Asuka Village, Asuka''') * '''Haseyamaguchi Shrine''' (Sakurai, Hase-Tejikarao)
Some shrines, such as '''Hibara Shrine, Kasayama Kōjinja, and Obu Tenjinsha''', are '''located deep in the mountains'''. Additionally, the name '''Jinraku (秦楽)''', associated with Kasanui Shrine, is also found '''in the plains below the front part of the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun|ja|築山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Yamatotakada, Nara|en|Yamatotakada, Nara|WD=Q838669|lt=Yamatotakada, Nara}}''', where it was historically written as '''"神楽" (Jinraku/Kagura)'''. This could suggest some connection.
From '''Tashinja''', looking in the direction of '''Yatsugikamino Mikoto Shrine (矢継ぎ神命神社)''' (dedicated to '''Tenmyō Toyotama no Mikoto'''), one faces the '''Jinraku and Tsukiyama Kofun direction'''. However, due to '''Yatsugi no Mori (矢継ぎの森)''', a sacred grove preserved by locals, '''the view is blocked'''.
It is also speculated that '''in ancient times, the view from Tashinja to Hime-Ōji Shrine may have included the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashihaka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 箸墓古墳|1=Hashihaka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603420}}'''. Originally, '''Hime-Ōji Shrine was located to the north (upper seat) of Tashinja''', but was possibly '''relocated southward (lower seat)'''.
If '''Tashinja is indeed the site of Kasanui-mura''', then these kofun formations might correspond to '''the origins of the {{ill|Daijosai|en|Daijosai|WD=Q11433653|lt=Daijosai}}'s {{ill|Yuki-den|ja|悠紀殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuki-den|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (which has '''cut-inward 千木 [Chigi] like {{ill|Kōtai Jingu|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''') and '''Shuki-den (主基殿)''' (which has '''cut-outward Chigi like {{ill|Toyoukedai Jingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoukedai Jingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}'''){{r|Heisei Daijōsai/p182}}.
== Kasanui Island in Osaka == At '''{{ill|Fukae Inari Shrine|ja|深江稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukae Inari Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Higashinari-ku, Osaka|ja|大阪市 東成区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinari-ku, Osaka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} '''Fukaeminami'''), there is a tradition that '''Fukae was once the residence of the {{ill|Kasanui clan|ja|笠縫氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasanui clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', who migrated from '''Kasanui-mura in Yamato'''.
The '''Man'yōshū poet, {{ill|Takechi no Kurohito|en|Takechi no Kurohito|WD=Q2389389|lt=Takechi no Kurohito}}''', wrote: > *"Shihatsu-yama uchi-koe mireba Kasanui no shima kogi-kakuru tana nashi kobune"* > *("Crossing over Mount Shihatsu, I see Kasanui Island, where small boats without roofs disappear.")*
In ancient times, '''Kasanui Island''' was believed to have been an island in an inlet '''stretching from present-day Fukae to {{ill|Higashiōsaka|en|Higashiōsaka|WD=Q243863|lt=Higashiōsaka, Osaka}} Ashiya'''. The '''Kasanui clan likely lived on the island''' to secure '''materials for making straw hats (笠, kasa)'''. This suggests that '''the original Kasanui-mura in Yamato might also have been a low-lying wetland or an island-like terrain'''{{who|date=June 2024}}.
Even today, '''the straw hats used in the Daijōsai are made in Fukae and presented to the Imperial Household'''. Additionally, '''Fukae has strong historical ties to the casting of the Imperial Divine Mirror (御神鏡)''', suggesting a connection with '''{{ill|Kagamitsukuri Shrine|ja|鏡作神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagamitsukuri Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鏡作坐天照御魂神社|1=Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q38278496}}''' in '''Shiki District, Tawaramoto Town, Yao'''.
== See Also == * {{ill|Motoise|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motoise|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} * {{ill|Kasanui Station|en|Kasanui Station|WD=Q6374013|lt=Kasanui Station}}
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="Heisei Daijōsai/p182">{{Citation|和書 | last = Kamata | first = Junichi | author-link = 鎌田純一 | title = Heisei Daijōsai Yōwa: Sokui-rei & Daijōsai | publisher = {{ill|Kinsei-sha|ja|錦正社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinsei-sha|1=Kinsei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650455}} | date = July 2003 | page = 182 | id = {{NDL|20452668}} | isbn = 4-7646-0262-8 }}</ref> }}
{{translated page|ja|笠縫邑|version=101402403|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:笠縫邑]]
